18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Defines consistent styling rules for JavaScript files to ensure readability, maintainability, and alignment across all OpenCode JavaScript code.
24: 
25: ### When to Use
26: 
27: - Writing new JavaScript files
28: - Reviewing JavaScript code for consistency
29: - Resolving style disagreements in code review
30: 
31: ---
32: 
33: ## 2. FILE HEADER FORMAT
34: 
35: All JavaScript files MUST begin with a boxed header identifying the module.
36: For `.mjs` files and OpenCode plugin loader paths, keep the same header style
37: but follow ESM module syntax instead of CommonJS.
38: 
39: ### Template
40: 
41: ```javascript
42: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
43: // ║ [Module Name]                                                             ║
44: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
45: ```
46: 
47: ### Requirements
48: 
49: - Box width: 78 characters total
50: - Module name: Centered or left-aligned within box
51: - Immediately followed by `'use strict';` directive for `.js/.cjs` files
52: - `.mjs` and plugin ESM files do not require `'use strict'`
53: 
54: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/utils/logger.ts:1-3`,
55: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/errors/core.ts:1-3`
56: 
57: ---
58: 
59: ## 3. USE STRICT DIRECTIVE
60: 
61: Every `.js` and `.cjs` JavaScript file MUST include the strict mode directive.
62: Do not add it to `.mjs` files; ES modules are strict by definition and the
63: alignment verifier intentionally skips `.mjs` strict-mode enforcement.
64: 
65: ```javascript
66: 'use strict';
67: ```
68: 
69: **Placement**: Immediately after file header, before any other code.
70: 
71: **Evidence**: `verify_alignment_drift.py` skips `.mjs`; CommonJS-style JS
72: helpers still use `'use strict'`.
73: 
74: ---
75: 
76: ## 4. SECTION ORGANIZATION
77: 
78: Large files are organized using numbered section dividers.
79: 
80: ### Section Divider Template
81: 
82: ```javascript
83: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
84: // 1. [SECTION NAME]
85: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
86: ```
87: 
88: ### Standard Section Order
89: 
90: | Order | Section Name     | Purpose                           |
91: |-------|------------------|-----------------------------------|
92: | 1     | IMPORTS/REQUIRES | Module dependencies               |
93: | 2     | CONSTANTS        | Configuration values, magic numbers |
94: | 3     | HELPERS          | Internal utility functions        |
95: | 4     | CORE LOGIC       | Main implementation               |
96: | 5     | EXPORTS          | Module public interface           |
97: 
98: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/scripts/core/config.ts`
99: 
100: ---
101: 
102: ## 5. NAMING CONVENTIONS
103: 
104: ### Function Names
105: 
106: **Style**: `camelCase`
107: 
108: ```javascript
109: // CORRECT
110: function loadConfig(path) { }
111: function memorySearch(query, options) { }
112: function validateInput(data) { }
113: 
114: // INCORRECT
115: function load_config(path) { }     // snake_case
116: function LoadConfig(path) { }      // PascalCase
117: function load-config(path) { }     // kebab-case (invalid syntax)
118: ```
119: 
120: **Evidence**: MDN Web Docs, Airbnb Style Guide, Node.js core APIs
121: 
122: ### Constant Names
123: 
124: **Style**: `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE`
125: 
126: ```javascript
127: // CORRECT
128: const MAX_RETRIES = 3;
129: const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 5000;
130: const SQLITE_BUSY_TIMEOUT = 30000;
131: 
132: // INCORRECT
133: const maxRetries = 3;       // camelCase
134: const max_retries = 3;      // snake_case (reserved for variables)
135: ```
136: 
137: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/core/config.ts`
138: 
139: ### Class Names
140: 
141: **Style**: `PascalCase`
142: 
143: ```javascript
144: // CORRECT
145: class MemoryError extends Error { }
146: class ConfigLoader { }
147: class VectorIndex { }
148: 
149: // INCORRECT
150: class memoryError { }       // camelCase
151: class memory_error { }      // snake_case
152: ```
153: 
154: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/errors/core.ts`
155: 
156: ### Variable Names
157: 
158: | Scope        | Style        | Example                          |
159: |--------------|--------------|----------------------------------|
160: | Local        | `camelCase`  | `const searchResults = []`       |
161: | Module-level | `camelCase`  | `const dbPath = '...'`           |
162: | Parameters   | `camelCase`  | `function search(query, maxResults)` |
163: 
164: ### Boolean Names
165: 
166: **Style**: `camelCase` with `is`/`has`/`can`/`should` prefix
167: 
168: ```javascript
169: // CORRECT
170: const isValid = true;
171: const hasResults = items.length > 0;
172: const canProceed = !isBlocked;
173: const shouldRetry = attempts < MAX_RETRIES;
174: 
175: // INCORRECT
176: const is_valid = true;       // snake_case
177: const valid = true;          // ambiguous
178: ```
179: 
180: ### Naming Summary Table
181: 
182: | Element          | Convention         | Example                |
183: |------------------|--------------------|------------------------|
184: | Functions        | `camelCase`        | `loadConfig`           |
185: | Constants        | `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` | `MAX_RETRIES`          |
186: | Classes          | `PascalCase`       | `MemoryError`          |
187: | Local variables  | `camelCase`        | `searchResults`        |
188: | Module variables | `camelCase`        | `dbConnection`         |
189: | Parameters       | `camelCase`        | `queryText`            |
190: | Booleans         | `is`/`has`/`can`   | `isValid`, `hasItems`  |
191: 
192: ---
193: 
194: ## 6. FORMATTING RULES
195: 
196: ### Indentation
197: 
198: - **Size**: 2 spaces
199: - **Tabs**: Never use tabs
200: 
201: ```javascript
202: // CORRECT (2 spaces)
203: function example() {
204:   if (condition) {
205:     return value;
206:   }
207: }
208: 
209: // INCORRECT (4 spaces or tabs)
210: function example() {
211:     if (condition) {
212:         return value;
213:     }
214: }
215: ```
216: 
217: ### Braces
218: 
219: **Style**: K&R (opening brace on same line)
220: 
221: ```javascript
222: // CORRECT (K&R style)
223: if (condition) {
224:   // code
225: } else {
226:   // code
227: }
228: 
229: function example() {
230:   // code
231: }
232: 
233: // INCORRECT (Allman style)
234: if (condition)
235: {
236:   // code
237: }
238: ```
239: 
240: ### Semicolons
241: 
242: **Rule**: Always use semicolons
243: 
244: ```javascript
245: // CORRECT
246: const value = 42;
247: return result;
248: 
249: // INCORRECT (ASI-dependent)
250: const value = 42
251: return result
252: ```
253: 
254: ### Quotes
255: 
256: **Rule**: Single quotes for strings
257: 
258: ```javascript
259: // CORRECT
260: const message = 'Hello world';
261: const template = `Value: ${value}`;  // Template literals OK
262: 
263: // INCORRECT
264: const message = "Hello world";  // Double quotes
265: ```
266: 
267: ### Line Length
268: 
269: - **Maximum**: 100 characters
270: - **Preferred**: 80 characters
271: - **Exception**: URLs and long strings
272: 
273: ---
274: 
275: ## 7. COMMENTING RULES
276: 
277: ### Principles
278: 
279: 1. **Quantity limit:** Maximum 3 comments per 10 lines of code
280: 2. **Focus on purposeful semantics:** Explain WHY something is done, not WHAT it does
281: 3. **Focus on WHY, not WHAT:** Explain intent, constraints, reasoning
282: 4. **No commented-out code:** Delete unused code (git preserves history)
283: 
284: ### Comment Examples
285: 
286: Use plain comments that explain reasoning:
287: 
288: - `// Reasoning for non-obvious choice`
289: - `// Precondition/invariant check`
290: - `// State that must always hold`
291: - `// SEC: security note tied to a stable standard (CWE-###)`
292: - `// Safety, performance, or reliability constraint`
293: 
294: ### Capitalization
295: 
296: All comment text MUST start with a capital letter:
297: 
298: ```javascript
299: // Reverse order preserves dependency chain     // correct
300: // reverse order preserves dependency chain     // wrong
301: ```
302: 
303: **Exceptions**: `eslint-disable` directives, `@ts-` annotations, and inline code references.
304: 
305: ### Reference Comments
306: 
307: Use a bracketed module prefix for inline comments:
308: 
309: ```javascript
310: // [module-name] Description of what this does
311: ```
312: 
313: Cite only durable references — never a spec-folder-internal id. Comments must not embed a spec folder/number, task/checklist/requirement id (`T###`, `CHK-###`, `REQ-###`), feature-catalog entry, ADR id, or ticket id; see [`../shared/universal_patterns.md`](../shared/universal_patterns.md) §4 and [`../../universal/code_style_guide.md`](../../universal/code_style_guide.md) §4 "No ephemeral-artifact pointers".
314: 
315: ```javascript
316: // GOOD — durable WHY, no ephemeral id
317: // Causal Memory Graph handlers: drift-why, causal-link, causal-stats, causal-unlink
318: // Recover pending writes on startup so a crash cannot lose data
319: // SEC: sanitize input to prevent stored XSS (CWE-79)
320: ```
321: 
322: ### Function Purpose Comments
323: 
324: Single line above function describing intent:
325: 
326: ```javascript
327: // Load configuration from YAML file, merging with defaults
328: // Returns validated config object or throws on invalid schema
329: function loadConfig(configPath) { }
330: 
331: // Calculate weighted memory score using decay function
332: // Higher scores indicate more recent and relevant memories
333: function calculateMemoryScore(memory, currentTime) { }
334: ```
335: 
336: ### Inline Comments (WHY, Not WHAT)
337: 
338: **Good examples (explain reasoning):**
339: 
340: ```javascript
341: // Skip if already initialized to prevent double-binding
342: if (window[INIT_FLAG]) return;
343: 
344: // Sort by recency so newest memories surface first
345: results.sort((a, b) => b.timestamp - a.timestamp);
346: 
347: // Add timeout to prevent hang on unresponsive database
348: const timeout = new Promise((_, reject) =>
349:   setTimeout(() => reject(new Error('Timeout')), 30000)
350: );
351: 
352: // Close DB connection on all control paths
353: try {
354:   await processBatch(items);
355: } finally {
356:   db.close();
357: }
358: ```
359: 
360: **Bad examples (narrate implementation):**
361: 
362: ```javascript
363: // Set the value to 42
364: const value = 42;
365: 
366: // Loop through items
367: for (const item of items) { }
368: 
369: // Check if result exists
370: if (result) { }
371: 
372: // Add item to array
373: items.push(item);
374: ```
375: 
376: ### Debug Logging Pattern
377: 
378: ```javascript
379: // Conditional logging for debug mode
380: const DEBUG = false;
381: const LOG_PREFIX = '[ModuleName]';
382: 
383: function log(...args) {
384:   if (DEBUG) {
385:     console.log(LOG_PREFIX, ...args);
386:   }
387: }
388: 
389: // Usage
390: log('Initialized with config:', config);
391: log('Processing batch:', batch.length, 'items');
392: ```
393: 
394: ### JSDoc Comments
395: 
396: Required for all exported functions. See `quality_standards.md` for full JSDoc format.
397: 
398: ```javascript
399: /**
400:  * Brief description of function purpose.
401:  *
402:  * @param {string} query - Search query text
403:  * @param {Object} options - Search options
404:  * @param {number} options.limit - Maximum results to return
405:  * @returns {Array} Array of search results
406:  */
407: function search(query, options = {}) {
408:   // implementation
409: }
410: ```
411: 
412: ### TODO Comments
413: 
414: Format for tracking incomplete work.
415: 
416: ```javascript
417: // TODO: [description of work needed]
418: // FIXME: [description of bug to fix]
419: ```
420: 
421: ---
422: 
423: ## 8. IMPORT ORDER
424: 
425: Organize imports in three groups with blank lines between.
426: 
427: ```javascript
428: 'use strict';
429: 
430: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
431: // 1. IMPORTS
432: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
433: 
434: // Node.js built-ins
435: const fs = require('fs');
436: const path = require('path');
437: 
438: // Third-party modules
439: const yaml = require('js-yaml');
440: const sqlite = require('better-sqlite3');
441: 
442: // Local modules
443: const { loadConfig } = require('./config');
444: const logger = require('../utils/logger');
445: ```
446: 
447: **Evidence**: `mcp_server/context-server.ts:5-68`
448: 
449: ---
450: 
451: ## 9. RELATED RESOURCES
452: 
453: - [quality_standards.md](./quality_standards.md) - Error handling, JSDoc, security patterns
454: - [quick_reference.md](./quick_reference.md) - Copy-paste templates and cheat sheets

(End of file - total 454 lines)
</content>

1: ---
2: title: JavaScript Quality Standards

4: trigger_phrases:

11: ---
12: 
13: # JavaScript Quality Standards
14: 
15: Module organization, error handling, documentation, and security patterns for JavaScript files.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Establishes module organization, error handling, documentation, and security patterns that all JavaScript code must follow.
24: 
25: ### When to Use
26: 
27: - Writing new JavaScript modules
28: - Reviewing code for quality compliance
29: - Setting up error handling and security patterns
30: 
31: ---
32: 
33: ## 2. MODULE ORGANIZATION
34: 
35: ### Module-System Boundary
36: 
37: Use the module format required by the file's package boundary and loader
38: contract. Do not force one JavaScript module style across all paths.
39: 
40: | Surface | Module Pattern | Notes |
41: |---------|----------------|-------|
42: | `.js/.cjs` Node utilities outside plugin loader paths | CommonJS with `'use strict'` | Default for legacy runtime helpers and standalone scripts |
43: | `.mjs` files | ESM | The verifier skips `'use strict'` enforcement for `.mjs` |
44: | `.opencode/plugins/*.{js,mjs,ts}` | ESM default export | Required by the OpenCode plugin loader |
45: | `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/*.{js,mjs,ts}` | ESM default export | Bridge helpers follow the plugin-loader contract |
46: 
47: ### CommonJS Pattern
48: 
49: Non-plugin `.js/.cjs` modules use CommonJS format with `'use strict'`
50: directive.
51: 
52: ```javascript
53: 'use strict';
54: 
55: // ... imports and implementation ...
56: 
57: module.exports = {
58:   // Exports here
59: };
60: ```
61: 
62: **Evidence**: Current TypeScript packages are ESM/NodeNext; this CommonJS
63: pattern applies to legacy `.js/.cjs` utility surfaces and test runners that use
64: `require`.
65: 
66: ### Export Pattern
67: 
68: Export functions using `camelCase` names. For MCP handlers, include
69: `snake_case` aliases for backward compatibility. Do not apply this export shape
70: to OpenCode plugin paths; those use `export default`.
71: 
72: ```javascript
73: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74: // 5. EXPORTS
75: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
76: 
77: module.exports = {
78:   // Primary exports (camelCase)
79:   loadConfig,
80:   memorySearch,
81:   validateInput,
82: 
83:   // Backward-compatible aliases (snake_case) — MCP handlers only
84:   load_config: loadConfig,
85:   memory_search: memorySearch,
86:   validate_input: validateInput
87: };
88: ```
89: 
90: **Evidence**: Legacy CommonJS export examples are historical; current
91: system-spec-kit TypeScript source uses ESM `export` statements and compiles per
92: workspace package settings.
93: 
94: ### Barrel Exports
95: 
96: Index files re-export from multiple modules.
97: 
98: ```javascript
99: // index.js
100: 'use strict';
101: 
102: module.exports = {
103:   ...require('./config'),
104:   ...require('./database'),
105:   ...require('./search')
106: };
107: ```
108: 
109: ---
110: 
111: ## 3. ERROR HANDLING
112: 
113: ### Guard Clause Pattern
114: 
115: Validate inputs at function start. Return early on failure.
116: 
117: ```javascript
118: function processData(input, options) {
119:   // Guard clauses first
120:   if (!input || typeof input !== 'string') {
121:     return { success: false, error: 'Invalid input: expected string' };
122:   }
123: 
124:   if (!options || typeof options !== 'object') {
125:     return { success: false, error: 'Invalid options: expected object' };
126:   }
127: 
128:   // Main logic after guards pass
129:   const result = transform(input, options);
130:   return { success: true, data: result };
131: }
132: ```
133: 
134: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`
135: 
136: ### Try-Catch Pattern
137: 
138: Wrap async operations with specific error handling.
139: 
140: ```javascript
141: async function fetchData(query) {
142:   try {
143:     const result = await database.query(query);
144:     return { success: true, data: result };
145:   } catch (error) {
146:     logger.error(`[fetchData] Query failed: ${error.message}`);
147:     return { success: false, error: error.message };
148:   }
149: }
150: ```
151: 
152: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`
153: 
154: ### Custom Error Classes
155: 
156: Extend Error for domain-specific errors.
157: 
158: ```javascript
159: /**
160:  * Custom error for memory operations.
161:  */
162: class MemoryError extends Error {
163:   /**
164:    * Create a MemoryError.
165:    * @param {string} message - Error description
166:    * @param {string} code - Error code (e.g., 'DB_CONNECTION_FAILED')
167:    * @param {Object} [context] - Additional context
168:    */
169:   constructor(message, code, context = {}) {
170:     super(message);
171:     this.name = 'MemoryError';
172:     this.code = code;
173:     this.context = context;
174:     Error.captureStackTrace(this, this.constructor);
175:   }
176: }
177: 
178: module.exports = { MemoryError };
179: ```
180: 
181: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/errors/core.ts`
182: 
183: ### Error Response Pattern
184: 
185: Consistent error response structure.
186: 
187: ```javascript
188: // Success response
189: { success: true, data: result }
190: 
191: // Error response
192: { success: false, error: 'Error message', code: 'ERROR_CODE' }
193: ```
194: 
195: ---
196: 
197: ## 4. CONSOLE LOGGING
198: 
199: ### Bracketed Module Prefix
200: 
201: All console output uses bracketed module identifier.
202: 
203: ```javascript
204: // Format: [module-name] Message
205: console.log(`[context-server] Server started on port ${port}`);
206: console.error(`[memory-search] Search failed: ${error.message}`);
207: console.warn(`[config] Using default value for missing key: ${key}`);
208: ```
209: 
210: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/context-server.ts`
211: 
212: ### Log Levels
213: 
214: | Level   | Usage                            | Example                                    |
215: |---------|----------------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
216: | `log`   | General information              | `[server] Request received`                |
217: | `error` | Errors requiring attention       | `[db] Connection failed: timeout`          |
218: | `warn`  | Potential issues, fallbacks      | `[config] Missing value, using default`    |
219: | `debug` | Development-only verbose output  | `[search] Query plan: ${JSON.stringify()}` |
220: 
221: ---
222: 
223: ## 5. JSDOC DOCUMENTATION
224: 
225: ### Function Documentation
226: 
227: Required for all exported functions.
228: 
229: ```javascript
230: /**
231:  * Search memory database for matching entries.
232:  *
233:  * @param {string} query - Search query text
234:  * @param {Object} options - Search configuration
235:  * @param {number} [options.limit=10] - Maximum results to return
236:  * @param {string} [options.specFolder] - Filter by spec folder path
237:  * @param {string[]} [options.anchors] - Filter by anchor types
238:  * @returns {Promise<Array<Object>>} Array of matching memory entries
239:  * @throws {MemoryError} If database connection fails
240:  *
241:  * @example
242:  * const results = await memorySearch('authentication', {
243:  *   limit: 5,
244:  *   specFolder: 'specs/007-auth'
245:  * });
246:  */
247: async function memorySearch(query, options = {}) {
248:   // implementation
249: }
250: ```
251: 
252: **Evidence**: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/handlers/memory-search.ts`
253: 
254: ### Type Annotations
255: 
256: Common JSDoc type patterns.
257: 
258: ```javascript
259: /**
260:  * @param {string} name - Required string parameter
261:  * @param {number} [count=10] - Optional with default
262:  * @param {Object} config - Object parameter
263:  * @param {string} config.path - Required property
264:  * @param {boolean} [config.verbose] - Optional property
265:  * @param {string|null} value - Union type
266:  * @param {Array<string>} items - Typed array
267:  * @param {Promise<Object>} result - Promise type
268:  * @param {Function} callback - Function type
269:  */
270: ```
271: 
272: ### Class Documentation
273: 
274: ```javascript
275: /**
276:  * Manages vector-based semantic search index.
277:  *
278:  * @class
279:  * @example
280:  * const index = new VectorIndex(dbPath);
281:  * await index.initialize();
282:  */
283: class VectorIndex {
284:   /**
285:    * Create a VectorIndex instance.
286:    * @param {string} dbPath - Path to SQLite database
287:    */
288:   constructor(dbPath) {
289:     this.dbPath = dbPath;
290:   }
291: }
292: ```
293: 
294: ---
295: 
296: ## 6. SECURITY PATTERNS
297: 
298: ### CWE-22: Path Traversal Prevention
299: 
300: Validate and sanitize file paths.
301: 
302: ```javascript
303: const path = require('path');
304: 
305: /**
306:  * Safely resolve path within allowed directory.
307:  * @param {string} basePath - Allowed base directory
308:  * @param {string} userPath - User-provided path
309:  * @returns {string|null} Resolved path or null if invalid
310:  */
311: function safeResolvePath(basePath, userPath) {
312:   // Normalize and resolve
313:   const resolvedBase = path.resolve(basePath);
314:   const resolvedPath = path.resolve(basePath, userPath);
315: 
316:   // Verify path stays within base
317:   if (!resolvedPath.startsWith(resolvedBase + path.sep)) {
318:     console.error(`[security] Path traversal attempt: ${userPath}`);
319:     return null;
320:   }
321: 
322:   return resolvedPath;
323: }
324: ```
325: 
326: ### CWE-400: Input Limits
327: 
328: Prevent resource exhaustion with input validation.
329: 
330: ```javascript
331: const MAX_QUERY_LENGTH = 10000;
332: const MAX_RESULTS = 100;
333: 
334: function validateSearchInput(query, options) {
335:   // Limit query length
336:   if (query && query.length > MAX_QUERY_LENGTH) {
337:     return { valid: false, error: `Query exceeds ${MAX_QUERY_LENGTH} characters` };
338:   }
339: 
340:   // Limit result count
341:   if (options.limit && options.limit > MAX_RESULTS) {
342:     options.limit = MAX_RESULTS;
343:     console.warn(`[search] Limit capped to ${MAX_RESULTS}`);
344:   }
345: 
346:   return { valid: true };
347: }
348: ```
349: 
350: ### Input Sanitization
351: 
352: ```javascript
353: /**
354:  * Sanitize string input for safe use.
355:  * @param {string} input - Raw user input
356:  * @returns {string} Sanitized string
357:  */
358: function sanitizeString(input) {
359:   if (typeof input !== 'string') {
360:     return '';
361:   }
362: 
363:   // Remove null bytes
364:   return input.replace(/\0/g, '');
365: }
366: ```
367: 
368: ---
369: 
370: ## 7. TESTING PATTERNS
371: 
372: ### Using Node.js Assert
373: 
374: ```javascript
375: const assert = require('assert');
376: 
377: // Test function
378: function testLoadConfig() {
379:   const config = loadConfig('./test-config.yaml');
380: 
381:   assert.ok(config, 'Config should be loaded');
382:   assert.strictEqual(config.version, '1.0.0', 'Version should match');
383:   assert.deepStrictEqual(config.features, ['a', 'b'], 'Features should match');
384: }
385: 
386: // Run tests
387: if (require.main === module) {
388:   testLoadConfig();
389:   console.log('[test] All tests passed');
390: }
391: ```
392: 
393: ### Test File Organization
394: 
395: ```javascript
396: // tests/test_config.js
397: 'use strict';
398: 
399: const assert = require('assert');
400: const { loadConfig, validateConfig } = require('../scripts/core/config');
401: 
402: describe('Config Module', () => {
403:   describe('loadConfig', () => {
404:     it('should load valid YAML config', () => {
405:       const config = loadConfig('./fixtures/valid.yaml');
406:       assert.ok(config);
407:     });
408: 
409:     it('should return null for missing file', () => {
410:       const config = loadConfig('./nonexistent.yaml');
411:       assert.strictEqual(config, null);
412:     });
413:   });
414: });
415: ```
416: 
417: ---
418: 
419: ## 8. ASYNC PATTERNS
420: 
421: ### Async/Await Style
422: 
423: Prefer async/await over callbacks and raw promises.
424: 
425: ```javascript
426: // CORRECT: async/await
427: async function fetchAndProcess() {
428:   try {
429:     const data = await fetchData();
430:     const result = await processData(data);
431:     return { success: true, data: result };
432:   } catch (error) {
433:     return { success: false, error: error.message };
434:   }
435: }
436: 
437: // AVOID: Promise chains
438: function fetchAndProcess() {
439:   return fetchData()
440:     .then(data => processData(data))
441:     .then(result => ({ success: true, data: result }))
442:     .catch(error => ({ success: false, error: error.message }));
443: }
444: ```
445: 
446: ### Parallel Operations
447: 
448: ```javascript
449: // Parallel execution
450: const [users, posts] = await Promise.all([
451:   fetchUsers(),
452:   fetchPosts()
453: ]);
454: 
455: // Sequential execution (when order matters)
456: const user = await fetchUser(id);
457: const posts = await fetchUserPosts(user.id);
458: ```
459: 
460: ---
461: 
462: ## 9. TEST FILE EXEMPTION TIER
463: 
464: CLI-only test runners, setup scripts, and similar utilities are exempt from certain quality standards because they are executed directly (not imported as modules) and serve a different purpose than library code.
465: 
466: ### Scope
467: 
468: This exemption applies to:
469: - Files in `scripts/tests/`
470: - Files in `scripts/setup/`
471: - Any file with a test runner shebang (e.g., `#!/usr/bin/env node`)
472: 
473: ### Exempted Standards
474: 
475: | Standard                           | Reason for Exemption                                           |
476: |------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------|
477: | `module.exports` requirement       | Test runners are executed directly, not imported by other code |
478: | Guard clause requirement           | Scripts run once at CLI level; input validation is minimal     |
479: | `[module-name]` error prefix       | Test output uses its own format (e.g., pass/fail assertions)   |
480: 
481: ### What Still Applies
482: 
483: All other quality standards remain in effect for test files, including:
484: - `'use strict'` directive
485: - JSDoc documentation for non-trivial functions
486: - Error handling with try-catch for async operations
487: - Security patterns (path traversal, input limits) if handling external input
488: 
489: ### Example
490: 
491: ```javascript
492: #!/usr/bin/env node
493: 'use strict';
494: 
495: const assert = require('assert');
496: const { loadConfig } = require('../core/config');
497: 
498: // No module.exports needed — this file is executed directly
499: // No guard clauses needed — CLI-level script
500: // No [module-name] prefix needed — test output format
501: 
502: const config = loadConfig('./fixtures/valid.yaml');
503: assert.ok(config, 'Config should load successfully');
504: assert.strictEqual(config.version, '1.0.0');
505: 
506: console.log('PASS: All config tests passed');
507: ```
508: 
509: ---
510: 
511: ## 10. OPENCODE PLUGIN EXEMPTION TIER
512: 
513: OpenCode plugin entrypoints and helper modules are exempt from the CommonJS export standard because the OpenCode plugin loader requires ESM default exports. This exemption is narrow and does not relax other JavaScript quality standards.
514: 
515: ### Scope
516: 
517: This exemption applies to:
518: - `.opencode/plugins/*.{js,mjs,ts}`
519: - `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/*.{js,mjs,ts}`
520: 
521: ### Exempted Standards
522: 
523: | Standard                           | Reason for Exemption                                                               |
524: |------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
525: | `module.exports` requirement       | OpenCode plugin loader requires ESM default export per `@opencode-ai/plugin/dist/index.d.ts` |
526: 
527: ### What Still Applies
528: 
529: All other quality standards remain in effect for OpenCode plugin files, including:
530: - JSDoc on exports
531: - Guard clauses
532: - Security patterns
533: - Error handling
534: - Bracketed logging if logging exists
535: - Numbered ALL-CAPS section dividers
536: - `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` constants
537: - `camelCase` functions
538: 
539: ### Brief Example
540: 
541: ```javascript
542: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
543: // ║ COMPONENT: Example OpenCode Plugin                                      ║
544: // ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
545: // ║ PURPOSE: Show the allowed ESM default export with required structure.    ║
546: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
547: 
548: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
549: // 1. IMPORTS
550: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
551: 
552: import { tool } from '@opencode-ai/plugin';
553: 
554: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
555: // 2. CONSTANTS
556: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
557: 
558: const PLUGIN_ID = 'example-plugin';
559: 
560: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
561: // 3. PLUGIN FACTORY
562: // ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
563: 
564: /**
565:  * Create the example OpenCode plugin hooks.
566:  *
567:  * @param {Object} ctx - OpenCode plugin context
568:  * @param {Object} [options] - Plugin options
569:  * @returns {Promise<Object>} Hooks object for the OpenCode plugin loader
570:  */
571: export default async function ExamplePlugin(ctx, options = {}) {
572:   return {
573:     tool: {
574:       example_status: tool({
575:         description: 'Show example plugin status',
576:         args: {},
577:         async execute() {
578:           return `plugin_id=${PLUGIN_ID}`;
579:         },
580:       }),
581:     },
582:   };
583: }
584: ```
585: 
586: ---
587: 
588: ## 11. RELATED RESOURCES
589: 
590: - [style_guide.md](./style_guide.md) - Formatting and naming conventions
591: - [quick_reference.md](./quick_reference.md) - Copy-paste templates and cheat sheets

(End of file - total 591 lines)
</content>

1: ---
2: title: JavaScript Code Quality Checklist
3: description: Quality validation checklist for JavaScript code in the OpenCode development environment.
4: trigger_phrases:

11: ---
12: 
13: # JavaScript Code Quality Checklist
14: 
15: Quality validation checklist for JavaScript code in the OpenCode development environment.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Specific quality checks for JavaScript files. Use alongside the [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) for complete validation.
24: 
25: ### Priority Levels
26: 
27: | Level | Meaning | Enforcement |
28: |-------|---------|-------------|
29: | P0 | HARD BLOCKER | Must fix before commit |
30: | P1 | Required | Must fix or get explicit approval |
31: | P2 | Recommended | Can defer with justification |
32: 
33: ---
34: 
35: ## 2. P0 - HARD BLOCKERS
36: 
37: These items MUST be fixed before any commit.
38: 
39: ### Box Header
40: 
41: ```markdown
42: [ ] File has box header with component identification
43: ```
44: 
45: **Required format**:
46: ```javascript
47: // ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
48: // ║ COMPONENT: [Component Name]                                               ║
49: // ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
50: // ║ PURPOSE: [Brief description of what this file does]                       ║
51: // ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
52: ```
53: 
54: ### 'use strict'
55: 
56: ```markdown
57: [ ] 'use strict' directive present at top of file
58: ```
59: 
60: **Required** for `.js/.cjs` files (immediately after header):
61: ```javascript
62: 'use strict';
63: ```
64: 
65: **Exception**: `.mjs` files and OpenCode plugin ESM paths are strict by module
66: semantics; the verifier intentionally skips `.mjs` strict-mode enforcement.
67: 
68: ### Function Naming
69: 
70: ```markdown
71: [ ] All functions use camelCase naming
72: ```
73: 
74: **Correct**:
75: ```javascript
76: function validateInput(data) { }
77: function processFile(path) { }
78: const handleError = (err) => { };
79: ```
80: 
81: **Wrong**:
82: ```javascript
83: function validate_input(data) { }    // snake_case
84: function ProcessFile(path) { }       // PascalCase
85: ```
86: 
87: ### No Commented-Out Code
88: 
89: ```markdown
90: [ ] No commented-out code blocks
91: ```
92: 
93: **Remove**:
94: ```javascript
95: // function old_implementation() {
96: //     return "deprecated";
97: // }
98: ```
99: 
100: ### WHY Comments
101: 
102: ```markdown
103: [ ] Purposeful WHY comments only (max 3 comments per 10 LOC)
104:     - Comments explain WHY, not WHAT
105:     - No narrative comments describing obvious mechanics
106: ```
107: 
108: **Required for**:
109: - Algorithm choices
110: - Performance optimizations
111: - Business rule implementations
112: - Workarounds for known issues
113: 
114: ### Numbered ALL-CAPS Sections
115: 
116: ```markdown
117: [ ] Numbered ALL-CAPS section headers are preserved (`## N. SECTION NAME`)
118: ```
119: 
120: ---
121: 
122: ## 3. P1 - REQUIRED
123: 
124: These must be addressed or receive approval to defer.
125: 
126: ### Module Exports by Path
127: 
128: ```markdown
129: [ ] Module export style matches the file path and package boundary
130: ```
131: 
132: **Correct for non-plugin `.js/.cjs` utilities**:
133: ```javascript
134: module.exports = { functionName, CONSTANT };
135: module.exports = functionName;
136: ```
137: 
138: **Correct for OpenCode plugin loader paths**:
139: ```javascript
140: export default async function ExamplePlugin(ctx, options = {}) {
141:   return {};
142: }
143: ```
144: 
145: **Plugin paths**: Files in `.opencode/plugins/` and
146: `.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/` MUST use ESM
147: (`export default`) — see `quality_standards.md` §10 OpenCode Plugin Exemption
148: Tier.
149: 
150: **Wrong for non-plugin CommonJS utilities**:
151: ```javascript
152: export { function_name };
153: export default function_name;
154: ```
155: 
156: ### Guard Clauses
157: 
158: ```markdown
159: [ ] Functions use early returns for invalid states
160: ```
161: 
162: **Correct**:
163: ```javascript
164: function processData(data) {
165:     if (!data) return null;
166:     if (!data.requiredField) return null;
167: 
168:     // Main logic here
169:     return result;
170: }
171: ```
172: 
173: **Wrong**:
174: ```javascript
175: function processData(data) {
176:     if (data) {
177:         if (data.requiredField) {
178:             // Deeply nested logic
179:         }
180:     }
181: }
182: ```
183: 
184: ### Bracketed Logging
185: 
186: ```markdown
187: [ ] Console output uses bracketed format
188: ```
189: 
190: **Correct**:
191: ```javascript
192: console.log('[COMPONENT] Processing file:', filename);
193: console.error('[COMPONENT] Error:', error.message);
194: ```
195: 
196: **Wrong**:
197: ```javascript
198: console.log('Processing file: ' + filename);
199: console.log(filename);  // No context
200: ```
201: 
202: ### Constants
203: 
204: ```markdown
205: [ ] Module-level constants use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
206: ```
207: 
208: **Correct**:
209: ```javascript
210: const MAX_RETRIES = 3;
211: const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 5000;
212: const VALID_TYPES = ['a', 'b', 'c'];
213: ```
214: 
215: ### Error Handling
216: 
217: ```markdown
218: [ ] Try/catch blocks handle errors appropriately
219: [ ] Error messages include context
220: ```
221: 
222: **Correct**:
223: ```javascript
224: try {
225:     const data = JSON.parse(content);
226: } catch (error) {
227:     console.error('[PARSER] Failed to parse JSON:', error.message);
228:     throw new Error(`Invalid JSON in ${filename}: ${error.message}`);
229: }
230: ```
231: 
232: ### KISS / DRY / SOLID Checks
233: 
234: ```markdown
235: [ ] KISS: avoid unnecessary abstraction or layering
236: [ ] DRY: repeated rules/constants extracted to shared source
237: [ ] SOLID: SRP/OCP/LSP/ISP/DIP violations checked and documented
238: ```
239: 
240: ---
241: 
242: ## 4. P2 - RECOMMENDED
243: 
244: These improve quality but can be deferred.
245: 
246: ### JSDoc Comments
247: 
248: ```markdown
249: [ ] Public functions have JSDoc documentation
250: ```
251: 
252: **Example**:
253: ```javascript
254: /**
255:  * Validates input data against schema.
256:  * @param {Object} data - The data to validate
257:  * @param {Object} schema - Validation schema
258:  * @returns {Object} Validation result with isValid and errors
259:  */
260: function validateInput(data, schema) { }
261: ```
262: 
263: ### Consistent String Quotes
264: 
265: ```markdown
266: [ ] Strings use single quotes consistently
267: ```
268: 
269: **Preferred**:
270: ```javascript
271: const message = 'Hello world';
272: const config = { key: 'value' };
273: ```
274: 
275: ### Async/Await Pattern
276: 
277: ```markdown
278: [ ] Async operations use async/await (not callbacks where possible)
279: ```
280: 
281: **Preferred**:
282: ```javascript
283: async function fetchData() {
284:     const response = await fetch(url);
285:     const data = await response.json();
286:     return data;
287: }
288: ```
289: 
290: ### Destructuring
291: 
292: ```markdown
293: [ ] Object destructuring used where it improves clarity
294: ```
295: 
296: **Good**:
297: ```javascript
298: const { name, value } = config;
299: function process({ data, options = {} }) { }
300: ```
301: 
302: ---
303: 
304: ## 5. CHECKLIST TEMPLATE
305: 
306: For formal findings-first review output, use `sk-code-review` as the baseline and treat this file as JavaScript surface evidence.
307: 
308: ```markdown
309: ## JavaScript Standards Surface Evidence
310: 
311: - [ ] JavaScript standards validated in `javascript_checklist.md`
312: - [ ] Universal standards validated in `universal_checklist.md`
313: - [ ] Findings severity/order produced with `sk-code-review/references/quick_reference.md`
314: - [ ] Baseline security, quality, and test checks sourced from `sk-code-review`
315: - [ ] Surface-specific deviations documented with file:line evidence
316: ```
317: 
318: ---
319: 
320: ## 6. VALIDATION COMMANDS
321: 
322: ```bash
323: # Syntax check
324: node --check file.js
325: 
326: # ESLint (if configured)
327: npx eslint file.js
328: 
329: # Check for common issues in non-plugin CommonJS utilities
330: grep -n "function [a-z]*_[a-z]" file.js  # Find snake_case functions
331: grep -n "^export " file.js              # Review: allowed only for ESM/plugin paths
332: ```
333: 
334: ---
335: 
336: ## 7. RELATED RESOURCES
337: 
338: ### Checklists
339: - [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) - Language-agnostic checks
340: 
341: ### Style Guide
342: - [JavaScript Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/javascript/style_guide.md)
343: - [JavaScript Quality Standards](../../../references/opencode/javascript/quality_standards.md)

(End of file - total 343 lines)
</content>

---
title: JavaScript Code Quality Checklist
description: Quality validation checklist for JavaScript code in the OpenCode development environment.
trigger_phrases:
  - "opencode javascript checklist"
  - "javascript quality validation checklist"
  - "js box header check"
  - "use strict checklist"
importance_tier: normal
contextType: implementation
---

# JavaScript Code Quality Checklist

Quality validation checklist for JavaScript code in the OpenCode development environment.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Specific quality checks for JavaScript files. Use alongside the [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) for complete validation.

### Priority Levels

| Level | Meaning | Enforcement |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| P0 | HARD BLOCKER | Must fix before commit |
| P1 | Required | Must fix or get explicit approval |
| P2 | Recommended | Can defer with justification |

---

## 2. P0 - HARD BLOCKERS

These items MUST be fixed before any commit.

### Box Header

```markdown
[ ] File has box header with component identification
```

**Required format**:
```javascript
// ╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
// ║ COMPONENT: [Component Name]                                               ║
// ╠══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
// ║ PURPOSE: [Brief description of what this file does]                       ║
// ╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

### 'use strict'

```markdown
[ ] 'use strict' directive present at top of file
```

**Required** for `.js/.cjs` files (immediately after header):
```javascript
'use strict';
```

**Exception**: `.mjs` files and OpenCode plugin ESM paths are strict by module
semantics; the verifier intentionally skips `.mjs` strict-mode enforcement.

### Function Naming

```markdown
[ ] All functions use camelCase naming
```

**Correct**:
```javascript
function validateInput(data) { }
function processFile(path) { }
const handleError = (err) => { };
```

**Wrong**:
```javascript
function validate_input(data) { }    // snake_case
function ProcessFile(path) { }       // PascalCase
```

### No Commented-Out Code

```markdown
[ ] No commented-out code blocks
```

**Remove**:
```javascript
// function old_implementation() {
//     return "deprecated";
// }
```

### WHY Comments

```markdown
[ ] Purposeful WHY comments only (max 3 comments per 10 LOC)
    - Comments explain WHY, not WHAT
    - No narrative comments describing obvious mechanics
```

**Required for**:
- Algorithm choices
- Performance optimizations
- Business rule implementations
- Workarounds for known issues

### Numbered ALL-CAPS Sections

```markdown
[ ] Numbered ALL-CAPS section headers are preserved (`## N. SECTION NAME`)
```

---

## 3. P1 - REQUIRED

These must be addressed or receive approval to defer.

### Module Exports by Path

```markdown
[ ] Module export style matches the file path and package boundary
```

**Correct for non-plugin `.js/.cjs` utilities**:
```javascript
module.exports = { functionName, CONSTANT };
module.exports = functionName;
```

**Correct for OpenCode plugin loader paths**:
```javascript
export default async function ExamplePlugin(ctx, options = {}) {
  return {};
}
```

**Plugin paths**: Files in `.opencode/plugins/` and
`.opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/plugin_bridges/` MUST use ESM
(`export default`) — see `quality_standards.md` §10 OpenCode Plugin Exemption
Tier.

**Wrong for non-plugin CommonJS utilities**:
```javascript
export { function_name };
export default function_name;
```

### Guard Clauses

```markdown
[ ] Functions use early returns for invalid states
```

**Correct**:
```javascript
function processData(data) {
    if (!data) return null;
    if (!data.requiredField) return null;

    // Main logic here
    return result;
}
```

**Wrong**:
```javascript
function processData(data) {
    if (data) {
        if (data.requiredField) {
            // Deeply nested logic
        }
    }
}
```

### Bracketed Logging

```markdown
[ ] Console output uses bracketed format
```

**Correct**:
```javascript
console.log('[COMPONENT] Processing file:', filename);
console.error('[COMPONENT] Error:', error.message);
```

**Wrong**:
```javascript
console.log('Processing file: ' + filename);
console.log(filename);  // No context
```

### Constants

```markdown
[ ] Module-level constants use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE
```

**Correct**:
```javascript
const MAX_RETRIES = 3;
const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT = 5000;
const VALID_TYPES = ['a', 'b', 'c'];
```

### Error Handling

```markdown
[ ] Try/catch blocks handle errors appropriately
[ ] Error messages include context
```

**Correct**:
```javascript
try {
    const data = JSON.parse(content);
} catch (error) {
    console.error('[PARSER] Failed to parse JSON:', error.message);
    throw new Error(`Invalid JSON in ${filename}: ${error.message}`);
}
```

### KISS / DRY / SOLID Checks

```markdown
[ ] KISS: avoid unnecessary abstraction or layering
[ ] DRY: repeated rules/constants extracted to shared source
[ ] SOLID: SRP/OCP/LSP/ISP/DIP violations checked and documented
```

---

## 4. P2 - RECOMMENDED

These improve quality but can be deferred.

### JSDoc Comments

```markdown
[ ] Public functions have JSDoc documentation
```

**Example**:
```javascript
/**
 * Validates input data against schema.
 * @param {Object} data - The data to validate
 * @param {Object} schema - Validation schema
 * @returns {Object} Validation result with isValid and errors
 */
function validateInput(data, schema) { }
```

### Consistent String Quotes

```markdown
[ ] Strings use single quotes consistently
```

**Preferred**:
```javascript
const message = 'Hello world';
const config = { key: 'value' };
```

### Async/Await Pattern

```markdown
[ ] Async operations use async/await (not callbacks where possible)
```

**Preferred**:
```javascript
async function fetchData() {
    const response = await fetch(url);
    const data = await response.json();
    return data;
}
```

### Destructuring

```markdown
[ ] Object destructuring used where it improves clarity
```

**Good**:
```javascript
const { name, value } = config;
function process({ data, options = {} }) { }
```

---

## 5. CHECKLIST TEMPLATE

For formal findings-first review output, use `sk-code-review` as the baseline and treat this file as JavaScript surface evidence.

```markdown
## JavaScript Standards Surface Evidence

- [ ] JavaScript standards validated in `javascript_checklist.md`
- [ ] Universal standards validated in `universal_checklist.md`
- [ ] Findings severity/order produced with `sk-code-review/references/quick_reference.md`
- [ ] Baseline security, quality, and test checks sourced from `sk-code-review`
- [ ] Surface-specific deviations documented with file:line evidence
```

---

## 6. VALIDATION COMMANDS

```bash
# Syntax check
node --check file.js

# ESLint (if configured)
npx eslint file.js

# Check for common issues in non-plugin CommonJS utilities
grep -n "function [a-z]*_[a-z]" file.js  # Find snake_case functions
grep -n "^export " file.js              # Review: allowed only for ESM/plugin paths
```

---

<content>
1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
3: // MODULE: mk-code-index MCP Server Entrypoint
4: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
5: /**
6:  * mk-code-index MCP server entry point.
7:  *
8:  * ## Environment Variables
9:  *
10:  * - `MK_CODE_INDEX_ROOT_DIR` — Workspace root directory used for readiness
11:  *   snapshot evaluation and the marker JSON body. The marker file path is
12:  *   resolved from the configured code-graph database directory.
13:  */
14: import { Server } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/index.js';
15: import { StdioServerTransport } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/stdio.js';
16: import { CallToolRequestSchema, ListToolsRequestSchema } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/types.js';
17: import * as codeGraphTools from './tools/index.js';
18: import { CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS } from './tool-schemas.js';
19: import { writeCodeGraphReadinessMarker } from './lib/readiness-marker.js';
20: import { closeDbWithAssertion, refreshOwnerLease } from './lib/index.js';
21: import { DATABASE_DIR } from './core/config.js';
22: import {
23:   getIpcBridgeStats,
24:   resolveIpcSocketPath,
25:   startIpcSocketServer,
26:   type IpcSocketServerHandle,
27: } from './lib/ipc/socket-server.js';
28: import {
29:   createLauncherIdleMonitor,
30:   type LauncherIdleMonitor,
31: } from './lib/ipc/launcher-idle-timeout.js';
32: 
33: const DEFAULT_OWNER_LEASE_TTL_MS = 60_000;
34: const OWNER_LEASE_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS = DEFAULT_OWNER_LEASE_TTL_MS / 3;
35: let ownerLeaseRefreshTimer: ReturnType<typeof setInterval> | null = null;
36: let ownerLeaseMismatchShutdownStarted = false;
37: 
38: function clearOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer(): void {
39:   if (!ownerLeaseRefreshTimer) return;
40:   clearInterval(ownerLeaseRefreshTimer);
41:   ownerLeaseRefreshTimer = null;
42: }
43: 
44: function startOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer(): void {
45:   if (ownerLeaseRefreshTimer) return;
46:   ownerLeaseRefreshTimer = setInterval(() => {
47:     try {
48:       const refreshed = refreshOwnerLease(DATABASE_DIR, process.pid);
49:       if (!refreshed && !ownerLeaseMismatchShutdownStarted) {
50:         ownerLeaseMismatchShutdownStarted = true;
51:         void shutdownCodeIndex('owner lease moved to another process').finally(() => process.exit(0));
52:       }
53:     } catch (error: unknown) {
54:       const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
55:       console.error(`[owner-lease] heartbeat refresh failed: ${message}`);
56:     }
57:   }, OWNER_LEASE_REFRESH_INTERVAL_MS);
58:   ownerLeaseRefreshTimer.unref();
59: }
60: 
61: process.on('uncaughtException', (err) => {
62:   console.error('[mk-code-index] uncaughtException:', err);
63:   clearOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer();
64:   closeDbWithAssertion();
65:   process.exit(1);
66: });
67: process.on('unhandledRejection', (reason) => {
68:   console.error('[mk-code-index] unhandledRejection:', reason);
69:   clearOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer();
70:   closeDbWithAssertion();
71:   process.exit(1);
72: });
73: 
74: function createCodeIndexMcpServer(): Server {
75:   const codeIndexServer = new Server(
76:     { name: 'mk-code-index', version: '1.0.0' },
77:     { capabilities: { tools: {} } }
78:   );
79: 
80:   codeIndexServer.setRequestHandler(ListToolsRequestSchema, async () => ({
81:     tools: CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS,
82:   }));
83: 
84:   codeIndexServer.setRequestHandler(CallToolRequestSchema, async (request): Promise<any> => {
85:     const args = (request.params.arguments ?? {}) as Record<string, unknown>;
86:     return await codeGraphTools.dispatch(request.params.name, args);
87:   });
88: 
89:   return codeIndexServer;
90: }
91: 
92: let ipcBridge: IpcSocketServerHandle | null = null;
93: let launcherIdleMonitor: LauncherIdleMonitor | null = null;
94: let shutdownPromise: Promise<void> | null = null;
95: 
96: async function shutdownCodeIndex(reason: string): Promise<void> {
97:   if (shutdownPromise) return shutdownPromise;
98: 
99:   shutdownPromise = (async () => {
100:     console.error(`[mk-code-index] ${reason}`);
101:     clearOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer();
102:     if (launcherIdleMonitor) {
103:       launcherIdleMonitor.stop();
104:       launcherIdleMonitor = null;
105:     }
106:     if (ipcBridge) {
107:       const bridge = ipcBridge;
108:       ipcBridge = null;
109:       await bridge.close().catch((error: unknown) => {
110:         const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
111:         console.error(`[mk-code-index] ipc-bridge close error: ${message}`);
112:       });
113:     }
114:     closeDbWithAssertion();
115:   })();
116: 
117:   return shutdownPromise;
118: }
119: 
120: process.once('SIGINT', () => {
121:   void shutdownCodeIndex('SIGINT').finally(() => process.exit(0));
122: });
123: process.once('SIGTERM', () => {
124:   void shutdownCodeIndex('SIGTERM').finally(() => process.exit(0));
125: });
126: 
127: const server = createCodeIndexMcpServer();
128: 
129: try {
130:   writeCodeGraphReadinessMarker(process.env.MK_CODE_INDEX_ROOT_DIR || process.cwd());
131: } catch (error: unknown) {
132:   const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
133:   console.error(`[mk-code-index] readiness marker write failed: ${message}`);
134: }
135: 
136: const transport = new StdioServerTransport();
137: try {
138:   await server.connect(transport);
139:   startOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer();
140:   launcherIdleMonitor = createLauncherIdleMonitor({
141:     serviceName: 'mk-code-index',
142:     getActiveClientCount: () => getIpcBridgeStats().secondary_clients_count,
143:     onIdle: async () => {
144:       await shutdownCodeIndex('launcher idle timeout');
145:       process.exit(0);
146:     },
147:     log: (message: string) => console.error(message),
148:   });
149:   ipcBridge = await startIpcSocketServer({
150:     socketPath: resolveIpcSocketPath(DATABASE_DIR),
151:     createServer: () => createCodeIndexMcpServer(),
152:     log: (message: string) => console.error(message),
153:     onActivity: () => launcherIdleMonitor?.markActivity(),
154:   });
155: } catch (error: unknown) {
156:   clearOwnerLeaseRefreshTimer();
157:   const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
158:   const stack = error instanceof Error ? error.stack : undefined;
159:   console.error('[mk-code-index] connect failed:', message);
160:   if (stack) console.error(stack);
161:   process.exit(1);
162: }

(End of file - total 162 lines)
</content>

205:  * advertised `additionalProperties:false`, `enum`, and `minLength` constraints
206:  * were never enforced and malformed calls reached handlers. We now enforce the
207:  * unambiguous constraints: unknown-key rejection, enum membership, and string
208:  * minLength. Required-field presence is enforced at the dispatcher
209:  * (`getMissingRequiredStringArgs`); numeric range is intentionally left to the
210:  * handler clamps (e.g. `limit`, `maxDepth`) to avoid rejecting values the
211:  * handler safely coerces and bounds. Throws a field-specific Error on the first
212:  * violation so the dispatcher can surface a structured validation error.
213:  */
214: export function validateToolArgs(toolName: string, rawInput: Record<string, unknown>): Record<string, unknown> {
215:   const tool = CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS.find((definition) => definition.name === toolName);
216:   if (!tool) {
217:     throw new Error(`Unknown tool: ${toolName}`);
218:   }
219:   if (!rawInput || typeof rawInput !== 'object' || Array.isArray(rawInput)) {
220:     throw new Error(`Invalid arguments for ${toolName}: expected object`);
221:   }
222: 
223:   const schema = tool.inputSchema as {
224:     properties?: Record<string, Record<string, unknown>>;
225:     additionalProperties?: boolean;
226:   };
227:   const properties = schema.properties ?? {};
228: 
229:   // additionalProperties:false -> reject keys not in the published schema.
230:   if (schema.additionalProperties === false) {
231:     const allowed = new Set(Object.keys(properties));
232:     const unexpected = Object.keys(rawInput).filter((key) => !allowed.has(key));
233:     if (unexpected.length > 0) {
234:       throw new Error(
235:         `Invalid arguments for ${toolName}: unexpected propert${unexpected.length === 1 ? 'y' : 'ies'}: ${unexpected.join(', ')}`,
236:       );
237:     }
238:   }
239: 
240:   // Validate each PRESENT value: enum membership and string minLength only.
241:   for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(rawInput)) {
242:     const prop = properties[key];
243:     if (!prop || value === undefined || value === null) {
244:       continue;
245:     }
246:     const enumValues = prop.enum as unknown[] | undefined;
247:     if (Array.isArray(enumValues) && !enumValues.includes(value)) {
248:       throw new Error(
249:         `Invalid arguments for ${toolName}: field '${key}' must be one of: ${enumValues.map(String).join(', ')}`,
250:       );
251:     }
252:     const minLength = prop.minLength as number | undefined;
253:     if (typeof minLength === 'number' && typeof value === 'string' && value.length < minLength) {
254:       throw new Error(
255:         `Invalid arguments for ${toolName}: field '${key}' must be at least ${minLength} character(s)`,
256:       );
257:     }
258:   }
259: 
260:   return rawInput;
261: }

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Code Graph Tools
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Dispatch for code graph MCP tools: scan, query, status, context, verify, apply, detect_changes.
5: import {
6:   handleCodeGraphScan,
7:   handleCodeGraphQuery,
8:   handleCodeGraphStatus,
9:   handleCodeGraphContext,
10:   handleClassifyQueryIntent,
11:   handleCodeGraphVerify,
12:   handleCodeGraphApply,
13:   handleDetectChanges,
14:   // Reserved import slots for future hld/lld, trace, and impact-analysis handlers.
15: } from '../handlers/index.js';
16: 
17: import type { MCPResponse } from '../lib/shared/mcp-types.js';
18: import { parseArgs } from '../lib/shared/mcp-types.js';
19: import { CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS, validateToolArgs } from '../tool-schemas.js';
20: 
21: /**
22:  * Tool names handled by this module, derived from the schema registry so the
23:  * membership gate cannot drift from the advertised tool surface. tool-schemas
24:  * is the single source of truth; the dispatch switch below adds the one thing
25:  * a schema can't carry — which handler each name maps to.
26:  */
27: export const TOOL_NAMES = new Set(CODE_GRAPH_TOOL_SCHEMAS.map((schema) => schema.name));
28: 
29: /** Coerce handler response to MCPResponse (fix type literal narrowing) */
30: function toMCP(result: { content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> }): MCPResponse {
31:   return {
32:     content: result.content.map(c => ({ type: 'text' as const, text: c.text })),
33:   };
34: }
35: 
36: function getMissingRequiredStringArgs(args: Record<string, unknown>, requiredKeys: string[]): string[] {
37:   return requiredKeys.filter((key) => {
38:     const value = args[key];
39:     return typeof value !== 'string' || value.trim().length === 0;
40:   });
41: }
42: 
43: function validationError(tool: string, missingKeys: string[]): MCPResponse {
44:   return {
45:     content: [{
46:       type: 'text',
47:       text: JSON.stringify({
48:         status: 'error',
49:         error: `Missing required field${missingKeys.length === 1 ? '' : 's'}: ${missingKeys.join(', ')}`,
50:         tool,
51:       }),
52:     }],
53:   };
54: }
55: 
56: /** Dispatch a tool call. Returns null if tool name not handled. */
57: export async function handleTool(name: string, args: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<MCPResponse | null> {
58:   // Enforce the published inputSchema (enum / additionalProperties /
59:   // minLength) before dispatch for known tools, so malformed calls are rejected
60:   // with a field-specific error rather than reaching handlers. Required-field
61:   // presence is still checked per-case below; numeric range stays handler-clamped.
62:   if (TOOL_NAMES.has(name)) {
63:     try {
64:       validateToolArgs(name, args);
65:     } catch (err) {
66:       return {
67:         content: [{
68:           type: 'text',
69:           text: JSON.stringify({
70:             status: 'error',
71:             error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err),
72:             tool: name,
73:           }),
74:         }],
75:       };
76:     }
77:   }
78: 
79:   switch (name) {
80:     case 'code_graph_scan':
81:       return toMCP(await handleCodeGraphScan(parseArgs<Parameters<typeof handleCodeGraphScan>[0]>(args)));
82:     case 'code_graph_query': {
83:       const missingKeys = getMissingRequiredStringArgs(args, ['operation', 'subject']);
84:       if (missingKeys.length > 0) {
85:         return validationError(name, missingKeys);
86:       }
87:       return toMCP(await handleCodeGraphQuery(parseArgs<Parameters<typeof handleCodeGraphQuery>[0]>(args)));
88:     }
89:     case 'code_graph_status':
90:       parseArgs<Record<string, never>>(args);
91:       return toMCP(await handleCodeGraphStatus());
92:     case 'code_graph_context':
93:       return toMCP(await handleCodeGraphContext(parseArgs<Parameters<typeof handleCodeGraphContext>[0]>(args)));
94:     case 'code_graph_classify_query_intent': {
95:       const missingKeys = getMissingRequiredStringArgs(args, ['query']);
96:       if (missingKeys.length > 0) {
97:         return validationError(name, missingKeys);
98:       }
99:       return toMCP(await handleClassifyQueryIntent(parseArgs<Parameters<typeof handleClassifyQueryIntent>[0]>(args)));
100:     }
101:     case 'code_graph_verify':
102:       return toMCP(await handleCodeGraphVerify(parseArgs<Parameters<typeof handleCodeGraphVerify>[0]>(args)));
103:     case 'code_graph_apply':
104:       return toMCP(await handleCodeGraphApply(parseArgs<Parameters<typeof handleCodeGraphApply>[0]>(args)));
105:     case 'detect_changes': {
106:       const missingKeys = getMissingRequiredStringArgs(args, ['diff']);
107:       if (missingKeys.length > 0) {
108:         return validationError(name, missingKeys);
109:       }
110:       return toMCP(await handleDetectChanges(parseArgs<Parameters<typeof handleDetectChanges>[0]>(args)));
111:     }
112:     // Reserved dispatch slots for future hld/lld, trace, and impact-analysis cases.
113:     default:
114:       return null;
115:   }
116: }

1: #!/usr/bin/env node
2: // -------------------------------------------------------------------
3: // MODULE: Code Index CLI
4: // -------------------------------------------------------------------
5: // Daemon-backed command line client for the mk-code-index tool surface.
6: // The CLI is IPC-only: graph storage and mutation remain daemon-owned.
7: 
8: import { spawn } from 'node:child_process';
9: import { existsSync, lstatSync, mkdirSync, readFileSync, statSync } from 'node:fs';
10: import net from 'node:net';
11: import { createRequire } from 'node:module';
12: import path from 'node:path';
13: import { StringDecoder } from 'node:string_decoder';
14: import { fileURLToPath } from 'node:url';
15: 
16: import {
17:   assertCodeIndexCliManifest,
18:   CODE_INDEX_CLI_TOOL_DEFINITIONS,

20: import {
21:   type ToolDefinition,
22:   validateToolArgs,

24: 
25: const JSON_RPC_PROTOCOL_VERSION = '2025-06-18';
26: const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS = 30_000;
27: const DEFAULT_SOCKET_DIR = '/tmp/mk-code-index';
28: const SOCKET_FILE_NAME = 'daemon-ipc.sock';
29: const RESERVED_COMMANDS = new Set(['list-tools', 'completion']);
30: const BLOCKABLE_READ_TOOLS = new Set(['code_graph_query', 'code_graph_context', 'detect_changes']);
31: 
32: const EXIT_SUCCESS = 0;
33: const EXIT_RUNTIME = 1;
34: const EXIT_USAGE = 64;
35: const EXIT_PROTOCOL = 69;
36: const EXIT_RETRYABLE = 75;
37: 
38: type OutputFormat = 'json' | 'text' | 'jsonl';

40: type CompletionShell = 'bash' | 'zsh';
41: 
42: interface ParsedCommand {
43:   readonly command: string;

45:   readonly timeoutMs: number;
46:   readonly warmOnly: boolean;
47:   readonly toolListMode: ToolListMode;
48:   readonly completionShell?: CompletionShell;
49:   readonly args: Record<string, unknown>;
50:   readonly help: boolean;
51:   readonly version: boolean;
52: }
53: 
54: interface CliIo {

56:   readonly stderr: Pick<NodeJS.WriteStream, 'write'>;
57: }
58: 
59: interface RepoPaths {

76:   readonly maybeBridgeLeaseHolder: (options: unknown) => Promise<unknown>;
77:   readonly probeDaemon: (socketPath: string, options?: { timeoutMs?: number; deepProbe?: boolean }) => Promise<BridgeProbeResult>;
78:   readonly toConnectionOptions: (socketPath: string) => string | net.NetConnectOpts;
79: }

86:     readonly code?: number;

94: }

96: interface ToolResult {

98:   readonly isError?: boolean;
99: }
100: 
101: class CliUsageError extends Error {
102:   constructor(message: string) {
103:     super(message);
104:     this.name = 'CliUsageError';
105:   }
106: }
107: 
108: class CliRetryableError extends Error {
109:   constructor(message: string) {
110:     super(message);
111:     this.name = 'CliRetryableError';
112:   }
113: }
114: 
115: class CliProtocolError extends Error {
116:   constructor(message: string) {
117:     super(message);
118:     this.name = 'CliProtocolError';
119:   }
120: }
121: 
122: class JsonRpcError extends Error {
123:   public readonly code?: number;
124:   public readonly data?: unknown;
125: 
126:   constructor(error: NonNullable<JsonRpcResponse['error']>) {
127:     super(error.message ?? 'JSON-RPC request failed');
128:     this.name = 'JsonRpcError';
129:     this.code = error.code;
130:     this.data = error.data;
131:   }
