133: 
134: // Awaits drain when the kernel pipe buffer is full: a fire-and-forget
135: // write followed by process.exit() truncates large payloads at the pipe
136: // buffer boundary (observed as exactly 64KB on darwin). Streams without
137: // emitter methods (test fakes) resolve immediately.
138: async function writeLine(stream: Pick<NodeJS.WriteStream, 'write'>, value: string): Promise<void> {
139:   const flushed = stream.write(`${value}\n`);
140:   if (flushed) return;
141:   const drainable = stream as Pick<NodeJS.WriteStream, 'write'> & Partial<Pick<NodeJS.WriteStream, 'once' | 'off'>>;
142:   if (typeof drainable.once !== 'function') return;
143:   await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
144:     const settle = (): void => {
145:       drainable.off?.('drain', settle);
146:       drainable.off?.('error', settle);
147:       drainable.off?.('close', settle);
148:       resolve();
149:     };
150:     drainable.once?.('drain', settle);
151:     drainable.once?.('error', settle);
152:     drainable.once?.('close', settle);
153:   });
154: }
155: 
156: function currentModulePath(): string {
157:   return fileURLToPath(import.meta.url);
158: }
159: 
160: function findRepoPaths(startFile = currentModulePath()): RepoPaths {
161:   let current = path.dirname(startFile);
162: 
163:   while (true) {
164:     const directOpencodeDir = path.basename(current) === '.opencode' ? current : path.join(current, '.opencode');
165:     const launcherPath = path.join(directOpencodeDir, 'bin', 'mk-code-index-launcher.cjs');
166:     const bridgePath = path.join(directOpencodeDir, 'bin', 'lib', 'launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs');
167:     if (existsSync(launcherPath) && existsSync(bridgePath)) {
168:       const repoRoot = path.dirname(directOpencodeDir);
169:       return {
170:         opencodeDir: directOpencodeDir,
171:         repoRoot,
172:         launcherPath,
173:         bridgePath,
174:         dbDir: path.join(directOpencodeDir, 'skills', 'system-code-graph', 'mcp_server', 'database'),
175:         packageJsonPath: path.join(directOpencodeDir, 'skills', 'system-code-graph', 'package.json'),
176:         packageLockPath: path.join(directOpencodeDir, 'skills', 'system-code-graph', 'package-lock.json'),
177:       };
178:     }
179: 
180:     const parent = path.dirname(current);
181:     if (parent === current) {
182:       throw new CliProtocolError('Unable to locate .opencode/bin launcher assets from CLI path');
183:     }
184:     current = parent;
185:   }
186: }
187: 
188: function loadBridge(paths: RepoPaths): BridgeModule {
189:   const require = createRequire(import.meta.url);
190:   return require(paths.bridgePath) as BridgeModule;
191: }
192: 
193: function readCliVersion(paths: RepoPaths): string {
194:   try {
195:     const parsed = JSON.parse(readFileSync(paths.packageJsonPath, 'utf8')) as { readonly version?: unknown };
196:     if (typeof parsed.version === 'string') return parsed.version;
197:   } catch {
198:     // Fall back to package-lock for older local checkouts with no package.json.
199:   }
200:   try {
201:     const parsed = JSON.parse(readFileSync(paths.packageLockPath, 'utf8')) as {
202:       readonly version?: unknown;
203:       readonly packages?: { readonly ['']?: { readonly version?: unknown } };
204:     };
205:     const version = parsed.version ?? parsed.packages?.['']?.version;
206:     return typeof version === 'string' ? version : '0.0.0';
207:   } catch {
208:     return '0.0.0';
209:   }
210: }
211: 
212: function toKebabCommand(name: string): string {
213:   return name.replace(/_/g, '-');
214: }
215: 
216: function toCamelCommand(name: string): string {
217:   return name.replace(/_([a-z0-9])/g, (_match, char: string) => char.toUpperCase());
218: }
219: 
220: function commandAliases(name: string): string[] {
221:   return Array.from(new Set([name, toKebabCommand(name), toCamelCommand(name)]));
222: }
223: 
224: function usageText(command?: string): string {
225:   const tool = command ? getToolDefinition(command) : null;
226:   if (tool) {
227:     return `code-index ${tool.name}
228: 
229: Description:
230:   ${tool.description}
231: 
232: Aliases:
233:   ${commandAliases(tool.name).join(', ')}
234: 
235: Input schema:
236: ${JSON.stringify(tool.inputSchema, null, 2)}`;
237:   }
238: 
239:   return `code-index - daemon-backed CLI for mk-code-index
240: 
241: Usage:
242:   code-index list-tools [--format json|text|jsonl] [--compact|--names-only]
243:   code-index completion bash|zsh
244:   code-index <tool_name> [--json '{...}'] [--format json|text|jsonl] [--timeout-ms N] [--warm-only]
245:   code-index <tool_name> --param value [--another-param value]
246: 
247: Examples:
248:   code-index list-tools --format text
249:   code-index list-tools --compact
250:   code-index completion zsh
251:   code-index code_graph_status --format json
252:   code-index code_graph_query --operation outline --subject .opencode/skills/system-code-graph/mcp_server/index.ts
253:   code-index detect_changes --json '{"diff":"diff --git a/a.ts b/a.ts\n"}' --warm-only --timeout-ms 2000
254: 
255: Exit codes:
256:   0 success, 1 runtime error, 64 usage/schema error, 69 protocol/dist mismatch, 75 retryable daemon error
257: 
258: Exit code notes:
259:   detect_changes status:"parse_error" (malformed diff input) exits 64.
260:   status:"blocked" readiness refusals exit 0 deliberately: blocked is an
261:   actionable answer (run the surfaced requiredAction), not a CLI failure.`;
262: }
263: 
264: function parseCompletionShell(raw: string): CompletionShell {
265:   if (raw === 'bash' || raw === 'zsh') return raw;
266:   throw new CliUsageError('completion shell must be one of: bash, zsh');
267: }
268: 
269: function normalizeName(value: string): string {
270:   return value.replace(/[-_]/g, '').toLowerCase();
271: }
272: 
273: function toCamelCaseFlag(value: string): string {
274:   return value.replace(/-([a-z0-9])/g, (_match, char: string) => char.toUpperCase());
275: }
276: 
277: function toolDefinitions(): readonly ToolDefinition[] {
278:   try {
279:     assertCodeIndexCliManifest();
280:   } catch (error: unknown) {
281:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
282:     throw new CliProtocolError(message);
283:   }
284:   return CODE_INDEX_CLI_TOOL_DEFINITIONS;
285: }
286: 
287: function commandMap(): Map<string, ToolDefinition> {
288:   const map = new Map<string, ToolDefinition>();
289:   for (const tool of toolDefinitions()) {
290:     for (const alias of commandAliases(tool.name)) {
291:       const existing = map.get(alias);
292:       if (existing && existing.name !== tool.name) {
293:         throw new CliProtocolError(`Command alias collision: ${alias} maps to ${existing.name} and ${tool.name}`);
294:       }
295:       map.set(alias, tool);
296:     }
297:   }
298:   return map;
299: }
300: 
301: function getToolDefinition(command: string): ToolDefinition | null {
302:   return commandMap().get(command) ?? null;
303: }
304: 
305: function inputPropertyNames(tool: ToolDefinition): string[] {
306:   const schema = tool.inputSchema;
307:   const properties = typeof schema === 'object' && schema !== null && !Array.isArray(schema)
308:     ? (schema as { readonly properties?: unknown }).properties
309:     : null;
310:   if (!properties || typeof properties !== 'object' || Array.isArray(properties)) return [];
311:   return Object.keys(properties);
312: }
313: 
314: function requiredPropertyNames(tool: ToolDefinition): string[] {
315:   const schema = tool.inputSchema;
316:   const required = typeof schema === 'object' && schema !== null && !Array.isArray(schema)
317:     ? (schema as { readonly required?: unknown }).required
318:     : null;
319:   if (!Array.isArray(required)) return [];
320:   return required.filter((value): value is string => typeof value === 'string');
321: }
322: 
323: function resolvePropertyName(rawFlag: string, tool: ToolDefinition): string {
324:   const exact = inputPropertyNames(tool).find((name) => name === rawFlag);
325:   if (exact) return exact;
326: 
327:   const normalized = normalizeName(rawFlag);
328:   const matched = inputPropertyNames(tool).find((name) => normalizeName(name) === normalized);
329:   if (matched) return matched;
330: 
331:   return rawFlag.includes('-') ? toCamelCaseFlag(rawFlag) : rawFlag;
332: }
333: 
334: function parseValue(raw: string): unknown {
335:   const trimmed = raw.trim();
336:   if (trimmed === 'true') return true;
337:   if (trimmed === 'false') return false;
338:   if (trimmed === 'null') return null;
339:   if ((trimmed.startsWith('{') && trimmed.endsWith('}')) || (trimmed.startsWith('[') && trimmed.endsWith(']'))) {
340:     try {
341:       return JSON.parse(trimmed) as unknown;
342:     } catch (error: unknown) {
343:       const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
344:       throw new CliUsageError(`Invalid JSON value: ${message}`);
345:     }
346:   }
347:   return raw;
348: }
349: 
350: function parsePositiveInteger(raw: string, flagName: string): number {
351:   if (!/^\d+$/.test(raw)) {
352:     throw new CliUsageError(`${flagName} must be a positive integer`);
353:   }
354:   const value = Number.parseInt(raw, 10);
355:   if (!Number.isFinite(value) || value <= 0) {
356:     throw new CliUsageError(`${flagName} must be a positive integer`);
357:   }
358:   return value;
359: }
360: 
361: function parseFormat(raw: string): OutputFormat {
362:   if (raw === 'json' || raw === 'text' || raw === 'jsonl') return raw;
363:   throw new CliUsageError('--format must be one of: json, text, jsonl');
364: }
365: 
366: function parseInlineBoolean(raw: string, flagName: string): boolean {
367:   if (raw === 'true' || raw === '1') return true;
368:   if (raw === 'false' || raw === '0') return false;
369:   throw new CliUsageError(`${flagName} must be true or false`);
370: }
371: 
372: function readOptionValue(tokens: string[], index: number, flagName: string): { readonly value: string; readonly nextIndex: number } {
373:   const inline = tokens[index].match(/^--[^=]+=(.*)$/);
374:   if (inline) return { value: inline[1], nextIndex: index + 1 };
375:   const value = tokens[index + 1];
376:   if (value === undefined) {
377:     throw new CliUsageError(`${flagName} requires a value`);
378:   }
379:   return { value, nextIndex: index + 2 };
380: }
381: 
382: function parseJsonObject(raw: string): Record<string, unknown> {
383:   let parsed: unknown;
384:   try {
385:     parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as unknown;
386:   } catch (error: unknown) {
387:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
388:     throw new CliUsageError(`--json must be a valid JSON object: ${message}`);
389:   }
390:   if (!parsed || typeof parsed !== 'object' || Array.isArray(parsed)) {
391:     throw new CliUsageError('--json must be a JSON object');
392:   }
393:   return parsed as Record<string, unknown>;
394: }
395: 
396: function envFlagEnabled(name: string): boolean {
397:   const raw = process.env[name];
398:   if (raw === undefined || raw === null) return false;
399:   return ['1', 'true', 'yes', 'on'].includes(raw.trim().toLowerCase());
400: }
401: 
402: function defaultWarmOnly(): boolean {
403:   return envFlagEnabled('SPECKIT_CODE_INDEX_CLI_WARM_ONLY')
404:     || envFlagEnabled('SPECKIT_CODE_INDEX_CLI_PROMPT_TIME')
405:     || envFlagEnabled('SPECKIT_CLI_PROMPT_TIME')
406:     || envFlagEnabled('OPENCODE_PROMPT_TIME')
407:     || envFlagEnabled('CODEX_PROMPT_TIME')
408:     || envFlagEnabled('CLAUDE_CODE_PROMPT_TIME');
409: }
410: 
411: export function parseCliArgs(argv: string[]): ParsedCommand {
412:   const command = argv[0];
413:   const warmOnlyDefault = defaultWarmOnly();
414:   if (!command || command === '--help' || command === '-h') {
415:     return { command: '', format: 'text', timeoutMs: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS, warmOnly: warmOnlyDefault, toolListMode: 'full', args: {}, help: true, version: false };
416:   }
417:   if (command === '--version' || command === '-v') {
418:     return { command: '', format: 'text', timeoutMs: DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS, warmOnly: warmOnlyDefault, toolListMode: 'full', args: {}, help: false, version: true };
419:   }
420: 
421:   let format: OutputFormat = 'json';
422:   let timeoutMs = DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS;
423:   let warmOnly = warmOnlyDefault;
424:   let toolListMode: ToolListMode = 'full';
425:   let completionShell: CompletionShell | undefined;
426:   let jsonPayload: Record<string, unknown> | null = null;
427:   const tool = getToolDefinition(command);
428:   const parsedArgs: Record<string, unknown> = {};
429:   const tokens = argv.slice(1);
430:   let index = 0;
431: 
432:   while (index < tokens.length) {
433:     const token = tokens[index];
434:     if (command === 'completion' && completionShell === undefined && !token.startsWith('--')) {
435:       completionShell = parseCompletionShell(token);
436:       index += 1;
437:       continue;
438:     }
439:     if (!token.startsWith('--')) {
440:       throw new CliUsageError(`Unexpected positional argument: ${token}`);
441:     }
442:     const rawFlag = token.slice(2).split('=', 1)[0];
443:     const option = `--${rawFlag}`;
444: 
445:     if (rawFlag === 'help' || rawFlag === 'h') {
446:       return { command, format: 'text', timeoutMs, warmOnly, toolListMode, completionShell, args: {}, help: true, version: false };
447:     }
448:     if (rawFlag === 'format') {
449:       const read = readOptionValue(tokens, index, option);
450:       format = parseFormat(read.value);
451:       index = read.nextIndex;
452:       continue;
453:     }
454:     if (rawFlag === 'timeout-ms') {
455:       const read = readOptionValue(tokens, index, option);
456:       timeoutMs = parsePositiveInteger(read.value, option);
457:       index = read.nextIndex;
458:       continue;
459:     }
460:     if (rawFlag === 'warm-only') {
461:       const inline = token.match(/^--[^=]+=(.*)$/);
462:       warmOnly = inline ? parseInlineBoolean(inline[1], option) : true;
463:       index += 1;
464:       continue;
465:     }
466:     if (rawFlag === 'no-warm-only') {
467:       warmOnly = false;
468:       index += 1;
469:       continue;
470:     }
471:     if (rawFlag === 'compact') {
472:       toolListMode = 'compact';
473:       index += 1;
474:       continue;
475:     }
476:     if (rawFlag === 'names-only') {
477:       toolListMode = 'names-only';
478:       index += 1;
479:       continue;
480:     }
481:     if (rawFlag === 'json') {
482:       const read = readOptionValue(tokens, index, option);
483:       jsonPayload = parseJsonObject(read.value);
484:       index = read.nextIndex;
485:       continue;
486:     }
487: 
488:     if (!tool) {
489:       throw new CliUsageError(`Unknown command: ${command}`);
490:     }
491: 
492:     const propertyName = resolvePropertyName(rawFlag, tool);
493:     const inline = token.match(/^--[^=]+=(.*)$/);
494:     if (inline) {
495:       parsedArgs[propertyName] = parseValue(inline[1]);
496:       index += 1;
497:       continue;
498:     }
499: 
500:     const next = tokens[index + 1];
501:     if (next === undefined || next.startsWith('--')) {
502:       parsedArgs[propertyName] = true;
503:       index += 1;
504:       continue;
505:     }
506:     parsedArgs[propertyName] = parseValue(next);
507:     index += 2;
508:   }
509: 
510:   if (jsonPayload && Object.keys(parsedArgs).length > 0) {
511:     throw new CliUsageError('--json cannot be combined with per-parameter flags');
512:   }
513: 
514:   return {
515:     command,
516:     format,
517:     timeoutMs,
518:     warmOnly,
519:     toolListMode,
520:     completionShell,
521:     args: jsonPayload ?? parsedArgs,
522:     help: false,
523:     version: false,
524:   };
525: }
526: 
527: function declaredSchemaTypes(propertySchema: unknown): string[] {
528:   if (!isRecord(propertySchema)) return [];
529:   const type = propertySchema.type;
530:   if (typeof type === 'string') return [type];
531:   if (Array.isArray(type)) return type.filter((entry): entry is string => typeof entry === 'string');
532:   return [];
533: }
534: 
535: // Flag values arrive as strings; without schema-driven coercion a numeric
536: // filter like `--min-confidence 0.8` reaches the daemon as "0.8", which
537: // numeric clamps silently treat as 0 — the user's filter is dropped under
538: // a status:ok response. Coerce strings to the schema-declared primitive
539: // and reject unparseable values as usage errors instead.
540: function coerceArgsToSchema(tool: ToolDefinition, args: Record<string, unknown>): Record<string, unknown> {
541:   const schema = tool.inputSchema;
542:   const properties = isRecord(schema) && isRecord(schema.properties) ? schema.properties : null;
543:   if (!properties) return args;
544: 
545:   const coerced: Record<string, unknown> = { ...args };
546:   for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(args)) {
547:     if (typeof value !== 'string') continue;
548:     const types = declaredSchemaTypes(properties[key]);
549:     if (types.includes('number') || types.includes('integer')) {
550:       const trimmed = value.trim();
551:       const parsed = trimmed.length > 0 ? Number(trimmed) : Number.NaN;
552:       if (!Number.isFinite(parsed)) {
553:         throw new CliUsageError(`Invalid value for ${key}: expected ${types.includes('integer') ? 'an integer' : 'a number'}, received "${value}"`);
554:       }
555:       if (types.includes('integer') && !Number.isInteger(parsed)) {
556:         throw new CliUsageError(`Invalid value for ${key}: expected an integer, received "${value}"`);
557:       }
558:       coerced[key] = parsed;
559:       continue;
560:     }
561:     if (types.includes('boolean')) {
562:       if (value === 'true' || value === '1') {
563:         coerced[key] = true;
564:         continue;
565:       }
566:       if (value === 'false' || value === '0') {
567:         coerced[key] = false;
568:         continue;
569:       }
570:       throw new CliUsageError(`Invalid value for ${key}: expected true or false, received "${value}"`);
571:     }
572:   }
573:   return coerced;
574: }
575: 
576: function missingRequiredArgs(tool: ToolDefinition, args: Record<string, unknown>): string[] {
577:   return requiredPropertyNames(tool).filter((key) => {
578:     const value = args[key];
579:     return value === undefined || value === null || (typeof value === 'string' && value.trim().length === 0);
580:   });
581: }
582: 
583: function validateCommand(parsed: ParsedCommand): { readonly tool: ToolDefinition; readonly args: Record<string, unknown> } | null {
584:   if (RESERVED_COMMANDS.has(parsed.command)) return null;
585:   const tool = getToolDefinition(parsed.command);
586:   if (!tool) {
587:     throw new CliUsageError(`Unknown command: ${parsed.command}`);
588:   }
589: 
590:   const args = coerceArgsToSchema(tool, { ...parsed.args });
591:   const missing = missingRequiredArgs(tool, args);
592:   if (missing.length > 0) {
593:     throw new CliUsageError(`Missing required field${missing.length === 1 ? '' : 's'}: ${missing.join(', ')}`);
594:   }
595: 
596:   // A bare apply defaults to rescan routing, which on a soft-stale graph is
597:   // an incremental index mutation nobody explicitly asked for. From this CLI
598:   // mutation intent must be spelled out: an operation, a dryRun preview, or
599:   // the explicit default-routing opt-in used by doctor-style flows.
600:   if (tool.name === 'code_graph_apply'
601:     && args.operation === undefined
602:     && args.dryRun !== true
603:     && process.env.MK_CODE_INDEX_CLI_ALLOW_DEFAULT_APPLY !== '1') {
604:     throw new CliUsageError(
605:       'code_graph_apply without an operation mutates via default rescan routing; '
606:       + 'pass --operation=<op>, preview with --dry-run=true, or set '
607:       + 'MK_CODE_INDEX_CLI_ALLOW_DEFAULT_APPLY=1 to opt in to default routing.',
608:     );
609:   }
610: 
611:   try {
612:     return {
613:       tool,
614:       args: validateToolArgs(tool.name, args) as Record<string, unknown>,
615:     };
616:   } catch (error: unknown) {
617:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
618:     throw new CliUsageError(message);
619:   }
620: }

622: function renderJson(value: unknown, format: OutputFormat): string {
623:   if (format === 'jsonl') return JSON.stringify(value);
624:   return JSON.stringify(value, null, 2);
625: }

627: function compactTool(tool: ToolDefinition): Record<string, unknown> {
628:   return {
629:     name: tool.name,
630:     command: tool.name,
631:     kebabCommand: toKebabCommand(tool.name),
632:     camelCommand: toCamelCommand(tool.name),
633:     aliases: commandAliases(tool.name),
634:     description: tool.description,
635:   };

638: function renderToolList(format: OutputFormat, mode: ToolListMode = 'full'): string {
639:   const tools = toolDefinitions();
640:   const payload = {
641:     status: 'ok',
642:     data: {
643:       count: tools.length,
644:       mode,
645:       tools: tools.map((tool) => ({
646:         name: tool.name,
647:         command: tool.name,
648:         kebabCommand: toKebabCommand(tool.name),
649:         camelCommand: toCamelCommand(tool.name),
650:         aliases: commandAliases(tool.name),
651:         description: tool.description,
652:         inputSchema: tool.inputSchema,
653:       })),
654:     },
655:   };
656:   const compactPayload = {
657:     status: 'ok',
658:     data: {
659:       count: tools.length,
660:       mode,
661:       tools: tools.map(compactTool),
662:     },
663:   };
664:   const namesOnlyPayload = {
665:     status: 'ok',
666:     data: {
667:       count: tools.length,
668:       mode,
669:       names: tools.map((tool) => tool.name),
670:     },
671:   };
672: 
673:   if (format === 'text') {
674:     return tools.map((tool) => tool.name).join('\n');
675:   }
676:   if (mode === 'compact') return renderJson(compactPayload, format);
677:   if (mode === 'names-only') return renderJson(namesOnlyPayload, format);
678:   return renderJson(payload, format);
679: }

681: function completionWords(): string[] {
682:   return ['list-tools', 'completion', ...toolDefinitions().map((tool) => tool.name)];
683: }
684: 
685: function shellWords(words: readonly string[]): string {
686:   return words.join(' ');
687: }
688: 
689: function renderBashCompletion(): string {
690:   const commands = shellWords(completionWords());
691:   const commonFlags = shellWords(['--help', '--format', '--json', '--timeout-ms', '--warm-only', '--no-warm-only']);
692:   const listFlags = shellWords(['--help', '--format', '--compact', '--names-only']);
693:   return `_code_index_completion() {
694:   local cur
695:   COMPREPLY=()
696:   cur="\${COMP_WORDS[COMP_CWORD]}"
697:   if [[ \${COMP_CWORD} -eq 1 ]]; then
698:     COMPREPLY=( $(compgen -W "${commands}" -- "\${cur}") )
699:     return 0
700:   fi
701:   case "\${COMP_WORDS[1]}" in
702:     completion) COMPREPLY=( $(compgen -W "bash zsh" -- "\${cur}") ) ;;
703:     list-tools) COMPREPLY=( $(compgen -W "${listFlags}" -- "\${cur}") ) ;;
704:     *) COMPREPLY=( $(compgen -W "${commonFlags}" -- "\${cur}") ) ;;
705:   esac
706: }
707: complete -F _code_index_completion code-index`;
708: }
709: 
710: function renderZshCompletion(): string {
711:   const commands = shellWords(completionWords());
712:   const commonFlags = shellWords(['--help', '--format', '--json', '--timeout-ms', '--warm-only', '--no-warm-only']);
713:   const listFlags = shellWords(['--help', '--format', '--compact', '--names-only']);
714:   return `#compdef code-index
715: _code_index() {
716:   local -a commands common_flags list_flags shells
717:   commands=(${commands})
718:   common_flags=(${commonFlags})
719:   list_flags=(${listFlags})
720:   shells=(bash zsh)
721:   if (( CURRENT == 2 )); then
722:     _describe 'commands' commands
723:     return
724:   fi
725:   case \${words[2]} in
726:     completion) _describe 'shells' shells ;;
727:     list-tools) _describe 'flags' list_flags ;;
728:     *) _describe 'flags' common_flags ;;
729:   esac
730: }
731: _code_index "$@"`;
732: }

734: function renderCompletion(shell: CompletionShell): string {
735:   return shell === 'bash' ? renderBashCompletion() : renderZshCompletion();
736: }

738: function extractToolPayload(toolName: string, result: unknown): { readonly payload: unknown; readonly isError: boolean } {
739:   const toolResult = result && typeof result === 'object' ? result as ToolResult : null;
740:   const firstText = toolResult?.content?.find((entry) => typeof entry.text === 'string')?.text;
741:   if (firstText) {
742:     try {
743:       return {
744:         payload: JSON.parse(firstText) as unknown,
745:         isError: toolResult?.isError === true,
746:       };
747:     } catch {
748:       return {
749:         payload: {
750:           status: toolResult?.isError ? 'error' : 'ok',
751:           tool: toolName,
752:           text: firstText,
753:         },
754:         isError: toolResult?.isError === true,
755:       };
756:     }
757:   }
758:   return {
759:     payload: {
760:       status: toolResult?.isError ? 'error' : 'ok',
761:       tool: toolName,
762:       result,
763:     },
764:     isError: toolResult?.isError === true,
765:   };
766: }

768: function isRecord(value: unknown): value is Record<string, unknown> {
769:   return typeof value === 'object' && value !== null && !Array.isArray(value);
770: }
771: 
772: function stringField(record: Record<string, unknown>, key: string): string | undefined {
773:   const value = record[key];
774:   return typeof value === 'string' && value.length > 0 ? value : undefined;
775: }
776: 
777: function inferRequiredAction(payload: Record<string, unknown>): string | undefined {
778:   const data = isRecord(payload.data) ? payload.data : null;
779:   const payloadFallback = isRecord(payload.fallbackDecision) ? payload.fallbackDecision : null;
780:   const dataFallback = data && isRecord(data.fallbackDecision) ? data.fallbackDecision : null;
781:   const readiness = data && isRecord(data.readiness) ? data.readiness : null;
782:   const text = [
783:     stringField(payload, 'message'),
784:     stringField(payload, 'blockedReason'),
785:     data ? stringField(data, 'blockReason') : undefined,
786:     data ? stringField(data, 'reason') : undefined,
787:     readiness ? stringField(readiness, 'action') : undefined,
788:   ].filter((value): value is string => Boolean(value)).join(' ').toLowerCase();
789: 
790:   return stringField(payload, 'requiredAction')
791:     ?? (data ? stringField(data, 'requiredAction') : undefined)
792:     ?? (payloadFallback ? stringField(payloadFallback, 'nextTool') : undefined)
793:     ?? (dataFallback ? stringField(dataFallback, 'nextTool') : undefined)
794:     ?? (text.includes('readiness_check_crashed') || text.includes('scan_failed') ? 'rg' : undefined)
795:     ?? (text.includes('code_graph_scan') || text.includes('full_scan') || text.includes('selective_reindex') || text.includes('stale') || text.includes('empty') ? 'code_graph_scan' : undefined);
796: }
797: 
798: function normalizeBlockedPayload(toolName: string, payload: unknown): unknown {
799:   if (!BLOCKABLE_READ_TOOLS.has(toolName) || !isRecord(payload) || payload.status !== 'blocked') {
800:     return payload;
801:   }
802: 
803:   const requiredAction = inferRequiredAction(payload);
804:   if (!requiredAction) return payload;
805:   const data = isRecord(payload.data) ? payload.data : null;
806: 
807:   return {
808:     ...payload,
809:     requiredAction,
810:     data: data ? { ...data, requiredAction } : { requiredAction },
811:   };
812: }
813: 
814: function renderBlockedText(payload: Record<string, unknown>): string {
815:   const data = isRecord(payload.data) ? payload.data : null;
816:   const reason = stringField(payload, 'message')
817:     ?? stringField(payload, 'blockedReason')
818:     ?? (data ? stringField(data, 'blockReason') : undefined)
819:     ?? (data ? stringField(data, 'reason') : undefined)
820:     ?? 'code graph read is blocked';
821:   const requiredAction = inferRequiredAction(payload) ?? 'UNKNOWN';
822:   return `blocked: ${reason}\nrequiredAction: ${requiredAction}`;
823: }
824: 
825: function renderPayload(toolName: string, payload: unknown, format: OutputFormat): string {
826:   const normalized = normalizeBlockedPayload(toolName, payload);
827:   if (format === 'json' || format === 'jsonl') return renderJson(normalized, format);
828:   if (isRecord(normalized) && normalized.status === 'blocked') {
829:     return renderBlockedText(normalized);
830:   }
831:   if (isRecord(normalized)) {
832:     if (typeof normalized.summary === 'string') return normalized.summary;
833:     if (typeof normalized.error === 'string') return normalized.error;
834:     if (typeof normalized.message === 'string') return normalized.message;
835:   }
836:   return typeof normalized === 'string' ? normalized : JSON.stringify(normalized, null, 2);
837: }
838: 
839: function isErrorPayload(payload: unknown): boolean {
840:   return isRecord(payload) && payload.status === 'error';
841: }
842: 
843: function isParseErrorPayload(payload: unknown): boolean {
844:   return isRecord(payload) && payload.status === 'parse_error';
845: }
846: 
847: function isProtocolMismatch(error: unknown): boolean {
848:   if (error instanceof JsonRpcError && error.code === -32002) return true;
849:   const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
850:   return /protocol.*(mismatch|changed|version)/i.test(message);
851: }
852: 
853: function isRetryableError(error: unknown): boolean {
854:   if (error instanceof CliRetryableError) return true;
855:   if (error instanceof JsonRpcError && error.code === -32001) return true;
856:   const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
857:   return /SQLITE_BUSY|ECONNREFUSED|ENOENT|ECONNRESET|EPIPE|ETIMEDOUT|backend unavailable|retry|cold start/i.test(message);
858: }
859: 
860: function exitCodeForError(error: unknown): number {
861:   if (error instanceof CliUsageError) return EXIT_USAGE;
862:   if (error instanceof CliProtocolError || isProtocolMismatch(error)) return EXIT_PROTOCOL;
863:   if (isRetryableError(error)) return EXIT_RETRYABLE;
864:   if (error instanceof JsonRpcError && error.code === -32602) return EXIT_USAGE;
865:   return EXIT_RUNTIME;
866: }
867: 
868: function levenshteinDistance(left: string, right: string): number {
869:   const previous = Array.from({ length: right.length + 1 }, (_value, index) => index);
870:   const current = new Array<number>(right.length + 1);
871: 
872:   for (let leftIndex = 1; leftIndex <= left.length; leftIndex += 1) {
873:     current[0] = leftIndex;
874:     for (let rightIndex = 1; rightIndex <= right.length; rightIndex += 1) {
875:       const cost = left[leftIndex - 1] === right[rightIndex - 1] ? 0 : 1;
876:       current[rightIndex] = Math.min(
877:         current[rightIndex - 1] + 1,
878:         previous[rightIndex] + 1,
879:         previous[rightIndex - 1] + cost,
880:       );
881:     }
882:     previous.splice(0, previous.length, ...current);
883:   }
884: 
885:   return previous[right.length];
886: }
887: 
888: function closestCommand(command: string): string | undefined {
889:   const targets = new Map<string, string>([['list-tools', 'list-tools']]);
890:   targets.set('completion', 'completion');
891:   for (const tool of toolDefinitions()) {
892:     for (const alias of commandAliases(tool.name)) {
893:       targets.set(alias, tool.name);
894:     }
895:   }
896: 
897:   let best: { readonly command: string; readonly distance: number } | null = null;
898:   for (const [alias, canonical] of targets) {
899:     const distance = levenshteinDistance(normalizeName(command), normalizeName(alias));
900:     if (!best || distance < best.distance || (distance === best.distance && canonical < best.command)) {
901:       best = { command: canonical, distance };
902:     }
903:   }
904: 
905:   if (!best) return undefined;
906:   const threshold = Math.max(2, Math.floor(normalizeName(command).length * 0.4));
907:   return best.distance <= threshold ? best.command : undefined;
908: }

910: function unknownCommandContext(message: string): { readonly hint: string; readonly suggestion?: string } | null {
911:   const match = message.match(/^Unknown command: (.+)$/);
912:   if (!match) return null;
913:   return {
914:     hint: 'Try `list-tools` to see available commands.',
915:     suggestion: closestCommand(match[1]),
916:   };
917: }

919: function socketPathTooLong(socketPath: string): boolean {
920:   if (socketPath.startsWith('tcp://')) return false;
921:   return process.platform === 'darwin' && Buffer.byteLength(socketPath) > 103;
922: }
923: 
924: // `mode: 0o700` only applies when mkdir CREATES the dir. A pre-existing socket dir
925: // (e.g. an attacker-planted dir or symlink on a shared host) is not protected by the
926: // mkdir above, so the client must refuse to probe/connect under a dir it does not own,
927: // a symlinked dir, or a group/world-writable dir, and must reject a foreign-owned or
928: // symlinked socket node — otherwise a fake daemon could inject attacker-controlled
929: // status text into prompt-time context instead of skipping or spawning safely.
930: function assertSocketPerimeter(socketDir: string): void {
931:   const dirLink = lstatSync(socketDir);
932:   if (dirLink.isSymbolicLink()) {
933:     throw new CliProtocolError(`IPC socket directory is a symlink; refusing to connect: ${socketDir}`);
934:   }
935:   const dirStat = statSync(socketDir);
936:   const uid = typeof process.getuid === 'function' ? process.getuid() : null;
937:   if (uid !== null && dirStat.uid !== uid) {
938:     throw new CliProtocolError(`IPC socket directory is not owned by the current user (uid ${dirStat.uid} != ${uid}): ${socketDir}`);
939:   }
940:   if ((dirStat.mode & 0o022) !== 0) {
941:     throw new CliProtocolError(`IPC socket directory is group/world-writable: ${socketDir}`);
942:   }
943:   const socketPath = path.join(socketDir, SOCKET_FILE_NAME);
944:   let nodeLink;
945:   try {
946:     nodeLink = lstatSync(socketPath);
947:   } catch (error: unknown) {
948:     const code = error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error ? (error as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code : undefined;
949:     if (code === 'ENOENT') return;
950:     throw error;
951:   }
952:   if (nodeLink.isSymbolicLink()) {
953:     throw new CliProtocolError(`IPC socket path is a symlink; refusing to connect: ${socketPath}`);
954:   }
955:   if (uid !== null && nodeLink.uid !== uid) {
956:     throw new CliProtocolError(`IPC socket is not owned by the current user (uid ${nodeLink.uid} != ${uid}): ${socketPath}`);
957:   }
958: }
959: 
960: function ensureSocketEnvironment(): void {
961:   if (!process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR) {
962:     process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR = DEFAULT_SOCKET_DIR;
963:   }
964:   const socketDir = process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR;
965:   if (!socketDir || socketDir.startsWith('tcp://')) return;
966:   mkdirSync(socketDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
967:   assertSocketPerimeter(socketDir);
968: }

970: function sleep(ms: number): Promise<void> {
971:   return new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, ms));

984: async function waitForDaemon(socketPath: string, bridge: BridgeModule, timeoutMs: number): Promise<void> {
985:   const deadline = Date.now() + timeoutMs;
986:   let lastReason = 'not probed';
987: 
988:   while (Date.now() <= deadline) {
989:     const remaining = Math.max(1, deadline - Date.now());
990:     const probe = await bridge.probeDaemon(socketPath, {
991:       timeoutMs: Math.min(remaining, 5000),
992:       deepProbe: true,
993:     });
994:     if (probe.status === 'alive') return;
995:     lastReason = probe.reason ?? probe.status;
996:     await sleep(100);
997:   }
998: 
999:   throw new CliRetryableError(`backend unavailable: ${lastReason}`);
1000: }

1002: class JsonRpcSocketClient {
1003:   private nextId = 1;
1004:   private readonly decoder = new StringDecoder('utf8');
1005: 
1006:   private constructor(
1007:     private readonly socket: net.Socket,
1008:   ) {}
1009: 
1010:   static connect(socketPath: string, bridge: BridgeModule, timeoutMs: number): Promise<JsonRpcSocketClient> {
1011:     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
1012:       let socket: net.Socket;
1013:       let settled = false;
1014:       const finish = (error?: Error): void => {
1015:         if (settled) return;
1016:         settled = true;
1017:         clearTimeout(timer);
1018:         socket.off?.('connect', onConnect);
1019:         socket.off?.('error', onError);
1020:         if (error) {
1021:           socket.destroy?.();
1022:           reject(error);
1023:           return;
1024:         }
1025:         resolve(new JsonRpcSocketClient(socket));
1026:       };
1027:       const onConnect = (): void => finish();
1028:       const onError = (error: Error): void => finish(error);
1029:       const timer = setTimeout(() => finish(new CliRetryableError('connection timed out')), timeoutMs);
1030:       timer.unref?.();
1031: 
1032:       try {
1033:         const connectionOptions = bridge.toConnectionOptions(socketPath);
1034:         socket = typeof connectionOptions === 'string'
1035:           ? net.createConnection(connectionOptions)
1036:           : net.createConnection(connectionOptions);
1037:       } catch (error: unknown) {
1038:         clearTimeout(timer);
1039:         reject(error instanceof Error ? error : new Error(String(error)));
1040:         return;
1041:       }
1042:       socket.once('connect', onConnect);
1043:       socket.once('error', onError);
1044:     });
1045:   }
1046: 
1047:   notify(method: string, params?: unknown): void {
1048:     const frame = params === undefined
1049:       ? { jsonrpc: '2.0', method }
1050:       : { jsonrpc: '2.0', method, params };
1051:     this.socket.write(`${JSON.stringify(frame)}\n`);
1052:   }
1053: 
1054:   request(method: string, params: unknown, timeoutMs: number): Promise<unknown> {
1055:     const id = this.nextId++;
1056:     const request = { jsonrpc: '2.0', id, method, params };
1057:     return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
1058:       let buffer = '';
1059:       let settled = false;
1060: 
1061:       const cleanup = (): void => {
1062:         clearTimeout(timer);
1063:         this.socket.off('data', onData);
1064:         this.socket.off('error', onError);
1065:         this.socket.off('close', onClose);
1066:       };
1067:       const finish = (error: unknown, value?: unknown): void => {
1068:         if (settled) return;
1069:         settled = true;
1070:         cleanup();
1071:         if (error) {
1072:           reject(error);
1073:           return;
1074:         }
1075:         resolve(value);
1076:       };
1077:       const onData = (chunk: Buffer | string): void => {
1078:         buffer += Buffer.isBuffer(chunk) ? this.decoder.write(chunk) : String(chunk ?? '');
1079:         let newlineIndex = buffer.indexOf('\n');
1080:         while (newlineIndex !== -1) {
1081:           const line = buffer.slice(0, newlineIndex).trim();
1082:           buffer = buffer.slice(newlineIndex + 1);
1083:           newlineIndex = buffer.indexOf('\n');
1084:           if (!line) continue;
1085:           let parsed: JsonRpcResponse;
1086:           try {
1087:             parsed = JSON.parse(line) as JsonRpcResponse;
1088:           } catch {
1089:             continue;
1090:           }
1091:           if (parsed.id !== id) continue;
1092:           if (parsed.error) {
1093:             finish(new JsonRpcError(parsed.error));
1094:             return;
1095:           }
1096:           finish(null, parsed.result);
1097:           return;
1098:         }
1099:       };
1100:       const onError = (error: Error): void => finish(error);
1101:       const onClose = (): void => finish(new CliRetryableError('socket closed before response'));
1102:       const timer = setTimeout(() => finish(new CliRetryableError(`${method} timed out`)), timeoutMs);
1103:       timer.unref?.();
1104: 
1105:       this.socket.on('data', onData);
1106:       this.socket.once('error', onError);
1107:       this.socket.once('close', onClose);
1108:       this.socket.write(`${JSON.stringify(request)}\n`);
1109:     });
1110:   }
1111: 
1112:   close(): void {
1113:     this.socket.end();
1114:     this.socket.destroy();
1115:   }
1116: }

1118: async function ensureDaemonReady(socketPath: string, bridge: BridgeModule, paths: RepoPaths, timeoutMs: number, warmOnly: boolean): Promise<void> {
1119:   const initialProbe = await bridge.probeDaemon(socketPath, { timeoutMs: Math.min(timeoutMs, 5000), deepProbe: true });
1120:   if (initialProbe.status === 'alive') return;
1121:   if (warmOnly) {
1122:     throw new CliRetryableError(`backend unavailable: ${initialProbe.reason ?? initialProbe.status}`);
1123:   }
1124:   spawnLauncher(paths);
1125:   await waitForDaemon(socketPath, bridge, timeoutMs);
1126: }
1127: 
1128: async function callTool(toolName: string, args: Record<string, unknown>, timeoutMs: number, warmOnly: boolean): Promise<unknown> {
1129:   ensureSocketEnvironment();
1130:   const paths = findRepoPaths();
1131:   const bridge = loadBridge(paths);
1132:   const socketPath = bridge.getIpcSocketPath('mk-code-index', { dbDir: paths.dbDir });
1133:   if (socketPathTooLong(socketPath)) {
1134:     throw new CliProtocolError(`IPC socket path exceeds the Darwin sun_path limit: ${socketPath}`);
1135:   }
1136: 
1137:   await ensureDaemonReady(socketPath, bridge, paths, timeoutMs, warmOnly);
1138: 
1139:   const client = await JsonRpcSocketClient.connect(socketPath, bridge, timeoutMs);
1140:   try {
1141:     const initialize = await client.request('initialize', {
1142:       protocolVersion: JSON_RPC_PROTOCOL_VERSION,
1143:       capabilities: {},
1144:       clientInfo: { name: 'code-index-cli', version: readCliVersion(paths) },
1145:     }, timeoutMs) as InitializeResult;
1146:     if (initialize.protocolVersion && initialize.protocolVersion !== JSON_RPC_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
1147:       throw new CliProtocolError(`backend protocol version changed: ${initialize.protocolVersion}`);
1148:     }
1149:     client.notify('notifications/initialized');
1150:     return await client.request('tools/call', { name: toolName, arguments: args }, timeoutMs);
1151:   } finally {
1152:     client.close();
1153:   }
1154: }

1156: export async function runCodeIndexCli(argv: string[], io: CliIo = { stdout: process.stdout, stderr: process.stderr }): Promise<number> {
1157:   try {
1158:     const parsed = parseCliArgs(argv);
1159:     if (parsed.help) {
1160:       await writeLine(io.stdout, usageText(parsed.command || undefined));
1161:       return EXIT_SUCCESS;
1162:     }
1163:     if (parsed.version) {
1164:       await writeLine(io.stdout, readCliVersion(findRepoPaths()));
1165:       return EXIT_SUCCESS;
1166:     }
1167:     if (parsed.command === 'list-tools') {
1168:       await writeLine(io.stdout, renderToolList(parsed.format, parsed.toolListMode));
1169:       return EXIT_SUCCESS;
1170:     }
1171:     if (parsed.command === 'completion') {
1172:       if (!parsed.completionShell) {
1173:         throw new CliUsageError('completion requires a shell: bash or zsh');
1174:       }
1175:       await writeLine(io.stdout, renderCompletion(parsed.completionShell));
1176:       return EXIT_SUCCESS;
1177:     }
1178: 
1179:     const validated = validateCommand(parsed);
1180:     if (!validated) {
1181:       throw new CliUsageError(`Unknown command: ${parsed.command}`);
1182:     }
1183:     const result = await callTool(validated.tool.name, validated.args, parsed.timeoutMs, parsed.warmOnly);
1184:     const { payload, isError } = extractToolPayload(validated.tool.name, result);
1185:     const renderedPayload = normalizeBlockedPayload(validated.tool.name, payload);
1186:     await writeLine(io.stdout, renderPayload(validated.tool.name, renderedPayload, parsed.format));
1187:     if (validated.tool.name === 'detect_changes' && isParseErrorPayload(renderedPayload)) {
1188:       return EXIT_USAGE;
1189:     }
1190:     return isError || isErrorPayload(renderedPayload) ? EXIT_RUNTIME : EXIT_SUCCESS;
1191:   } catch (error: unknown) {
1192:     const exitCode = exitCodeForError(error);
1193:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
1194:     const unknownCommand = unknownCommandContext(message);
1195:     const payload = {
1196:       status: 'error',
1197:       error: message,
1198:       exitCode,
1199:       ...(unknownCommand ? { hint: unknownCommand.hint } : {}),
1200:       ...(unknownCommand?.suggestion ? { suggestion: unknownCommand.suggestion } : {}),
1201:     };
1202:     const format = (() => {
1203:       try {
1204:         return parseCliArgs(argv).format;
1205:       } catch {
1206:         return 'text' as OutputFormat;
1207:       }
1208:     })();
1209:     if (format === 'json' || format === 'jsonl') {
1210:       await writeLine(io.stderr, renderJson(payload, format));
1211:     } else {
1212:       await writeLine(io.stderr, [
1213:         `ERROR: ${message}`,
1214:         unknownCommand ? `Hint: ${unknownCommand.hint}` : null,
1215:         unknownCommand?.suggestion ? `Did you mean \`${unknownCommand.suggestion}\`?` : null,
1216:       ].filter((line): line is string => Boolean(line)).join('\n'));
1217:     }
1218:     return exitCode;
1219:   }

248:   for (let index = 0; index < argv.length;) {
249:     const value = argv[index];
250:     if (value === '--minimal') {
251:       options.minimal = true;
252:       index += 1;
253:       continue;
254:     }
255:     if (value === '--spec-folder') {
256:       const read = readOptionValue(argv, index, value);
257:       options.specFolder = read.value;
258:       index = read.nextIndex;
259:       continue;
260:     }
261:     if (value === '--tool' || value === '--tool-name') {
262:       const read = readOptionValue(argv, index, value);
263:       options.toolName = read.value;
264:       index = read.nextIndex;
265:       continue;
266:     }
267:     if (value === '--json') {
268:       const read = readOptionValue(argv, index, value);
269:       const parsed = JSON.parse(read.value);
270:       options.args = isRecord(parsed) ? parsed : {};
271:       index = read.nextIndex;
272:       continue;
273:     }
274:     if (value === '--timeout-ms') {
275:       const read = readOptionValue(argv, index, value);
276:       options.timeoutMs = positiveInt(Number(read.value), DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS);
277:       index = read.nextIndex;
278:       continue;
279:     }
280:     if (value === '--probe-timeout-ms') {
281:       const read = readOptionValue(argv, index, value);
282:       options.probeTimeoutMs = positiveInt(Number(read.value), DEFAULT_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS);
283:       index = read.nextIndex;
284:       continue;
285:     }
286:     if (value === '--max-brief-chars') {
287:       const read = readOptionValue(argv, index, value);
288:       options.maxBriefChars = positiveInt(Number(read.value), 2400);
289:       index = read.nextIndex;
290:       continue;
291:     }
292:     index += 1;
293:   }
294: 
295:   return options;
296: }
297: 
298: async function runCli(input) {
299:   const startedAt = Date.now();
300:   const timeoutMs = positiveInt(Number(input.timeoutMs), DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS);
301:   const probeTimeoutMs = Math.min(positiveInt(Number(input.probeTimeoutMs), DEFAULT_PROBE_TIMEOUT_MS), timeoutMs);
302:   const toolName = typeof input.toolName === 'string' && input.toolName.trim()
303:     ? input.toolName.trim()
304:     : 'code-graph-status';
305:   if (MAINTENANCE_TOOLS.has(normalizeToolName(toolName))) {
306:     return skipped('maintenance_tool_blocked', {
307:       route: 'prompt_safe_policy',
308:       toolName,
309:       retryable: false,
310:       durationMs: Date.now() - startedAt,
311:     });
312:   }
313: 
314:   const probe = await warmProbe(probeTimeoutMs);
315:   const afterProbeMs = Date.now() - startedAt;
316:   if (!probe.warm) {
317:     return skipped(probe.reason, {
318:       route: 'warm_probe',
319:       retryable: true,
320:       durationMs: afterProbeMs,
321:       socketPath: probe.socketPath ? '[code-index-socket]' : null,
322:       exitCode: 75,
323:     });
324:   }
325: 
326:   const remainingMs = Math.max(1, timeoutMs - afterProbeMs);
327:   const args = isRecord(input.args) ? { ...input.args } : {};
328:   const cliArgs = [
329:     CLI_SHIM,
330:     toolName,
331:     '--format',
332:     'json',
333:     '--timeout-ms',
334:     String(remainingMs),
335:     '--warm-only',
336:   ];
337:   if (Object.keys(args).length > 0) {
338:     cliArgs.push('--json', JSON.stringify(args));
339:   }
340: 
341:   return new Promise((resolveResult) => {
342:     const child = spawn(process.execPath, cliArgs, {
343:       cwd: typeof input.workspaceRoot === 'string' && input.workspaceRoot.trim() ? input.workspaceRoot.trim() : REPO_ROOT,
344:       env: {
345:         ...process.env,
346:         SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR: process.env.SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR ?? DEFAULT_SOCKET_DIR,
347:         SPECKIT_CODE_INDEX_CLI_PROMPT_TIME: '1',
348:       },
349:       stdio: ['ignore', 'pipe', 'pipe'],
350:     });
351:     let stdout = '';
352:     let stderr = '';
353:     let settled = false;
354:     let timedOut = false;
355:     const finish = (exitCode, error) => {
356:       if (settled) return;
357:       settled = true;
358:       clearTimeout(timer);
359:       const payload = parseStdout(stdout);
360:       const durationMs = Date.now() - startedAt;
361:       if (exitCode === 0 && payload) {
362:         const brief = renderCodeGraphBrief(payload, positiveInt(Number(input.maxBriefChars), 2400));
363:         const opencodeTransport = brief ? buildTransportPlan(brief) : null;
364:         resolveResult(response({
365:           status: 'ok',
366:           brief,
367:           data: {
368:             toolName,
369:             codeGraphStatus: payload,
370:             ...(opencodeTransport ? { opencodeTransport } : {}),
371:           },
372:           metadata: {
373:             route: 'cli',
374:             toolName,
375:             warm: true,
376:             durationMs,
377:             exitCode,
378:             socketPath: '[code-index-socket]',
379:           },
380:         }));
381:         return;
382:       }
383:       const failure = classifyCliFailure(exitCode, stderr, timedOut);
384:       resolveResult(response({
385:         status: failure.status,
386:         brief: failure.brief,
387:         data: payload,
388:         error: error ?? failure.error,
389:         metadata: {
390:           route: 'cli',
391:           toolName,
392:           warm: true,
393:           durationMs,
394:           exitCode,
395:           retryable: failure.retryable,
396:           ...failure.metadata,
397:           stderr: stderr ? '[stderr-present]' : null,
398:         },
399:       }));
400:     };
401:     const timer = setTimeout(() => {
402:       timedOut = true;
403:       child.kill('SIGTERM');
404:       setTimeout(() => {
405:         if (!settled) child.kill('SIGKILL');
406:       }, 100).unref?.();
407:     }, remainingMs);
408:     timer.unref?.();
409:     child.stdout?.setEncoding?.('utf8');
410:     child.stderr?.setEncoding?.('utf8');
411:     child.stdout?.on('data', (chunk) => {
412:       stdout = capOutput(stdout, chunk);
413:     });
414:     child.stderr?.on('data', (chunk) => {
415:       stderr = capOutput(stderr, chunk);
416:     });
417:     child.on('error', (error) => finish(null, error.message));
418:     child.on('close', (code) => finish(code, null));
419:   });
420: }
421: 
422: async function main() {
423:   return runCli(parseArgs(process.argv.slice(2)));
424: }
425: 
426: if (process.argv[1] && fileURLToPath(import.meta.url) === process.argv[1]) {
427:   main()
428:     .then((payload) => {
429:       process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(payload));
430:     })
431:     .catch((error) => {
432:       process.stdout.write(JSON.stringify(failOpen(error?.code ?? error?.name ?? 'UNKNOWN')));
433:     });
434: }
435: 
436: export { classifyCliFailure, renderCodeGraphBrief, response, runCli, warmProbe };

86: function summarizeDetectorProvenance(
87:   results: Array<{ detectorProvenance?: DetectorProvenance }>,
88: ): graphDb.DetectorProvenanceSummary {
89:   const counts: Partial<Record<DetectorProvenance, number>> = {};
90:   let dominant: DetectorProvenance | 'unknown' = 'unknown';
91:   let dominantCount = 0;
92: 
93:   for (const result of results) {
94:     if (!result.detectorProvenance) {
95:       continue;
96:     }
97:     const nextCount = (counts[result.detectorProvenance] ?? 0) + 1;
98:     counts[result.detectorProvenance] = nextCount;
99:     if (nextCount > dominantCount) {
100:       dominantCount = nextCount;
101:       dominant = result.detectorProvenance;
102:     }
103:   }
104: 
105:   return { dominant, counts };

108: function summarizeGraphEdgeEnrichment(
109:   results: Array<{ edges: CodeEdge[] }>,
110: ): graphDb.GraphEdgeEnrichmentSummary | null {
111:   let best: graphDb.GraphEdgeEnrichmentSummary | null = null;
112: 
113:   for (const result of results) {
114:     for (const edge of result.edges) {
115:       const metadata = edge.metadata;
116:       if (!metadata || typeof metadata.confidence !== 'number') {
117:         continue;
118:       }
119: 
120:       const edgeEvidenceClass = (() => {
121:         switch (edge.edgeType) {
122:           case 'IMPORTS':
123:           case 'EXPORTS':
124:             return 'import' as const;
125:           case 'EXTENDS':
126:           case 'IMPLEMENTS':
127:           case 'TYPE_OF':
128:             return 'type_reference' as const;
129:           case 'TESTED_BY':
130:             return 'test_coverage' as const;
131:           default:
132:             return metadata.detectorProvenance === 'heuristic' || metadata.evidenceClass === 'INFERRED'
133:               ? 'inferred_heuristic' as const
134:               : 'direct_call' as const;
135:         }
136:       })();
137: 
138:       if (!best || metadata.confidence > best.numericConfidence) {
139:         best = {
140:           edgeEvidenceClass,
141:           numericConfidence: metadata.confidence,
142:         };
143:       }
144:     }
145:   }
146: 
147:   return best;
148: }

324: /** Handle code_graph_scan tool call */
325: export async function handleCodeGraphScan(args: ScanArgs): Promise<{ content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> }> {
326:   const startTime = Date.now();
327:   const rootDir = args.rootDir ?? process.cwd();
328:   const incremental = args.incremental !== false;
329:   const resolvedRootDir = resolve(rootDir);
330: 
331:   // SECURITY: Canonicalize paths via realpathSync() to prevent symlink bypass.
332:   // A symlink inside the workspace pointing outside it would pass a lexical
333:   // startsWith() check on the resolved (but not canonicalized) path.
334:   const canonical = canonicalizeWorkspacePaths(resolvedRootDir);
335:   if (!canonical) {
336:     // Broken symlink or non-existent path — reject immediately
337:     return {
338:       content: [{
339:         type: 'text',
340:         text: JSON.stringify({
341:           status: 'error',
342:           error: `rootDir path is invalid or contains a broken symlink: ${relativize(resolvedRootDir, process.cwd())}`,
343:         }),
344:       }],
345:     };
346:   }
347:   const { canonicalWorkspace, canonicalRootDir } = canonical;
348: 
349:   if (!isWithinWorkspace(canonicalWorkspace, canonicalRootDir)) {
350:     return {
351:       content: [{
352:         type: 'text',
353:         text: JSON.stringify({
354:           status: 'error',
355:           error: `rootDir must stay within the workspace root; received: ${relativize(canonicalRootDir, canonicalWorkspace)}`,
356:         }),
357:       }],
358:     };
359:   }
360: 
361:   const scopePolicy = resolveIndexScopePolicy({
362:     includeSkills: args.includeSkills,
363:     includeAgents: args.includeAgents,
364:     includeCommands: args.includeCommands,
365:     includeSpecs: args.includeSpecs,
366:     includePlugins: args.includePlugins,
367:     includeGlobs: args.includeGlobs,
368:     excludeGlobs: args.excludeGlobs,
369:   });
370:   const config = getDefaultConfig(canonicalRootDir, scopePolicy);
371:   const initialSkipListCount = getSkipListSummary().count;
372: 
373:   const previousGitHead = graphDb.getLastGitHead();
374:   const currentGitHead = getCurrentGitHead(canonicalRootDir);
375:   const gitHeadChanged = previousGitHead !== null
376:     && currentGitHead !== null
377:     && previousGitHead !== currentGitHead;
378:   const effectiveIncremental = incremental;
379:   const fullReindexTriggered = gitHeadChanged && !effectiveIncremental;
380: 
381:   if (gitHeadChanged && incremental) {
382:     console.error(`[code-graph-scan] Git HEAD changed (${previousGitHead} -> ${currentGitHead}); honoring incremental content-hash reindex`);
383:   }
384: 
385:   // An explicit full scan is a deliberate retry, so clear any prior global parser
386:   // quarantine before indexing. A B2 ("memory access out of bounds") quarantine
387:   // otherwise persists until a launcher restart, making every full scan return
388:   // zero nodes (then rejected by the zero-node guard below). resetParserHealth()
389:   // drops the corrupted parser instance so indexing re-initializes a fresh
390:   // web-tree-sitter instance. Incremental scans do NOT reset — only an explicit
391:   // `incremental: false` request signals intent to retry.
392:   if (args.incremental === false) {
393:     resetParserHealth();
394:   }
395: 
396:   const results = await indexFiles(config, { skipFreshFiles: effectiveIncremental });
397:   const detectorProvenanceSummary = summarizeDetectorProvenance(results);
398:   let graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary = summarizeGraphEdgeEnrichment(results);
399:   const preParseSkippedCount = effectiveIncremental ? (results.preParseSkippedCount ?? 0) : 0;
400:   const unsupportedLanguageSkipped = results.unsupportedLanguageSkipped ?? 0;
401:   const priorStats = graphDb.getStats();
402:   const priorNodeCount = priorStats.totalNodes;
403:   const candidatePersistableNodeCount = countPersistableNodes(results);
404:   const parseErrorClassification = classifyParseErrorFiles(results);
405:   const parseErrorCount = parseErrorClassification.realErrorCount;
406:   const parserSkipListBypassCount = parseErrorClassification.skipListBypassCount;
407:   // Skip-list bypasses are intentionally not parsed, so exclude them from the
408:   // ratio denominator. The fatal threshold should measure degradation among
409:   // files the parser actually attempted, especially in narrow incremental scans.
410:   const parseErrorRatio = computeParseErrorRatio(parseErrorCount, results.length - parserSkipListBypassCount);
411:   const severeParseErrorScan = parseErrorRatio > DEFAULT_FATAL_PARSE_ERROR_RATIO;
412:   const fullScan = !effectiveIncremental;
413:   const storedScope = graphDb.getStoredCodeGraphScope();
414:   const candidateFingerprint = scopePolicy.fingerprint;
415:   const scopeChangePromotionBlocked = fullScan
416:     && priorStats.totalNodes > 0
417:     && storedScope?.fingerprint
418:     && !scopeFingerprintsMatchOrLegacy(storedScope.fingerprint, candidateFingerprint)
419:     && args.forceScopeChange !== true;
420:   const zeroNodePromotionBlocked = fullScan
421:     && candidatePersistableNodeCount === 0
422:     && priorNodeCount > 0
423:     && args.forceZeroNodeReset !== true;
424: 
425:   if (scopeChangePromotionBlocked) {
426:     recordParseDiagnosticsForResults(results);
427:     const reason = 'scope_change_scan_rejected';
428:     const failedScan = graphDb.recordFailedScan({
429:       reason,
430:       totalFiles: results.length,
431:       totalNodes: candidatePersistableNodeCount,
432:       parseErrorCount,
433:       parseErrorRatio,
434:       previousGitHead,
435:       currentGitHead,
436:       scopeFingerprint: scopePolicy.fingerprint,
437:       scopeLabel: scopePolicy.label,

442:     );
443:     const parseDiagnostics = relativizeParseDiagnostics(graphDb.getParseDiagnosticsSummary(), canonicalWorkspace);
444:     const skipListSummary = getSkipListSummary();
445:     const readinessBlock = buildReadinessBlock({
446:       freshness: 'stale',
447:       action: 'full_scan',
448:       inlineIndexPerformed: false,
449:       reason: 'scope-change scan rejected to preserve existing graph state',

451: 
452:     return {
453:       content: [{
454:         type: 'text',
455:         text: JSON.stringify({
456:           status: 'blocked',
457:           reason,
458:           data: {
459:             filesScanned: results.length,
460:             filesIndexed: 0,
461:             filesSkipped: preParseSkippedCount + unsupportedLanguageSkipped,
462:             unsupportedLanguageSkipped,
463:             parserSkipListBypassCount,
464:             totalNodes: priorStats.totalNodes,
465:             totalEdges: priorStats.totalEdges,
466:             errors: results.flatMap((result) => {
467:               const filePath = relativize(result.filePath, canonicalWorkspace);
468:               return result.parseErrors.map((error) => `${filePath}: ${relativizeScanError(error, canonicalWorkspace)}`);
469:             }).slice(0, 10),
470:             durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,
471:             fullScanRequested: args.incremental === false,
472:             effectiveIncremental,
473:             fullReindexTriggered,
474:             currentGitHead,
475:             previousGitHead,
476:             detectorProvenanceSummary,
477:             graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary,
478:             parseDiagnostics,

481:               healed: 0,

483:             },
484:             staleButValidGraphFiles: graphDb.countStaleButValidParseDiagnostics(),
485:             failedScan,

500:   }
501: 
502:   if (zeroNodePromotionBlocked) {
503:     recordParseDiagnosticsForResults(results);
504:     const reason = 'zero_node_scan_rejected';
505:     const failedScan = graphDb.recordFailedScan({
506:       reason,
507:       totalFiles: results.length,
508:       totalNodes: candidatePersistableNodeCount,
509:       parseErrorCount,
510:       parseErrorRatio,
511:       previousGitHead,
512:       currentGitHead,
513:       scopeFingerprint: scopePolicy.fingerprint,
514:       scopeLabel: scopePolicy.label,
515:       errors: results.flatMap((result) => result.parseErrors).slice(0, 10),
516:     });
517:     console.warn(
518:       `[code-graph-scan] Blocked zero-node full scan promotion over existing graph (${priorNodeCount} prior node(s)); pass forceZeroNodeReset:true to allow destructive reset.`,
519:     );
520:     const parseDiagnostics = relativizeParseDiagnostics(graphDb.getParseDiagnosticsSummary(), canonicalWorkspace);
521:     const skipListSummary = getSkipListSummary();
522:     const readinessBlock = buildReadinessBlock({
523:       freshness: 'stale',
524:       action: 'full_scan',
525:       inlineIndexPerformed: false,
526:       reason: 'zero-node scan rejected to preserve existing graph state',
527:     });
528: 
529:     return {
530:       content: [{
531:         type: 'text',
532:         text: JSON.stringify({
533:           status: 'blocked',
534:           reason,
535:           data: {
536:             filesScanned: results.length,
537:             filesIndexed: 0,
538:             filesSkipped: preParseSkippedCount + unsupportedLanguageSkipped,
539:             unsupportedLanguageSkipped,
540:             parserSkipListBypassCount,
541:             totalNodes: priorStats.totalNodes,
542:             totalEdges: priorStats.totalEdges,
543:             errors: results.flatMap((result) => {
544:               const filePath = relativize(result.filePath, canonicalWorkspace);
545:               return result.parseErrors.map((error) => `${filePath}: ${relativizeScanError(error, canonicalWorkspace)}`);
546:             }).slice(0, 10),
547:             durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,
548:             fullScanRequested: args.incremental === false,
549:             effectiveIncremental,
550:             fullReindexTriggered,
551:             currentGitHead,
552:             previousGitHead,
553:             detectorProvenanceSummary,
554:             graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary,
555:             parseDiagnostics,
556:             parserSkipList: {
557:               added: Math.max(0, skipListSummary.count - initialSkipListCount),
558:               healed: 0,
559:               totalAfterScan: skipListSummary.count,
560:             },
561:             staleButValidGraphFiles: graphDb.countStaleButValidParseDiagnostics(),
562:             failedScan,
563:             warnings: [
564:               `zero-node scan rejected; existing graph has ${priorNodeCount} node(s)`,
565:               ...(results.warnings ?? []).map(warning => relativizeScanWarning(warning, canonicalWorkspace)),
566:             ],
567:             capExceeded: results.capExceeded ?? { maxNodes: false, depth: false, gitignoreSize: false },
568:             readiness: readinessBlock,
569:             canonicalReadiness: readinessBlock.canonicalReadiness,
570:             trustState: readinessBlock.trustState,
571:             lastPersistedAt: priorStats.lastScanTimestamp,
572:           },
573:         }, null, 2),
574:       }],
575:     };
576:   }
577: 
578:   let filesIndexed = 0;
579:   let filesSkipped = preParseSkippedCount + unsupportedLanguageSkipped;
580:   let totalNodes = 0;
581:   let totalEdges = 0;
582:   const errors: string[] = [];
583:   const structuralErrors: string[] = [];
584:   const parserSkipWarnings: string[] = [];
585: 
586:   if (effectiveIncremental) {
587:     cleanupMissingTrackedFiles(graphDb.getTrackedFiles());
588:   } else if (!severeParseErrorScan) {
589:     const indexedPaths = new Set(results.map((result) => result.filePath));
590:     for (const filePath of graphDb.getTrackedFiles()) {
591:       if (!indexedPaths.has(filePath)) {
592:         graphDb.removeFile(filePath, { reason: 'full_scan_unindexed_tracked_file' });
593:       }
594:     }
595:   }
596: 
597:   for (const result of results) {
598:     // Skip unchanged files in incremental mode
599:     if (
600:       effectiveIncremental
601:       && !graphDb.isFileStale(
602:         result.filePath,
603:         result.contentHash ? { currentContentHash: result.contentHash } : undefined,
604:       )
605:     ) {
606:       filesSkipped++;
607:       continue;
608:     }
609: 
610:     try {
611:       // Defer the dangling-target edge prune per-file. A full scan
612:       // persists files one at a time, so a cross-file IMPORTS edge whose target
613:       // lives in a not-yet-persisted file would be pruned here and never
614:       // restored. We sweep once with pruneDanglingEdges() after the loop and
615:       // cross-file resolution, when every target node exists.
616:       persistIndexedFileResult(result, { deferDanglingTargetPrune: true });
617: 
618:       if (result.parseHealth !== 'error') {
619:         filesIndexed++;
620:         totalNodes += result.nodes.length;
621:         totalEdges += result.edges.length;
622:       }
623:     } catch (err: unknown) {
624:       const filePath = relativize(result.filePath, canonicalWorkspace);
625:       const message = `${filePath}: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`;
626:       errors.push(message);
627:       structuralErrors.push(message);
628:     }
629: 
630:     if (result.parseErrors.length > 0) {
631:       const filePath = relativize(result.filePath, canonicalWorkspace);
632:       // Parser skip-list entries are the designed safety net for grammars that
633:       // crash on certain inputs (B1: tree-sitter API quirk; B2: WASM trap).
634:       // Route them to warnings so they do not pollute the error count or
635:       // obscure real persistence failures in failedScan.errors.
636:       for (const parseError of result.parseErrors) {
637:         const message = `${filePath}: ${parseError}`;
638:         if (parseError.includes('Parser skipped by skip-list')) {
639:           parserSkipWarnings.push(message);
640:         } else {
641:           errors.push(message);
642:         }
643:       }
644:     }
645:   }
646: 
647:   const scanPromotable = !severeParseErrorScan && structuralErrors.length === 0;
648:   const failedScanErrors = [
649:     ...structuralErrors,
650:     ...errors.filter(error => !structuralErrors.includes(error)),
651:   ].slice(0, 10);
652:   const failedScan = scanPromotable
653:     ? null
654:     : graphDb.recordFailedScan({
655:       reason: severeParseErrorScan ? 'parse_error_threshold_exceeded' : 'structural_persistence_error',
656:       totalFiles: results.length,
657:       totalNodes: candidatePersistableNodeCount,
658:       parseErrorCount,
659:       parseErrorRatio,
660:       previousGitHead,
661:       currentGitHead,
662:       scopeFingerprint: scopePolicy.fingerprint,
663:       scopeLabel: scopePolicy.label,
664:       errors: failedScanErrors,
665:     });
666: 
667:   if (scanPromotable && filesIndexed > 0 && results.length > 0) {
668:     graphDb.setLastDetectorProvenance(results[0].detectorProvenance);
669:   }
670: 
671:   if (scanPromotable && currentGitHead) {
672:     graphDb.setLastGitHead(currentGitHead);
673:   }
674:   if (scanPromotable && detectorProvenanceSummary.dominant !== 'unknown') {
675:     graphDb.setLastDetectorProvenance(detectorProvenanceSummary.dominant);
676:   }
677:   if (scanPromotable) {
678:     graphDb.setLastDetectorProvenanceSummary(detectorProvenanceSummary);
679:     graphDb.setCodeGraphScope(scopePolicy);
680:   }
681: 
682:   // Refresh candidate manifest after a successful scan so
683:   // the next detectState() has a current baseline to compare against. Without
684:   // this, code_graph_status reports stale ("candidate manifest drift") on the
685:   // very next call after an explicit user-triggered scan. Incremental scans
686:   // also discover new indexable files via find-candidates, so refresh their
687:   // manifest after successful promotion too.
688:   if (scanPromotable) {
689:     try {
690:       recordCandidateManifest(graphDb.getTrackedFiles());
691:     } catch {
692:       // Best-effort: manifest recording must never block a successful scan
693:     }
694:   }
695: 
696:   const hasPersistedBaseline = hasUsablePersistedEdgeDistributionBaseline();
697:   if (
698:     !effectiveIncremental
699:     && scanPromotable
700:     && (!hasPersistedBaseline || args.persistBaseline === true)
701:   ) {
702:     const distribution = summarizeEdgeDistribution(results);
703:     graphDb.setCodeGraphMetadata('edge_distribution_baseline', JSON.stringify(distribution));
704:   }
705: 
706:   const crossFileCallResolution = filesIndexed > 0 && scanPromotable
707:     ? resolveCrossFileCallEdges()
708:     : { resolved: 0, unresolved: 0, ambiguousSkipped: 0 };
709:   if (hasCrossFileCallResolutionActivity(crossFileCallResolution)) {
710:     graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary = {
711:       ...(graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary ?? {
712:         edgeEvidenceClass: 'inferred_heuristic' as const,
713:         numericConfidence: 0.8,
714:       }),
715:       crossFileCallResolution,
716:     };
717:   }
718: 
719:   if (scanPromotable && filesIndexed > 0 && graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary) {
720:     graphDb.setLastGraphEdgeEnrichmentSummary(graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary);
721:   } else if (scanPromotable && filesIndexed > 0) {
722:     graphDb.clearLastGraphEdgeEnrichmentSummary();
723:   }
724: 
725:   // Now that every file's nodes are persisted and cross-file CALL
726:   // edges are resolved, sweep genuinely-dangling edges ONCE. Per-file
727:   // replaceEdges deferred this prune so forward-referenced cross-file IMPORTS
728:   // edges (importer persisted before the imported file) survived; here their
729:   // targets exist, so only truly-orphaned edges are removed.
730:   if (filesIndexed > 0) {
731:     graphDb.pruneDanglingEdges();
732:   }
733: 
734:   // Report POST-PERSIST DB counts so the scan response
735:   // matches what the next code_graph_status will see. The pre-persist sums
736:   // (totalNodes/totalEdges accumulated above) double-count edges that get
737:   // deduped during persistence and miss edges added by enrichment that runs
738:   // before the response is built — leading to a confusing ~1k delta between
739:   // scan response and immediate status. The DB read is cheap (2 COUNT(*)
740:   // queries) and gives a single source of truth for graph cardinality.
741:   const persistedStats = graphDb.getStats();
742:   const responseTotalNodes = persistedStats.totalNodes;
743:   const responseTotalEdges = persistedStats.totalEdges;
744:   const tombstones = persistedStats.tombstones ?? emptyTombstoneSummary();
745:   const parseDiagnostics = relativizeParseDiagnostics(graphDb.getParseDiagnosticsSummary(), canonicalWorkspace);
746:   const skipListSummary = getSkipListSummary();
747: 
748:   const scanResult: ScanResult = {
749:     filesScanned: results.length,
750:     filesIndexed,
751:     filesSkipped,
752:     unsupportedLanguageSkipped,
753:     parserSkipListBypassCount,
754:     totalNodes: responseTotalNodes,
755:     totalEdges: responseTotalEdges,
756:     errors: errors.slice(0, 10).map(error => relativizeScanError(error, canonicalWorkspace)),
757:     durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,
758:     fullScanRequested: args.incremental === false,
759:     effectiveIncremental,
760:     fullReindexTriggered,
761:     currentGitHead,
762:     previousGitHead,
763:     detectorProvenanceSummary,
764:     graphEdgeEnrichmentSummary,
765:     tombstones,
766:     parseDiagnostics,
767:     parserSkipList: {
768:       added: Math.max(0, skipListSummary.count - initialSkipListCount),
769:       healed: 0,
770:       totalAfterScan: skipListSummary.count,
771:     },
772:     staleButValidGraphFiles: graphDb.countStaleButValidParseDiagnostics(),
773:     failedScan,
774:     warnings: [
775:       ...(severeParseErrorScan
776:         ? [`scan metadata promotion blocked: real parse error ratio ${parseErrorRatio.toFixed(2)} exceeds ${DEFAULT_FATAL_PARSE_ERROR_RATIO}`]
777:         : []),
778:       ...parserSkipWarnings.slice(0, 10),
779:       ...(results.warnings ?? []).map(warning => relativizeScanWarning(warning, canonicalWorkspace)),
780:     ],
781:     capExceeded: results.capExceeded ?? { maxNodes: false, depth: false, gitignoreSize: false },
782:   };
783:   const lastPersistedAt = persistedStats.lastScanTimestamp;
784:   const shouldVerify = args.verify === true && incremental === false;
785: 
786:   if (shouldVerify) {
787:     const verification = {
788:       ...(await executeBattery(loadGoldBattery(DEFAULT_GOLD_BATTERY_PATH), handleCodeGraphQuery)),
789:       batteryPath: DEFAULT_GOLD_BATTERY_PATH,
790:     };
791:     graphDb.setLastGoldVerification(verification);
792:     scanResult.verification = verification;
793:   }
794: 
795:   const readinessBlock = buildReadinessBlock({
796:     freshness: lastPersistedAt ? 'fresh' : 'empty',
797:     action: fullReindexTriggered || !effectiveIncremental ? 'full_scan' : 'selective_reindex',
798:     inlineIndexPerformed: true,
799:     reason: lastPersistedAt
800:       ? 'scan completed and persisted current graph state'
801:       : 'scan completed but no graph data was persisted',
802:   });
803: 
804:   return {
805:     content: [{
806:       type: 'text',
807:       text: JSON.stringify({
808:         status: 'ok',
809:         data: {
810:           ...scanResult,
811:           readiness: readinessBlock,
812:           canonicalReadiness: readinessBlock.canonicalReadiness,
813:           trustState: readinessBlock.trustState,
814:           lastPersistedAt,
815:         },
816:       }, null, 2),
817:     }],
818:   };
819: }

103: function resolveIgnoreFactory(ignoreModule: unknown): IgnoreFactory | null {
104:   let candidate = ignoreModule;
105:   for (let i = 0; i < 4; i++) {
106:     if (
107:       candidate
108:       && (typeof candidate === 'object' || typeof candidate === 'function')
109:       && 'default' in Object(candidate)
110:       && (candidate as { default?: unknown }).default !== candidate
111:     ) {
112:       candidate = (candidate as { default?: unknown }).default;
113:       continue;
114:     }
115:     if (typeof candidate === 'function') {
116:       return candidate as IgnoreFactory;
117:     }
118:     break;
119:   }
120:   return null;
121: }

196: function findBraceBlockEndLine(lines: string[], startIndex: number): number {
197:   const startLine = lines[startIndex];
198:   const firstBraceIndex = startLine.indexOf('{');
199:   if (firstBraceIndex === -1) return startIndex + 1;
200: 
201:   let depth = 0;
202:   for (let i = startIndex; i < lines.length; i++) {
203:     const line = lines[i];
204:     const columnStart = i === startIndex ? firstBraceIndex : 0;
205: 
206:     for (let j = columnStart; j < line.length; j++) {
207:       if (line[j] === '{') depth++;
208:       if (line[j] === '}') {
209:         depth--;
210:         if (depth === 0) return i + 1;
211:       }
212:     }
213:   }
214: 
215:   return startIndex + 1;
216: }
