220: }
221: 
222: function findPythonBlockEndLine(lines: string[], startIndex: number): number {
223:   const baseIndent = getIndentLevel(lines[startIndex]);
224:   let endLine = startIndex + 1;
225: 
226:   for (let i = startIndex + 1; i < lines.length; i++) {
227:     const line = lines[i];
228:     if (line.trim().length === 0) continue;
229: 
230:     const indent = getIndentLevel(line);
231:     if (indent <= baseIndent) break;
232:     endLine = i + 1;
233:   }
234: 
235:   return endLine;
236: }
237: 
238: function getCaptureFqName(capture: Pick<RawCapture, 'name' | 'parentName'>): string {
239:   return capture.parentName ? `${capture.parentName}.${capture.name}` : capture.name;
240: }
241: 
242: function splitTopLevel(text: string, delimiter: string): string[] {
243:   const parts: string[] = [];
244:   let current = '';
245:   let angleDepth = 0;
246:   let parenDepth = 0;
247:   let bracketDepth = 0;
248:   let braceDepth = 0;
249: 
250:   for (const ch of text) {
251:     if (
252:       ch === delimiter &&
253:       angleDepth === 0 &&
254:       parenDepth === 0 &&
255:       bracketDepth === 0 &&
256:       braceDepth === 0
257:     ) {
258:       const part = current.trim();
259:       if (part) parts.push(part);
260:       current = '';
261:       continue;
262:     }
263: 
264:     current += ch;
265: 
266:     if (ch === '<') angleDepth++;
267:     else if (ch === '>') angleDepth = Math.max(0, angleDepth - 1);
268:     else if (ch === '(') parenDepth++;
269:     else if (ch === ')') parenDepth = Math.max(0, parenDepth - 1);
270:     else if (ch === '[') bracketDepth++;
271:     else if (ch === ']') bracketDepth = Math.max(0, bracketDepth - 1);
272:     else if (ch === '{') braceDepth++;
273:     else if (ch === '}') braceDepth = Math.max(0, braceDepth - 1);
274:   }
275: 
276:   const tail = current.trim();
277:   if (tail) parts.push(tail);
278:   return parts;
279: }
280: 
281: function splitTopLevelOnce(text: string, delimiter: string): [string, string | undefined] {
282:   let angleDepth = 0;
283:   let parenDepth = 0;
284:   let bracketDepth = 0;
285:   let braceDepth = 0;
286: 
287:   for (let i = 0; i < text.length; i++) {
288:     const ch = text[i];
289:     if (
290:       ch === delimiter &&
291:       angleDepth === 0 &&
292:       parenDepth === 0 &&
293:       bracketDepth === 0 &&
294:       braceDepth === 0
295:     ) {
296:       return [text.slice(0, i).trim(), text.slice(i + 1).trim()];
297:     }
298: 
299:     if (ch === '<') angleDepth++;
300:     else if (ch === '>') angleDepth = Math.max(0, angleDepth - 1);
301:     else if (ch === '(') parenDepth++;
302:     else if (ch === ')') parenDepth = Math.max(0, parenDepth - 1);
303:     else if (ch === '[') bracketDepth++;
304:     else if (ch === ']') bracketDepth = Math.max(0, bracketDepth - 1);
305:     else if (ch === '{') braceDepth++;
306:     else if (ch === '}') braceDepth = Math.max(0, braceDepth - 1);
307:   }
308: 
309:   return [text.trim(), undefined];
310: }
311: 
312: function extractTypeReferences(typeExpression?: string): string[] | undefined {
313:   if (!typeExpression) return undefined;
314: 
315:   const excludedNames = new Set([
316:     'string', 'number', 'boolean', 'void', 'any', 'unknown', 'never',
317:     'object', 'undefined', 'null', 'true', 'false', 'keyof', 'typeof',
318:     'infer', 'extends', 'readonly', 'in', 'out', 'is', 'as',
319:   ]);
320: 
321:   const matches = typeExpression.match(/\b[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*\b/g) ?? [];
322:   const refs = matches.filter(name => !excludedNames.has(name));
323:   if (refs.length === 0) return undefined;
324:   return [...new Set(refs)];
325: }
326: 
327: function extractDecoratorNames(line: string): string[] {
328:   const matches = line.matchAll(/@([A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*)/g);
329:   const names = [...matches].map(match => match[1]);
330:   return [...new Set(names)];
331: }
332: 
333: function consumeDecorators(pendingDecorators: string[]): string[] | undefined {
334:   if (pendingDecorators.length === 0) return undefined;
335:   const names = [...new Set(pendingDecorators)];
336:   pendingDecorators.length = 0;
337:   return names;
338: }
339: 
340: function parseImportCaptureNames(
341:   clause: string,
342:   statementImportKind: RawCapture['importKind'],
343: ): Array<{ name: string; importKind: RawCapture['importKind'] }> {
344:   const captures: Array<{ name: string; importKind: RawCapture['importKind'] }> = [];
345:   const defaultAndRemainderMatch = clause.match(/^(\w+)\s*,\s*(.+)$/);
346:   let remainder = clause.trim();
347: 
348:   if (defaultAndRemainderMatch) {
349:     captures.push({ name: defaultAndRemainderMatch[1], importKind: statementImportKind });
350:     remainder = defaultAndRemainderMatch[2].trim();
351:   }
352: 
353:   const namespaceMatch = remainder.match(/^\*\s+as\s+(\w+)$/);
354:   if (namespaceMatch) {
355:     captures.push({ name: namespaceMatch[1], importKind: statementImportKind });
356:     return captures;
357:   }
358: 
359:   const namedImportsMatch = remainder.match(/^\{([^}]*)\}$/);
360:   if (namedImportsMatch) {
361:     for (const rawSpecifier of namedImportsMatch[1].split(',')) {
362:       const trimmedSpecifier = rawSpecifier.trim();
363:       if (!trimmedSpecifier) {
364:         continue;
365:       }
366: 
367:       const importKind: RawCapture['importKind'] = statementImportKind === 'type'
368:         || /^type\b/.test(trimmedSpecifier)
369:         ? 'type'
370:         : 'value';
371:       const normalizedSpecifier = trimmedSpecifier.replace(/^type\s+/, '');
372:       const importedName = normalizedSpecifier.split(/\s+as\s+/)[0]?.trim();
373:       if (importedName) {
374:         captures.push({ name: importedName, importKind });
375:       }
376:     }
377:     return captures;
378:   }
379: 
380:   const defaultImportMatch = remainder.match(/^(\w+)$/);
381:   if (defaultImportMatch) {
382:     captures.push({ name: defaultImportMatch[1], importKind: statementImportKind });
383:   }
384: 
385:   return captures;
386: }
387: 
388: function countBraces(line: string): number {
389:   let depth = 0;
390:   for (const ch of line) {
391:     if (ch === '{') depth++;
392:     else if (ch === '}') depth--;
393:   }
394:   return depth;
395: }
396: 
397: function extractParameterCaptures(
398:   paramsText: string,
399:   parentName: string,
400:   lineNum: number,
401:   lineLength: number,
402: ): RawCapture[] {
403:   if (!paramsText.trim()) return [];
404: 
405:   const parameterCaptures: RawCapture[] = [];
406:   for (const param of splitTopLevel(paramsText, ',')) {
407:     let segment = param.trim();
408:     if (!segment || segment.startsWith('{') || segment.startsWith('[')) continue;
409: 
410:     segment = segment.replace(/^@\w+\s+/, '').trim();
411:     segment = segment.replace(/^(?:public|private|protected|readonly)\s+/, '').trim();
412:     segment = segment.replace(/^\.\.\./, '').trim();
413: 
414:     const [namePart, typePart] = splitTopLevelOnce(segment, ':');
415:     const [rawName] = splitTopLevelOnce(namePart, '=');
416:     const name = rawName.replace(/\?$/, '').trim();
417:     if (!name) continue;
418: 
419:     const typeText = typePart ? splitTopLevelOnce(typePart, '=')[0] : undefined;
420:     parameterCaptures.push({
421:       name,
422:       kind: 'parameter',
423:       startLine: lineNum,
424:       endLine: lineNum,
425:       startColumn: 0,
426:       endColumn: lineLength,
427:       parentName,
428:       signature: segment,
429:       typeRefs: extractTypeReferences(typeText),
430:     });
431:   }
432: 
433:   return parameterCaptures;
434: }
435: 
436: function getModuleName(filePath: string): string {
437:   const fileName = filePath.split('/').pop() ?? filePath;
438:   return fileName.replace(/\.[^.]+$/, '') || fileName || 'module';
439: }
440: 
441: /** Parse JavaScript/TypeScript source for structural symbols */
442: function parseJsTs(content: string): RawCapture[] {
443:   const captures: RawCapture[] = [];
444:   const lines = content.split('\n');
445:   let currentClass: RawCapture | null = null;
446:   let braceDepth = 0;
447:   const pendingDecorators: string[] = [];
448: 
449:   for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {
450:     const line = lines[i];
451:     const lineNum = i + 1;
452:     const trimmedLine = line.trim();
453: 
454:     if (currentClass && lineNum > currentClass.endLine) {
455:       currentClass = null;
456:     }
457: 
458:     if (trimmedLine.startsWith('@')) {
459:       pendingDecorators.push(...extractDecoratorNames(trimmedLine));
460:       braceDepth += countBraces(line);
461:       continue;
462:     }
463: 
464:     if (trimmedLine.length === 0) {
465:       pendingDecorators.length = 0;
466:       braceDepth += countBraces(line);
467:       continue;
468:     }
469: 
470:     // Functions
471:     const funcMatch = line.match(/(?:export\s+)?(?:async\s+)?function\s+(\w+)\s*\(([^)]*)\)\s*(?::\s*([^{]+))?/);
472:     if (funcMatch) {
473:       const functionCapture: RawCapture = {
474:         name: funcMatch[1], kind: 'function', startLine: lineNum,
475:         endLine: findBraceBlockEndLine(lines, i), startColumn: 0,
476:         endColumn: line.length, signature: line.trim(),
477:         decoratorNames: consumeDecorators(pendingDecorators),
478:         typeRefs: extractTypeReferences(funcMatch[3]),
479:       };
480:       captures.push(functionCapture);
481:       captures.push(
482:         ...extractParameterCaptures(
483:           funcMatch[2] ?? '',
484:           getCaptureFqName(functionCapture),
485:           lineNum,
486:           line.length,
487:         ),
488:       );
489:     }
490: 
491:     // Arrow functions assigned to const/let/var
492:     const arrowMatch = line.match(
493:       /(?:export\s+)?(?:const|let|var)\s+(\w+)\s*=\s*(?:async\s+)?\(([^)]*)\)\s*(?::\s*([^=]+?))?\s*=>/,
494:     );
495:     if (arrowMatch && !funcMatch) {
496:       const functionCapture: RawCapture = {
497:         name: arrowMatch[1], kind: 'function', startLine: lineNum,
498:         endLine: findBraceBlockEndLine(lines, i), startColumn: 0,
499:         endColumn: line.length, signature: line.trim(),
500:         decoratorNames: consumeDecorators(pendingDecorators),
501:         typeRefs: extractTypeReferences(arrowMatch[3]),
502:       };
503:       captures.push(functionCapture);
504:       captures.push(
505:         ...extractParameterCaptures(
506:           arrowMatch[2] ?? '',
507:           getCaptureFqName(functionCapture),
508:           lineNum,
509:           line.length,
510:         ),
511:       );
512:     }
513: 
514:     // Classes (with extends and implements)
515:     const classMatch = line.match(/(?:export\s+)?class\s+(\w+)(?:\s+extends\s+(\w+))?(?:\s+implements\s+([^{]+))?/);
516:     if (classMatch) {
517:       const implNames = classMatch[3]
518:         ? classMatch[3].split(',').map(s => s.trim()).filter(Boolean)
519:         : undefined;
520:       currentClass = {
521:         name: classMatch[1], kind: 'class', startLine: lineNum,
522:         endLine: findBraceBlockEndLine(lines, i),
523:         startColumn: 0, endColumn: line.length, signature: line.trim(),
524:         extendsName: classMatch[2] || undefined,
525:         implementsNames: implNames,
526:         decoratorNames: consumeDecorators(pendingDecorators),
527:       };
528:       captures.push(currentClass);
529:     }
530: 
531:     // Class methods — require at least one modifier keyword OR a method body opener `{`
532:     // to distinguish declarations from bare function calls like `baz()`
533:     const methodMatchWithModifier = currentClass
534:       ? line.match(
535:         /^\s*(?:(?:public|private|protected|static|readonly|abstract|async|override|get|set)\s+)+(?:\*\s*)?(#?\w+)\s*(?:<[^>]*>)?\s*\(([^)]*)\)\s*(?::\s*([^{]+))?\s*\{?/,
536:       )
537:       : null;
538:     const methodMatchShorthand = currentClass && !methodMatchWithModifier
539:       ? line.match(
540:         /^\s*(#?\w+)\s*\(([^)]*)\)\s*(?::\s*([^{]+))?\s*\{/,
541:       )
542:       : null;
543:     const methodMatch = methodMatchWithModifier ?? methodMatchShorthand;
544:     if (methodMatch && currentClass) {
545:       const methodName = methodMatch[1];
546:       if (methodName !== 'constructor') {
547:         const methodCapture: RawCapture = {
548:           name: methodName.replace(/^#/, ''),
549:           kind: 'method',
550:           startLine: lineNum,
551:           endLine: findBraceBlockEndLine(lines, i),
552:           startColumn: 0,
553:           endColumn: line.length,
554:           signature: line.trim(),
555:           parentName: currentClass.name,
556:           decoratorNames: consumeDecorators(pendingDecorators),
557:           typeRefs: extractTypeReferences(methodMatch[3]),
558:         };
559:         captures.push(methodCapture);
560:         captures.push(
561:           ...extractParameterCaptures(
562:             methodMatch[2] ?? '',
563:             getCaptureFqName(methodCapture),
564:             lineNum,
565:             line.length,
566:           ),
567:         );
568:       }
569:     }
570: 
571:     // Interfaces
572:     const ifaceMatch = line.match(/(?:export\s+)?interface\s+(\w+)/);
573:     if (ifaceMatch) {
574:       captures.push({
575:         name: ifaceMatch[1], kind: 'interface', startLine: lineNum,
576:         endLine: findBraceBlockEndLine(lines, i), startColumn: 0,
577:         endColumn: line.length,
578:       });
579:     }
580: 
581:     // Type aliases
582:     const typeMatch = line.match(/(?:export\s+)?type\s+(\w+)\s*=/);
583:     if (typeMatch) {
584:       captures.push({
585:         name: typeMatch[1], kind: 'type_alias', startLine: lineNum,
586:         endLine: findBraceBlockEndLine(lines, i), startColumn: 0,
587:         endColumn: line.length,
588:       });
589:     }
590: 
591:     // Enums
592:     const enumMatch = line.match(/(?:export\s+)?enum\s+(\w+)/);
593:     if (enumMatch) {
594:       captures.push({
595:         name: enumMatch[1], kind: 'enum', startLine: lineNum,
596:         endLine: findBraceBlockEndLine(lines, i), startColumn: 0,
597:         endColumn: line.length,
598:       });
599:     }
600: 
601:     // Imports
602:     const importMatch = braceDepth === 0
603:       ? line.match(/^import\s+(type\s+)?(.+?)\s+from\s+['"](.+?)['"]/)
604:       : null;
605:     if (importMatch) {
606:       const statementImportKind: RawCapture['importKind'] = importMatch[1] ? 'type' : 'value';
607:       const names = parseImportCaptureNames(importMatch[2], statementImportKind);
608:       const moduleSpecifier = importMatch[3];
609:       for (const entry of names) {
610:         if (entry.name) {
611:           captures.push({
612:             name: entry.name, kind: 'import', startLine: lineNum,
613:             endLine: findBraceBlockEndLine(lines, i), startColumn: 0,
614:             endColumn: line.length,
615:             moduleSpecifier,
616:             importKind: entry.importKind,
617:           });
618:         }
619:       }
620:     }
621: 
622:     // Exports
623:     const exportStarMatch = braceDepth === 0 ? line.match(/^export\s+\*\s+from\s+['"](.+?)['"]/) : null;
624:     if (exportStarMatch) {
625:       captures.push({
626:         name: '*', kind: 'export', startLine: lineNum,
627:         endLine: findBraceBlockEndLine(lines, i), startColumn: 0,
628:         endColumn: line.length,
629:         moduleSpecifier: exportStarMatch[1],
630:         exportKind: 'star',
631:       });
632:     }
633: 
634:     const exportMatch = braceDepth === 0 ? line.match(/^export\s+\{([^}]+)\}(?:\s+from\s+['"](.+?)['"])?/) : null;
635:     if (exportMatch) {
636:       const moduleSpecifier = exportMatch[2];
637:       const exportKind: RawCapture['exportKind'] = moduleSpecifier ? 'named' : 'declaration';
638:       const names = exportMatch[1].split(',').map(s => s.trim().split(/\s+as\s+/)[0].trim());
639:       for (const name of names) {
640:         if (name) {
641:           captures.push({
642:             name, kind: 'export', startLine: lineNum,
643:             endLine: findBraceBlockEndLine(lines, i), startColumn: 0,
644:             endColumn: line.length,
645:             ...(moduleSpecifier ? { moduleSpecifier } : {}),
646:             exportKind,
647:           });
648:         }
649:       }
650:     }
651: 
652:     // Variables
653:     const variableMatch = braceDepth === 0
654:       ? line.match(/(?:export\s+)?(?:const|let|var)\s+(\w+)(?:\s*:\s*([^=;]+))?/)
655:       : null;
656:     if (variableMatch) {
657:       captures.push({
658:         name: variableMatch[1],
659:         kind: 'variable',
660:         startLine: lineNum,
661:         endLine: lineNum,
662:         startColumn: 0,
663:         endColumn: line.length,
664:         signature: line.trim(),
665:         typeRefs: extractTypeReferences(variableMatch[2]),
666:       });
667:     }
668: 
669:     if (pendingDecorators.length > 0 && !funcMatch && !arrowMatch && !classMatch && !methodMatch) {
670:       pendingDecorators.length = 0;
671:     }
672: 
673:     braceDepth += countBraces(line);
674:   }
675:   return captures;
676: }
677: 
678: /** Parse Python source for structural symbols */
679: function parsePython(content: string): RawCapture[] {
680:   const captures: RawCapture[] = [];
681:   const lines = content.split('\n');
682:   let currentClass: RawCapture | null = null;
683:   let classIndent = 0; // indentation column of the class keyword
684:   let classMethodIndent = -1; // indent of the first def in the class body (auto-detected)
685:   const pendingDecorators: string[] = [];
686: 
687:   for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {
688:     const line = lines[i];
689:     const lineNum = i + 1;
690:     const trimmedLine = line.trim();
691: 
692:     if (currentClass && lineNum > currentClass.endLine) {
693:       currentClass = null;
694:       classIndent = 0;
695:       classMethodIndent = -1;
696:     }
697: 
698:     if (trimmedLine.startsWith('@')) {
699:       pendingDecorators.push(...extractDecoratorNames(trimmedLine));
700:       continue;
701:     }
702: 
703:     const classMatch = line.match(/^(\s*)class\s+(\w+)(?:\(([^)]*)\))?/);
704:     if (classMatch) {
705:       classIndent = classMatch[1].length;
706:       const bases = classMatch[3]
707:         ? classMatch[3].split(',').map(base => base.trim()).filter(Boolean)
708:         : [];
709:       currentClass = {
710:         name: classMatch[2], kind: 'class', startLine: lineNum,
711:         endLine: findPythonBlockEndLine(lines, i), startColumn: classIndent,
712:         endColumn: line.length,
713:         extendsName: bases[0],
714:         signature: line.trim(),
715:         decoratorNames: consumeDecorators(pendingDecorators),
716:       };
717:       captures.push(currentClass);
718:       continue;
719:     }
720: 
721:     const funcMatch = line.match(/^(\s*)def\s+(\w+)\s*\(([^)]*)\)\s*(?:->\s*([^:]+))?/);
722:     if (funcMatch) {
723:       const indent = funcMatch[1].length;
724:       const name = funcMatch[2];
725:       // Auto-detect class method indent from the first def inside the class body
726:       const isClassMemberLevel = currentClass && indent > classIndent && (
727:         classMethodIndent === -1
728:           ? (classMethodIndent = indent, true)
729:           : indent === classMethodIndent
730:       );
731:       if (isClassMemberLevel && currentClass) {
732:         const methodCapture: RawCapture = {
733:           name, kind: 'method', startLine: lineNum,
734:           endLine: findPythonBlockEndLine(lines, i), startColumn: indent,
735:           endColumn: line.length, parentName: currentClass.name,
736:           signature: line.trim(),
737:           decoratorNames: consumeDecorators(pendingDecorators),
738:           typeRefs: extractTypeReferences(funcMatch[4]),
739:         };
740:         captures.push(methodCapture);
741:         captures.push(
742:           ...extractParameterCaptures(
743:             funcMatch[3] ?? '',
744:             getCaptureFqName(methodCapture),
745:             lineNum,
746:             line.length,
747:           ),
748:         );
749:       } else {
750:         currentClass = null;
751:         const functionCapture: RawCapture = {
752:           name, kind: 'function', startLine: lineNum,
753:           endLine: findPythonBlockEndLine(lines, i), startColumn: 0,
754:           endColumn: line.length,
755:           signature: line.trim(),
756:           decoratorNames: consumeDecorators(pendingDecorators),
757:           typeRefs: extractTypeReferences(funcMatch[4]),
758:         };
759:         captures.push(functionCapture);
760:         captures.push(
761:           ...extractParameterCaptures(
762:             funcMatch[3] ?? '',
763:             getCaptureFqName(functionCapture),
764:             lineNum,
765:             line.length,
766:           ),
767:         );
768:       }
769:     }
770: 
771:     const importMatch = line.match(/^(?:from\s+(\S+)\s+)?import\s+(.+)/);
772:     if (importMatch) {
773:       const names = importMatch[2].split(',').map(s => s.trim().split(/\s+as\s+/)[0].trim());
774:       for (const name of names) {
775:         if (name && name !== '*') {
776:           captures.push({ name, kind: 'import', startLine: lineNum, endLine: lineNum, startColumn: 0, endColumn: line.length });
777:         }
778:       }
779:     }
780: 
781:     if (pendingDecorators.length > 0 && trimmedLine.length > 0 && !funcMatch) {
782:       pendingDecorators.length = 0;
783:     }
784: 
785:     if (trimmedLine.length > 0 && !line.startsWith(' ') && !line.startsWith('\t') && !classMatch) {
786:       currentClass = null;
787:     }
788:   }
789:   return captures;
790: }
791: 
792: /** Parse Bash source for structural symbols */
793: function parseBash(content: string): RawCapture[] {
794:   const captures: RawCapture[] = [];
795:   const lines = content.split('\n');
796: 
797:   for (let i = 0; i < lines.length; i++) {
798:     const line = lines[i];
799:     const lineNum = i + 1;
800:     // Match 'foo() {' or 'function foo() {' form
801:     const funcMatch = line.match(/^(?:function\s+)?(\w+)\s*\(\s*\)/);
802:     if (funcMatch) {
803:       captures.push({
804:         name: funcMatch[1], kind: 'function', startLine: lineNum,
805:         endLine: findBraceBlockEndLine(lines, i), startColumn: 0,
806:         endColumn: line.length,
807:       });
808:       continue;
809:     }
810:     // Match 'function foo { }' and 'function foo() { }' (function keyword form without prior match)
811:     const funcKeywordMatch = line.match(/^function\s+(\w+)\s*(?:\(\s*\))?\s*\{?/);
812:     if (funcKeywordMatch) {
813:       captures.push({
814:         name: funcKeywordMatch[1], kind: 'function', startLine: lineNum,
815:         endLine: findBraceBlockEndLine(lines, i), startColumn: 0,
816:         endColumn: line.length,
817:       });
818:     }
819:   }
820:   return captures;
821: }
822: 
823: class RegexParser implements ParserAdapter {
824:   parse(
825:     content: string,
826:     language: SupportedLanguage,
827:     edgeWeights?: Partial<Record<EdgeType, number>>,
828:     _filePath?: string,
829:   ): ParseResult {
830:     const startTime = Date.now();
831:     const contentHash = generateContentHash(content);
832: 
833:     try {
834:       let captures: RawCapture[];
835:       switch (language) {
836:         case 'javascript':
837:         case 'typescript':
838:           captures = parseJsTs(content);
839:           break;
840:         case 'python':
841:           captures = parsePython(content);
842:           break;
843:         case 'bash':
844:           captures = parseBash(content);
845:           break;
846:         default:
847:           captures = [];
848:       }
849: 
850:       const nodes = capturesToNodes(captures, '', language, content);
851:       const contentLines = content.split('\n');
852:       const detectorProvenance = detectorProvenanceFromParserBackend('regex');
853:       const edges = extractEdges(nodes, contentLines, captures, detectorProvenance, edgeWeights);
854: 
855:       return rememberParseResultCaptures({
856:         filePath: '',
857:         language,
858:         nodes,
859:         edges,
860:         detectorProvenance,
861:         contentHash,
862:         parseHealth: captures.length > 0 ? 'clean' : 'recovered',
863:         parseErrors: [],
864:         parseDurationMs: Date.now() - startTime,
865:       }, captures);
866:     } catch (err: unknown) {
867:       return {
868:         filePath: '',
869:         language,
870:         nodes: [],
871:         edges: [],
872:         detectorProvenance: detectorProvenanceFromParserBackend('regex'),
873:         contentHash,
874:         parseHealth: 'error',
875:         parseErrors: [err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)],
876:         parseDurationMs: Date.now() - startTime,
877:       };
878:     }
879:   }
880: }
881: 
882: /** Cached tree-sitter parser instance (lazy-loaded to avoid circular import at module init) */
883: let treeSitterParser: ParserAdapter | null = null;
884: 
885: /**
886:  * Returns the active parser backend.
887:  *
888:  * SPECKIT_PARSER env var controls which backend is used:
889:  *   - 'treesitter' (default): AST-accurate parsing via web-tree-sitter WASM grammars
890:  *   - 'regex': Lightweight regex-based fallback with no WASM dependencies
891:  *
892:  * Tree-sitter is lazy-loaded on first call. If WASM init fails, falls back to regex automatically.
893:  */
894: export async function getParser(): Promise<ParserAdapter> {
895:   const parserEnv = process.env.SPECKIT_PARSER ?? 'treesitter';
896: 
897:   if (parserEnv === 'treesitter') {
898:     try {
899:       // Dynamic import avoids circular dependency (tree-sitter-parser imports extractEdges from here)
900:       const { TreeSitterParser } = await import('./tree-sitter-parser.js');
901:       if (treeSitterParser && TreeSitterParser.isReady()) {
902:         return treeSitterParser;
903:       }
904: 
905:       await TreeSitterParser.init();
906:       await TreeSitterParser.loadAllLanguages();
907:       treeSitterParser ??= new TreeSitterParser();
908:       return treeSitterParser;
909:     } catch (err: unknown) {
910:       // Reset so next call retries instead of returning a broken cached instance
911:       treeSitterParser = null;
912:       console.warn(`[structural-indexer] Tree-sitter init failed, falling back to regex: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`);
913:       return new RegexParser();
914:     }
915:   }
916: 
917:   return new RegexParser();
918: }
919: 
920: export function getRequestedParserBackend(): ParserBackend {
921:   return (process.env.SPECKIT_PARSER ?? 'treesitter') === 'regex' ? 'regex' : 'treesitter';
922: }
923: 
924: function attachFilePath(result: ParseResult, filePath: string): ParseResult {
925:   const symbolIdMap = new Map<string, string>();
926:   const nodes = result.nodes.map(node => {
927:     const fqName = node.kind === 'module' ? getModuleName(filePath) : node.fqName;
928:     const name = node.kind === 'module' ? getModuleName(filePath) : node.name;
929:     const symbolId = generateSymbolId(filePath, fqName, node.kind);
930:     symbolIdMap.set(node.symbolId, symbolId);
931:     return { ...node, filePath, symbolId, fqName, name };
932:   });
933: 
934:   const edges = result.edges.map(edge => ({
935:     ...edge,
936:     sourceId: symbolIdMap.get(edge.sourceId) ?? edge.sourceId,
937:     targetId: symbolIdMap.get(edge.targetId) ?? edge.targetId,
938:   }));
939: 
940:   const attachedResult = { ...result, filePath, nodes, edges };
941:   const captures = getParseResultCaptures(result);
942:   if (captures) {
943:     parseResultCaptures.set(attachedResult, captures);
944:   }
945:   return attachedResult;
946: }
947: 
948: /** Convert raw captures to CodeNode[] */
949: export function capturesToNodes(
950:   captures: RawCapture[],
951:   filePath: string,
952:   language: SupportedLanguage,
953:   content: string,
954: ): CodeNode[] {
955:   const lines = content.split('\n');
956:   const moduleNode: CodeNode = {
957:     symbolId: generateSymbolId(filePath, getModuleName(filePath), 'module'),
958:     filePath,
959:     fqName: getModuleName(filePath),
960:     kind: 'module',
961:     name: getModuleName(filePath),
962:     startLine: 1,
963:     endLine: Math.max(lines.length, 1),
964:     startColumn: 0,
965:     endColumn: lines[lines.length - 1]?.length ?? 0,
966:     language,
967:     contentHash: generateContentHash(content),
968:   };
969: 
970:   const seenSymbolIds = new Set<string>([moduleNode.symbolId]);
971:   const symbolNodes = captures.flatMap(c => {
972:     const fqName = getCaptureFqName(c);
973:     const symbolId = generateSymbolId(filePath, fqName, c.kind);
974:     if (seenSymbolIds.has(symbolId)) return [];
975:     seenSymbolIds.add(symbolId);
976:     const rangeText = lines.slice(c.startLine - 1, c.endLine).join('\n');
977:     return [{
978:       symbolId,
979:       filePath,
980:       fqName,
981:       kind: c.kind,
982:       name: c.name,
983:       startLine: c.startLine,
984:       endLine: c.endLine,
985:       startColumn: c.startColumn,
986:       endColumn: c.endColumn,
987:       language,
988:       signature: c.signature,
989:       contentHash: generateContentHash(rangeText),
990:     }];
991:   });
992: 
993:   if (content.trim().length === 0) {
994:     return symbolNodes;
995:   }
996: 
997:   return [moduleNode, ...symbolNodes];
998: }
999: 
1000: /** Extract edges from nodes, source content, and raw captures */
1001: export function extractEdges(
1002:   nodes: CodeNode[],
1003:   contentLines?: string[],
1004:   captures?: RawCapture[],
1005:   baseDetectorProvenance: DetectorProvenance = 'structured',
1006:   edgeWeights: Partial<Record<EdgeType, number>> = {},
1007: ): CodeEdge[] {
1008:   const edges: CodeEdge[] = [];
1009:   const resolvedWeights: Record<EdgeType, number> = {
1010:     ...DEFAULT_EDGE_WEIGHTS,
1011:     ...edgeWeights,
1012:   };
1013:   const nodesByName = new Map<string, CodeNode[]>();
1014:   const nodesByFqName = new Map<string, CodeNode>();
1015:   for (const node of nodes) {
1016:     const group = nodesByName.get(node.name) ?? [];
1017:     group.push(node);
1018:     nodesByName.set(node.name, group);
1019:     nodesByFqName.set(node.fqName, node);
1020:   }
1021: 
1022:   // Build capture lookup by name for extends/implements data
1023:   const captureByFqName = new Map<string, RawCapture>();
1024:   if (captures) {
1025:     for (const capture of captures) {
1026:       captureByFqName.set(getCaptureFqName(capture), capture);
1027:     }
1028:   }
1029: 
1030:   const preferredKinds = (
1031:     name: string,
1032:     kinds: SymbolKind[],
1033:     excludeId?: string,
1034:   ): CodeNode | undefined => {
1035:     const candidates = nodesByName.get(name) ?? [];
1036:     for (const kind of kinds) {
1037:       const match = candidates.find(candidate => candidate.kind === kind && candidate.symbolId !== excludeId);
1038:       if (match) return match;
1039:     }
1040:     return candidates.find(candidate => candidate.symbolId !== excludeId);
1041:   };
1042: 
1043:   // CONTAINS: class -> method (confidence 1.0)
1044:   const classes = nodes.filter(n => n.kind === 'class');
1045:   const methods = nodes.filter(n => n.kind === 'method');
1046:   for (const method of methods) {
1047:     const parent = classes.find(c => method.fqName.startsWith(c.name + '.'));
1048:     if (parent) {
1049:       edges.push({
1050:         sourceId: parent.symbolId, targetId: method.symbolId,
1051:         edgeType: 'CONTAINS', weight: resolvedWeights.CONTAINS,
1052:         metadata: buildEdgeMetadata(resolvedWeights.CONTAINS, baseDetectorProvenance, 'EXTRACTED'),
1053:       });
1054:     }
1055:   }
1056: 
1057:   // IMPORTS (confidence 1.0)
1058:   const imports = nodes.filter(n => n.kind === 'import');
1059:   for (const imp of imports) {
1060:     const capture = captureByFqName.get(imp.fqName);
1061:     if (capture?.moduleSpecifier) {
1062:       continue;
1063:     }
1064:     const target = preferredKinds(imp.name, ['function', 'class', 'interface', 'type_alias', 'enum', 'variable', 'module'], imp.symbolId);
1065:     if (target && target.symbolId !== imp.symbolId) {
1066:       edges.push({
1067:         sourceId: imp.symbolId, targetId: target.symbolId,
1068:         edgeType: 'IMPORTS', weight: resolvedWeights.IMPORTS,
1069:         metadata: buildEdgeMetadata(resolvedWeights.IMPORTS, baseDetectorProvenance, 'EXTRACTED'),
1070:       });
1071:     }
1072:   }
1073: 
1074:   // EXPORTS (confidence 1.0)
1075:   const exports = nodes.filter(n => n.kind === 'export');
1076:   for (const exp of exports) {
1077:     const target = preferredKinds(exp.name, ['function', 'class', 'interface', 'type_alias', 'enum', 'variable', 'module'], exp.symbolId);
1078:     if (target && target.symbolId !== exp.symbolId) {
1079:       edges.push({
1080:         sourceId: target.symbolId, targetId: exp.symbolId,
1081:         edgeType: 'EXPORTS', weight: resolvedWeights.EXPORTS,
1082:         metadata: buildEdgeMetadata(resolvedWeights.EXPORTS, baseDetectorProvenance, 'EXTRACTED'),
1083:       });
1084:     }
1085:   }
1086: 
1087:   // EXTENDS: class -> parent class (confidence 0.95)
1088:   for (const cls of classes) {
1089:     const cap = captureByFqName.get(cls.fqName);
1090:     if (cap?.extendsName) {
1091:       const parent = preferredKinds(cap.extendsName, ['class'], cls.symbolId);
1092:       if (parent) {
1093:         edges.push({
1094:           sourceId: cls.symbolId, targetId: parent.symbolId,
1095:           edgeType: 'EXTENDS', weight: resolvedWeights.EXTENDS,
1096:           metadata: buildEdgeMetadata(resolvedWeights.EXTENDS, baseDetectorProvenance, 'EXTRACTED'),
1097:         });
1098:       }
1099:     }
1100:   }
1101: 
1102:   // IMPLEMENTS: class -> interface (confidence 0.95)
1103:   for (const cls of classes) {
1104:     const cap = captureByFqName.get(cls.fqName);
1105:     if (cap?.implementsNames) {
1106:       for (const ifaceName of cap.implementsNames) {
1107:         const iface = preferredKinds(ifaceName, ['interface', 'type_alias', 'import'], cls.symbolId);
1108:         if (iface) {
1109:           edges.push({
1110:             sourceId: cls.symbolId, targetId: iface.symbolId,
1111:             edgeType: 'IMPLEMENTS', weight: resolvedWeights.IMPLEMENTS,
1112:             metadata: buildEdgeMetadata(resolvedWeights.IMPLEMENTS, baseDetectorProvenance, 'EXTRACTED'),
1113:           });
1114:         }
1115:       }
1116:     }
1117:   }
1118: 
1119:   // CALLS: function/method -> called function (confidence 0.8)
1120:   if (contentLines) {
1121:     const callableNodes = nodes.filter(
1122:       n => n.kind === 'function' || n.kind === 'method',
1123:     );
1124:     const callPattern = /\b(\w+)\s*\(/g;
1125: 
1126:     for (const caller of callableNodes) {
1127:       const bodyLines = contentLines.slice(caller.startLine - 1, caller.endLine);
1128:       const bodyText = bodyLines.join('\n');
1129:       const seen = new Set<string>();
1130:       let match: RegExpExecArray | null;
1131: 
1132:       while ((match = callPattern.exec(bodyText)) !== null) {
1133:         const calledName = match[1];
1134:         // Skip self-references, keywords, and duplicates
1135:         if (calledName === caller.name || seen.has(calledName)) continue;
1136:         // Skip common JS/TS keywords that precede parens
1137:         if (['if', 'for', 'while', 'switch', 'catch', 'return', 'throw', 'new', 'typeof', 'await', 'async', 'function', 'class', 'const', 'let', 'var'].includes(calledName)) continue;
1138:         seen.add(calledName);
1139: 
1140:         const target = preferredKinds(calledName, ['function', 'method', 'class', 'variable'], caller.symbolId);
1141:         if (target && target.symbolId !== caller.symbolId) {
1142:           edges.push({
1143:             sourceId: caller.symbolId, targetId: target.symbolId,
1144:             edgeType: 'CALLS', weight: resolvedWeights.CALLS,
1145:             metadata: buildEdgeMetadata(resolvedWeights.CALLS, 'heuristic', 'INFERRED'),
1146:           });
1147:         }
1148:       }
1149:     }
1150:   }
1151: 
1152:   // DECORATES: decorator symbol -> decorated class/function/method (confidence 0.9)
1153:   if (captures) {
1154:     for (const capture of captures) {
1155:       if (!capture.decoratorNames?.length) continue;
1156:       const decoratedNode = nodesByFqName.get(getCaptureFqName(capture));
1157:       if (!decoratedNode) continue;
1158: 
1159:       for (const decoratorName of capture.decoratorNames) {
1160:         const decoratorNode = preferredKinds(decoratorName, ['function', 'class', 'import', 'variable', 'method'], decoratedNode.symbolId);
1161:         if (!decoratorNode) continue;
1162: 
1163:         edges.push({
1164:           sourceId: decoratorNode.symbolId,
1165:           targetId: decoratedNode.symbolId,
1166:           edgeType: 'DECORATES',
1167:           weight: resolvedWeights.DECORATES,
1168:           metadata: buildEdgeMetadata(resolvedWeights.DECORATES, baseDetectorProvenance, 'EXTRACTED'),
1169:         });
1170:       }
1171:     }
1172:   }
1173: 
1174:   // OVERRIDES: method -> parent class method (confidence 0.9)
1175:   for (const method of methods) {
1176:     const ownerCapture = captureByFqName.get(method.fqName);
1177:     if (!ownerCapture?.parentName) continue;
1178: 
1179:     let parentClassCapture = captureByFqName.get(ownerCapture.parentName);
1180:     let parentClassName = parentClassCapture?.extendsName;
1181: 
1182:     while (parentClassName) {
1183:       const parentMethod = nodesByFqName.get(`${parentClassName}.${method.name}`);
1184:       if (parentMethod?.kind === 'method') {
1185:         edges.push({
1186:           sourceId: method.symbolId,
1187:           targetId: parentMethod.symbolId,
1188:           edgeType: 'OVERRIDES',
1189:           weight: resolvedWeights.OVERRIDES,
1190:           metadata: buildEdgeMetadata(resolvedWeights.OVERRIDES, baseDetectorProvenance, 'INFERRED'),
1191:         });
1192:         break;
1193:       }
1194: 
1195:       parentClassCapture = captureByFqName.get(parentClassName);
1196:       parentClassName = parentClassCapture?.extendsName;
1197:     }
1198:   }
1199: 
1200:   // TYPE_OF: symbol -> referenced type symbol (confidence 0.85)
1201:   if (captures) {
1202:     for (const capture of captures) {
1203:       if (!capture.typeRefs?.length) continue;
1204: 
1205:       const sourceNode = nodesByFqName.get(getCaptureFqName(capture));
1206:       if (!sourceNode) continue;
1207: 
1208:       for (const typeName of capture.typeRefs) {
1209:         const typeNode = preferredKinds(typeName, ['class', 'interface', 'type_alias', 'enum', 'import'], sourceNode.symbolId);
1210:         if (!typeNode) continue;
1211: 
1212:         edges.push({
1213:           sourceId: sourceNode.symbolId,
1214:           targetId: typeNode.symbolId,
1215:           edgeType: 'TYPE_OF',
1216:           weight: resolvedWeights.TYPE_OF,
1217:           metadata: buildEdgeMetadata(resolvedWeights.TYPE_OF, baseDetectorProvenance, 'EXTRACTED'),
1218:         });
1219:       }
1220:     }
1221:   }
1222: 
1223:   return edges;
1224: }
1225: 
1226: /** Parse a single file and return structural analysis */
1227: export async function parseFile(
1228:   filePath: string,
1229:   content: string,
1230:   language: SupportedLanguage,
1231:   edgeWeights?: Partial<Record<EdgeType, number>>,
1232: ): Promise<ParseResult> {
1233:   const startTime = Date.now();
1234:   const contentHash = generateContentHash(content);
1235: 
1236:   if (language === 'doc') {
1237:     return {
1238:       filePath,
1239:       language,
1240:       nodes: [],
1241:       edges: [],
1242:       detectorProvenance: detectorProvenanceFromParserBackend(getRequestedParserBackend()),
1243:       contentHash,
1244:       parseHealth: 'clean',
1245:       parseErrors: [],
1246:       parseDurationMs: 0,
1247:     };
1248:   }
1249: 
1250:   try {
1251:     const parser = await getParser();
1252:     const parserResult = parser.parse(content, language, edgeWeights, filePath);
1253:     return attachFilePath(parserResult, filePath);
1254:   } catch (err: unknown) {
1255:     return {
1256:       filePath, language,
1257:       nodes: [], edges: [], contentHash,
1258:       detectorProvenance: detectorProvenanceFromParserBackend(getRequestedParserBackend()),
1259:       parseHealth: 'error',
1260:       parseErrors: [err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)],
1261:       parseDurationMs: Date.now() - startTime,
1262:     };
1263:   }
1264: }
1265: 
1266: function normalizeGlobPath(pathValue: string): string {
1267:   return pathValue.replace(/\\/g, '/').replace(/^\.\//, '');
1268: }
1269: 
1270: function globToRegExp(glob: string): RegExp {
1271:   const normalized = normalizeGlobPath(glob);
1272:   let pattern = '^';
1273: 
1274:   for (let i = 0; i < normalized.length; i++) {
1275:     const char = normalized[i];
1276:     const next = normalized[i + 1];
1277:     const nextThree = normalized.slice(i, i + 3);
1278: 
1279:     if (nextThree === '**/') {
1280:       pattern += '(?:.*/)?';
1281:       i += 2;
1282:       continue;
1283:     }
1284: 
1285:     if (char === '*' && next === '*') {
1286:       pattern += '.*';
1287:       i += 1;
1288:       continue;
1289:     }
1290: 
1291:     if (char === '*') {
1292:       pattern += '[^/]*';
1293:       continue;
1294:     }
1295: 
1296:     if (char === '?') {
1297:       pattern += '[^/]';
1298:       continue;
1299:     }
1300: 
1301:     if ('\\.^$+{}()|[]'.includes(char)) {
1302:       pattern += `\\${char}`;
1303:       continue;
1304:     }
1305: 
1306:     pattern += char;
1307:   }
1308: 
1309:   pattern += '$';
1310:   return new RegExp(pattern);
1311: }
1312: 
1313: function shouldExcludePath(
1314:   rootDir: string,
1315:   fullPath: string,
1316:   excludePatterns: RegExp[],
1317:   isDirectory: boolean,
1318:   config: IndexerConfig,
1319: ): boolean {
1320:   if (!shouldIndexForCodeGraph(fullPath, config.scopePolicy)) {
1321:     return true;
1322:   }
1323:   const relativePath = normalizeGlobPath(relative(rootDir, fullPath));
1324:   const candidatePath = isDirectory ? `${relativePath}/` : relativePath;
1325:   return excludePatterns.some(pattern => pattern.test(candidatePath));
1326: }
1327: 
1328: function loadIgnoreFactory(): IgnoreFactory {
1329:   if (ignoreFactory) {
1330:     return ignoreFactory;
1331:   }
1332: 
1333:   try {
1334:     ignoreFactory = resolveIgnoreFactory(require('ignore'));
1335:   } catch {
1336:     const eslintPackagePath = require.resolve('eslint/package.json');
1337:     ignoreFactory = resolveIgnoreFactory(require(join(dirname(eslintPackagePath), 'node_modules/ignore/index.js')));
1338:   }
1339: 
1340:   if (!ignoreFactory) {
1341:     throw new Error('ignore package did not export a factory function');
1342:   }
1343:   return ignoreFactory;
1344: }
1345: 
1346: function loadGitignore(
1347:   directoryPath: string,
1348:   gitignoreCache: Map<string, IgnoreInstance | null>,
1349:   warnings: string[] = [],
1350:   capExceeded?: FileFindResult['capExceeded'],
1351: ): IgnoreInstance | null {
1352:   if (gitignoreCache.has(directoryPath)) {
1353:     return gitignoreCache.get(directoryPath) ?? null;
1354:   }
1355: 
1356:   try {
1357:     const gitignorePath = resolve(directoryPath, '.gitignore');
1358:     const stats = statSync(gitignorePath);
1359:     if (stats.size > MAX_GITIGNORE_BYTES) {
1360:       const warning = `[structural-indexer] Skipping oversized .gitignore (${stats.size} bytes > ${MAX_GITIGNORE_BYTES} bytes): ${gitignorePath}`;
1361:       console.warn(warning);
1362:       warnings.push(warning);
1363:       if (capExceeded) {
1364:         capExceeded.gitignoreSize = true;
1365:       }
1366:       gitignoreCache.set(directoryPath, null);
1367:       return null;
1368:     }
1369:     const content = readFileSync(gitignorePath, 'utf-8');
1370:     const factory = loadIgnoreFactory();
1371:     const matcher = factory();
1372:     matcher.add(content.split(/\r?\n/));
1373:     gitignoreCache.set(directoryPath, matcher);
1374:     return matcher;
1375:   } catch {
1376:     gitignoreCache.set(directoryPath, null);
1377:     return null;
1378:   }
1379: }
1380: 
1381: function isGitignored(
1382:   fullPath: string,
1383:   isDirectory: boolean,
1384:   contexts: GitignoreContext[],
1385: ): boolean {
1386:   return contexts.some(({ baseDir, matcher }) => {
1387:     const relativePath = normalizeGlobPath(relative(baseDir, fullPath));
1388:     if (relativePath.startsWith('../') || relativePath === '..' || relativePath === '') {
1389:       return false;
1390:     }
1391:     const candidatePath = isDirectory ? `${relativePath}/` : relativePath;
1392:     return matcher.ignores(candidatePath);
1393:   });
1394: }
1395: 
1396: /** Recursive file finder matching extension from glob pattern */
1397: function findFiles(config: IndexerConfig, pattern: string): FileFindResult {
1398:   const { rootDir, excludeGlobs, maxFileSizeBytes } = config;
1399:   const extMatch = pattern.match(/\*\.(\w+)$/);
1400:   const targetExt = extMatch ? '.' + extMatch[1] : null;
1401:   const results: string[] = [];
1402:   const excludePatterns = excludeGlobs.map(globToRegExp);
1403:   const gitignoreCache = new Map<string, IgnoreInstance | null>();
1404:   let excludedByDefault = 0;
1405:   let excludedByGitignore = 0;
1406:   let visitedNodes = 0;
1407:   let abortedByNodeLimit = false;
1408:   const warnings: string[] = [];
1409:   const capExceeded = { maxNodes: false, depth: false, gitignoreSize: false };
1410: 
1411:   function walk(currentDir: string, inheritedGitignores: GitignoreContext[], depth: number): void {
1412:     if (abortedByNodeLimit) {
1413:       return;
1414:     }
1415:     if (depth >= FIND_FILES_MAX_DEPTH) {
1416:       const warning = `[structural-indexer] Aborting descent at maxDepth=${FIND_FILES_MAX_DEPTH}: ${currentDir}`;
1417:       console.warn(warning);
1418:       warnings.push(warning);
1419:       capExceeded.depth = true;
1420:       return;
1421:     }
1422: 
1423:     const localGitignore = loadGitignore(currentDir, gitignoreCache, warnings, capExceeded);
1424:     const activeGitignores = localGitignore
1425:       ? [...inheritedGitignores, { baseDir: currentDir, matcher: localGitignore }]
1426:       : inheritedGitignores;
1427:     let entries;
1428:     try {
1429:       entries = readdirSync(currentDir, { withFileTypes: true });
1430:     } catch { return; }
1431: 
1432:     for (const entry of entries) {
1433:       visitedNodes += 1;
1434:       if (visitedNodes > FIND_FILES_MAX_NODES) {
1435:         const warning = `[structural-indexer] Aborting walk after ${FIND_FILES_MAX_NODES} filesystem nodes at ${currentDir}`;
1436:         console.warn(warning);
1437:         warnings.push(warning);
1438:         capExceeded.maxNodes = true;
1439:         abortedByNodeLimit = true;
1440:         return;
1441:       }
1442:       const fullPath = resolve(currentDir, entry.name);
1443:       if (shouldExcludePath(rootDir, fullPath, excludePatterns, entry.isDirectory(), config)) {
1444:         excludedByDefault++;
1445:         continue;
1446:       }
1447:       if (isGitignored(fullPath, entry.isDirectory(), activeGitignores)) {
1448:         excludedByGitignore++;
1449:         continue;
1450:       }
1451: 
1452:       if (entry.isDirectory()) {
1453:         walk(fullPath, activeGitignores, depth + 1);
1454:       } else if (entry.isFile()) {
1455:         if (targetExt && !entry.name.endsWith(targetExt)) continue;
1456:         try {
1457:           const stat = statSync(fullPath);
1458:           if (stat.size <= maxFileSizeBytes) {
1459:             results.push(normalizeFilesystemPath(fullPath));
1460:           } else {
1461:             console.warn(`[structural-indexer] Skipping large file (${(stat.size / 1024).toFixed(1)}KB > ${(maxFileSizeBytes / 1024).toFixed(1)}KB): ${fullPath}`);
1462:           }
1463:         } catch { /* skip */ }
1464:       }
1465:     }
1466:   }
1467: 
1468:   walk(rootDir, [], 0);
1469:   return { files: results, excludedByDefault, excludedByGitignore, warnings, capExceeded };
1470: }
1471: 
1472: function collectSpecificFiles(config: IndexerConfig, specificFiles: string[]): string[] {
1473:   const { rootDir, maxFileSizeBytes } = config;
1474:   const includePatterns = config.includeGlobs.map(globToRegExp);
1475:   const dedupedFiles: string[] = [];
1476:   const seenFiles = new Set<string>();
1477:   const canonicalWorkspaceRoot = resolveCanonicalPath(resolve(rootDir));
1478: 
1479:   for (const filePath of specificFiles) {
1480:     const absolutePath = normalizeFilesystemPath(resolve(filePath));
1481:     const workspaceCandidate = resolveWorkspaceCandidateWithinRoot(absolutePath, canonicalWorkspaceRoot);
1482:     if (!workspaceCandidate || seenFiles.has(workspaceCandidate.canonicalPath)) {
1483:       continue;
1484:     }
1485: 
1486:     const relativePath = normalizeGlobPath(relative(rootDir, workspaceCandidate.originalPath));
1487:     if (relativePath === '..' || relativePath.startsWith('../')) {
1488:       continue;
1489:     }
1490:     // Apply the same file-type allowlist the full walk enforces: an incremental
1491:     // or stale-file reindex must still match an include glob, so excluded types
1492:     // such as markdown are not re-added through this path when scope would allow
1493:     // the folder.
1494:     if (!includePatterns.some((pattern) => pattern.test(relativePath))) {
1495:       continue;
1496:     }
1497:     if (!shouldIndexForCodeGraph(workspaceCandidate.canonicalPath, config.scopePolicy)) {

1499:     }
1500: 
1501:     try {
1502:       const stat = statSync(workspaceCandidate.originalPath);
1503:       if (!stat.isFile()) {

1505:       }

1507:         console.warn(`[structural-indexer] Skipping large file (${(stat.size / 1024).toFixed(1)}KB > ${(maxFileSizeBytes / 1024).toFixed(1)}KB): ${workspaceCandidate.originalPath}`);
1508:         continue;
1509:       }
1510:     } catch {
1511:       continue;
1512:     }
1513: 
1514:     seenFiles.add(workspaceCandidate.canonicalPath);
1515:     dedupedFiles.push(workspaceCandidate.originalPath);
1516:   }
1517: 
1518:   return dedupedFiles;
1519: }

476:   `);
477: 
478:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
479:   database.prepare(`
480:     INSERT OR IGNORE INTO parser_skip_list (
481:       file_path, error_class, error_message, added_at, last_seen_at, attempt_count, source
482:     )
483:     SELECT
484:       file_path,
485:       'B1',
486:       error_message,
487:       ?,
488:       ?,
489:       MAX(1, error_count),
490:       'seed'
491:     FROM parse_diagnostics
492:     WHERE error_message LIKE '%resolved is not a function%'
493:   `).run(now, now);
494: }
495: 
496: /** Initialize (or get) the code graph database */
497: export function initDb(dbDir: string): Database.Database {
498:   if (db) return db;
499: 
500:   try {
501:     dbPath = join(dbDir, 'code-graph.sqlite');
502:     db = new Database(dbPath);
503:     // Explicit busy_timeout so concurrent writers wait up to 5s
504:     // for the writer lock instead of throwing SQLITE_BUSY immediately. Set
505:     // BEFORE journal_mode/foreign_keys so any incidental contention during
506:     // PRAGMA setup also benefits from the wait.
507:     db.pragma('busy_timeout = 5000');
508:     db.pragma('journal_mode = WAL'); // WAL enables concurrent readers without locks
509:     db.pragma('foreign_keys = ON');
510:     db.exec(SCHEMA_SQL);
511:     ensureSchemaMigrations(db);
512: 
513:     const versionRow = db.prepare('SELECT version FROM schema_version LIMIT 1').get() as { version: number } | undefined;
514:     if (!versionRow) {
515:       db.prepare('INSERT INTO schema_version (version) VALUES (?)').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
516:     } else if (versionRow.version < SCHEMA_VERSION) {
517:       db.prepare('UPDATE schema_version SET version = ?').run(SCHEMA_VERSION);
518:     }
519: 
520:     return db;
521:   } catch (err) {
522:     if (db) {
523:       try { db.close(); } catch { /* best effort cleanup for failed init */ }
524:     }
525:     db = null;
526:     dbPath = null;
527:     throw err;
528:   }
529: }
530: 
531: /** Get the current database instance (lazy-initializes if needed) */
532: export function getDb(): Database.Database {
533:   if (!db) initDb(DATABASE_DIR);
534:   // initDb either assigns the singleton database or throws before this return.
535:   return db!;
536: }
537: 
538: /** Close the database connection */
539: export function closeDb(): void {
540:   if (db) {
541:     db.close();
542:     db = null;
543:     dbPath = null;
544:   }
545: }
546: 
547: export function closeDbWithAssertion(): void {
548:   const handle = db;
549:   closeDb();
550:   assertDbHandleClosed(handle);
551: }

627: export function getLastDetectorProvenanceSummary(): DetectorProvenanceSummary | null {
628:   const result = getLastDetectorProvenanceSummaryWithDiagnostics();
629:   // Backward-compat: caller-visible API still returns null on
630:   // any non-resolved state; corrupt/invalid state is observable via the
631:   // *WithDiagnostics() companion below.
632:   return result.kind === 'resolved' ? result.value : null;
633: }

635: /**
636:  * Discriminated metadata read result so callers can distinguish
637:  * - 'absent': the row does not exist (first-write hasn't happened yet)
638:  * - 'resolved': the row exists, parsed cleanly, and matches the expected shape
639:  * - 'corrupt': the row exists but is not valid JSON (write-side bug or
640:  *   storage corruption)
641:  * - 'invalid-shape': the row parsed as JSON but does not match the expected
642:  *   shape (schema-version drift or write-side type bug)
643:  *
644:  * The original API (returns `null` on absent / corrupt / invalid-shape) is
645:  * preserved; new callers use these companions to react differently to corrupt
646:  * state vs absent state (e.g. quarantine the row instead of silently rebuilding).
647:  */
648: export type MetadataReadResult<T> =
649:   | { kind: 'absent' }
650:   | { kind: 'resolved'; value: T }
651:   | { kind: 'corrupt'; raw: string }
652:   | { kind: 'invalid-shape'; raw: string };
653: 
654: export function getLastDetectorProvenanceSummaryWithDiagnostics(): MetadataReadResult<DetectorProvenanceSummary> {
655:   // Typed read: distinguish absent vs corrupt vs invalid.
656:   const value = getMetadata('last_detector_provenance_summary');
657:   if (!value) {
658:     return { kind: 'absent' };
659:   }
660:   let parsed: unknown;
661:   try {
662:     parsed = JSON.parse(value);
663:   } catch {
664:     return { kind: 'corrupt', raw: value };
665:   }
666:   // Shape guard: must be an object with `dominant` and `counts` properties.
667:   if (
668:     typeof parsed !== 'object'
669:     || parsed === null
670:     || !('dominant' in parsed)
671:     || !('counts' in parsed)
672:   ) {
673:     return { kind: 'invalid-shape', raw: value };
674:   }
675:   return { kind: 'resolved', value: parsed as DetectorProvenanceSummary };
676: }
677: 
678: export function setLastDetectorProvenanceSummary(summary: DetectorProvenanceSummary): void {
679:   setMetadata('last_detector_provenance_summary', JSON.stringify(summary));
680: }

722: function isFailedScanRecord(value: unknown): value is FailedScanRecord {
723:   if (typeof value !== 'object' || value === null) {
724:     return false;
725:   }
726:   const record = value as Record<string, unknown>;
727:   return typeof record.reason === 'string'
728:     && typeof record.totalFiles === 'number'
729:     && typeof record.totalNodes === 'number'
730:     && typeof record.parseErrorCount === 'number'
731:     && typeof record.parseErrorRatio === 'number'
732:     && typeof record.recordedAt === 'string';
733: }
734: 
735: export function recordFailedScan(
736:   record: Omit<FailedScanRecord, 'recordedAt'>,
737: ): FailedScanRecord {
738:   const failedScan: FailedScanRecord = {
739:     ...record,
740:     recordedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
741:   };
742:   setMetadata('last_failed_scan', JSON.stringify(failedScan));
743:   return failedScan;
744: }
745: 
746: export function getLastFailedScan(): FailedScanRecord | null {
747:   const raw = getMetadata('last_failed_scan');
748:   if (!raw) {
749:     return null;
750:   }
751: 
752:   try {
753:     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw) as unknown;
754:     return isFailedScanRecord(parsed) ? parsed : null;
755:   } catch {
756:     return null;
757:   }
758: }

766: export function getLastGoldVerificationWithDiagnostics(): MetadataReadResult<object> {

772:   let parsed: unknown;
773:   try {
774:     parsed = JSON.parse(value);
775:   } catch {
776:     return { kind: 'corrupt', raw: value };
777:   }
778:   // Shape guard: gold verification must be an object (it's a free-form
779:   // record but never a primitive). The detailed shape check stays at the
780:   // call sites that already use isRecord() etc.
781:   if (typeof parsed !== 'object' || parsed === null) {
782:     return { kind: 'invalid-shape', raw: value };
783:   }
784:   return { kind: 'resolved', value: parsed as object };

801: /**
802:  * Insert or update a file record, returning the file ID.
803:  *
804:  * `options.fileMtimeMs` lets callers stage a placeholder mtime (e.g. `0`)
805:  * during multi-step structural persistence so `isFileStale()` continues
806:  * flagging the file as stale until nodes + edges have landed.
807:  * When omitted, the current on-disk mtime is used (original behavior).
808:  */
809: export function upsertFile(
810:   filePath: string,
811:   language: string,
812:   contentHash: string,
813:   nodeCount: number,
814:   edgeCount: number,
815:   parseHealth: string,
816:   parseDurationMs: number,
817:   options?: { fileMtimeMs?: number },
818: ): number {
819:   const d = getDb();
820:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
821:   const fileMtimeMs = options?.fileMtimeMs !== undefined
822:     ? options.fileMtimeMs
823:     : (getCurrentFileMtimeMs(filePath) ?? 0);
824: 
825:   const existing = d.prepare('SELECT id FROM code_files WHERE file_path = ?').get(filePath) as { id: number } | undefined;
826:   if (existing) {
827:     d.prepare(`
828:       UPDATE code_files SET language = ?, content_hash = ?, node_count = ?, edge_count = ?,
829:         file_mtime_ms = ?, parse_health = ?, indexed_at = ?, parse_duration_ms = ?
830:       WHERE id = ?
831:     `).run(language, contentHash, nodeCount, edgeCount, fileMtimeMs, parseHealth, now, parseDurationMs, existing.id);
832:     return existing.id;
833:   }
834: 
835:   const result = d.prepare(`
836:     INSERT INTO code_files (
837:       file_path, language, content_hash, file_mtime_ms, node_count, edge_count, parse_health, indexed_at, parse_duration_ms
838:     )
839:     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
840:   `).run(filePath, language, contentHash, fileMtimeMs, nodeCount, edgeCount, parseHealth, now, parseDurationMs);
841:   return Number(result.lastInsertRowid);
842: }
843: 
844: export function recordParseDiagnostic(filePath: string, errorMessage: string): ParseDiagnostic {
845:   const d = getDb();
846:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
847:   const message = errorMessage.length > 0
848:     ? errorMessage
849:     : 'Parser returned parseHealth="error" without diagnostics';
850:   d.prepare(`
851:     INSERT INTO parse_diagnostics (file_path, error_message, error_count, last_seen_at)
852:     VALUES (?, ?, 1, ?)
853:     ON CONFLICT(file_path) DO UPDATE SET
854:       error_message = excluded.error_message,
855:       error_count = parse_diagnostics.error_count + 1,
856:       last_seen_at = excluded.last_seen_at
857:   `).run(filePath, message, now);
858: 
859:   const row = d.prepare(`
860:     SELECT file_path, error_message, error_count, last_seen_at
861:     FROM parse_diagnostics
862:     WHERE file_path = ?
863:   `).get(filePath) as {
864:     file_path: string;
865:     error_message: string;
866:     error_count: number;
867:     last_seen_at: string;
868:   };
869: 
870:   return {
871:     filePath: row.file_path,
872:     errorMessage: row.error_message,
873:     errorCount: row.error_count,
874:     lastSeenAt: row.last_seen_at,
875:   };
876: }

878: export function clearParseDiagnostic(filePath: string): void {

883: export function getParseDiagnosticsSummary(limit: number = 5): ParseDiagnosticsSummary {
884:   const d = getDb();
885:   const safeLimit = Number.isFinite(limit) ? Math.max(1, Math.min(Math.trunc(limit), 20)) : 5;
886:   const affected = d.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM parse_diagnostics').get() as { count: number };
887:   const rows = d.prepare(`
888:     SELECT file_path, error_message, error_count, last_seen_at
889:     FROM parse_diagnostics
890:     ORDER BY last_seen_at DESC, file_path ASC
891:     LIMIT ?
892:   `).all(safeLimit) as Array<{
893:     file_path: string;
894:     error_message: string;
895:     error_count: number;
896:     last_seen_at: string;
897:   }>;
898: 
899:   return {
900:     affectedFiles: affected.count,
901:     recentErrors: rows.map((row) => ({
902:       filePath: row.file_path,
903:       errorMessage: row.error_message,
904:       errorCount: row.error_count,
905:       lastSeenAt: row.last_seen_at,
906:     })),
907:   };
908: }

913:     SELECT COUNT(*) AS count
914:     FROM parse_diagnostics diagnostic
915:     INNER JOIN code_files file ON file.file_path = diagnostic.file_path
916:   `).get() as { count: number };

920: /** Batch insert nodes for a file (deletes existing first) */
921: export function replaceNodes(fileId: number, nodes: CodeNode[]): void {
922:   const d = getDb();
923:   const insert = d.prepare(`
924:     INSERT OR IGNORE INTO code_nodes (symbol_id, file_id, file_path, fq_name, kind, name,
925:       start_line, end_line, start_column, end_column, language, signature, docstring, content_hash)
926:     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
927:   `);
928: 
929:   const tx = d.transaction(() => {
930:     const deletedAt = new Date().toISOString();
931:     const oldSymbolRows = d.prepare('SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes WHERE file_id = ?').all(fileId) as { symbol_id: string }[];
932:     const oldSymbolIds = oldSymbolRows.map(row => row.symbol_id);
933: 
934:     recordNodeTombstones(d, fileId, 'replace_nodes_reindex', deletedAt);
935:     recordEdgeTombstonesForSymbols(d, oldSymbolIds, 'replace_nodes_reindex', deletedAt);
936:     d.prepare('DELETE FROM code_nodes WHERE file_id = ?').run(fileId);
937: 
938:     if (oldSymbolIds.length > 0) {
939:       const placeholders = oldSymbolIds.map(() => '?').join(',');
940:       d.prepare(`
941:         DELETE FROM code_edges
942:         WHERE source_id IN (${placeholders}) OR target_id IN (${placeholders})
943:       `).run(...oldSymbolIds, ...oldSymbolIds);
944:     }
945: 
946:     for (const n of nodes) {
947:       insert.run(
948:         n.symbolId, fileId, n.filePath, n.fqName, n.kind, n.name,
949:         n.startLine, n.endLine, n.startColumn, n.endColumn,
950:         n.language, n.signature ?? null, n.docstring ?? null, n.contentHash,
951:       );
952:     }
953:   });
954:   tx();
955: }
956: 
957: export interface ReplaceEdgesOptions {
958:   /**
959:    * When true, skip the inline dangling-target prune. During a full
960:    * scan, files are persisted one at a time; a cross-file IMPORTS edge whose
961:    * target lives in a not-yet-persisted file would otherwise be deleted here
962:    * (its `target_id` is not yet in `code_nodes`) and is never restored, because
963:    * the post-persist resolver only reconciles CALLS edges. Callers that persist
964:    * many files in sequence pass `true` and run `pruneDanglingEdges()` ONCE after
965:    * all nodes + cross-file resolution land. Selective/single-file callers leave
966:    * it false so genuinely-dangling edges are cleaned inline.
967:    */
968:   deferDanglingTargetPrune?: boolean;
969: }
970: 
971: /** Batch insert edges (deletes edges from the source nodes first) */
972: export function replaceEdges(sourceIds: string[], edges: CodeEdge[], opts: ReplaceEdgesOptions = {}): void {
973:   const d = getDb();
974: 
975:   const insert = d.prepare(`
976:     INSERT INTO code_edges (source_id, target_id, edge_type, weight, metadata)
977:     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
978:   `);
979: 
980:   const tx = d.transaction(() => {
981:     const deletedAt = new Date().toISOString();
982:     if (sourceIds.length > 0) {
983:       const placeholders = sourceIds.map(() => '?').join(',');
984:       recordEdgeTombstonesForSources(d, sourceIds, 'replace_edges_reindex', deletedAt);
985:       d.prepare(`DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE source_id IN (${placeholders})`).run(...sourceIds);
986:     }
987: 
988:     const candidateSourceIds = [...new Set(edges.map((edge) => edge.sourceId))];
989:     const retainedSourceIds = new Set<string>();
990:     if (candidateSourceIds.length > 0) {
991:       const placeholders = candidateSourceIds.map(() => '?').join(',');
992:       const rows = d.prepare(`
993:         SELECT symbol_id
994:         FROM code_nodes
995:         WHERE symbol_id IN (${placeholders})
996:       `).all(...candidateSourceIds) as Array<{ symbol_id: string }>;
997:       for (const row of rows) {
998:         retainedSourceIds.add(row.symbol_id);
999:       }
1000:     }
1001: 
1002:     for (const e of edges) {
1003:       if (!retainedSourceIds.has(e.sourceId)) {
1004:         continue;
1005:       }
1006:       insert.run(e.sourceId, e.targetId, e.edgeType, e.weight, e.metadata ? JSON.stringify(e.metadata) : null);
1007:     }
1008: 
1009:     if (!opts.deferDanglingTargetPrune) {
1010:       recordDanglingEdgeTombstones(d, 'replace_edges_dangling_prune', deletedAt);
1011:       d.prepare(`
1012:         DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE
1013:           source_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes) OR
1014:           target_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes)
1015:       `).run();
1016:     }
1017:   });
1018:   tx();
1019: }
1020: 
1021: /**
1022:  * Remove edges whose source or target symbol no longer exists in
1023:  * `code_nodes`. Run ONCE after a multi-file scan (all nodes persisted +
1024:  * cross-file resolution complete) when per-file `replaceEdges` deferred its
1025:  * inline prune. Returns the number of edges removed.
1026:  */
1027: export function pruneDanglingEdges(): number {
1028:   const d = getDb();
1029:   recordDanglingEdgeTombstones(d, 'dangling_edge_prune', new Date().toISOString());
1030:   const result = d.prepare(`
1031:     DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE
1032:       source_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes) OR
1033:       target_id NOT IN (SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes)
1034:   `).run();
1035:   return result.changes;
1036: }
1037: 
1038: /** Check if a file needs re-indexing based on stored mtime and content hash */
1039: export function isFileStale(filePath: string, options?: { currentContentHash?: string }): boolean {
1040:   const d = getDb();
1041:   const row = d.prepare('SELECT file_mtime_ms, content_hash FROM code_files WHERE file_path = ?').get(filePath) as {
1042:     file_mtime_ms: number;
1043:     content_hash: string | null;
1044:   } | undefined;
1045:   if (!row) return true;
1046:   const currentMtimeMs = getCurrentFileMtimeMs(filePath);
1047:   if (currentMtimeMs === null) return true;
1048:   if (!row.content_hash) return true;
1049: 
1050:   // Hash content on mtime drift before declaring stale. A touch
1051:   // (mtime drift, content unchanged) used to force reindex; now it stays
1052:   // fresh as long as the content hash matches. Avoids gratuitous full-scans
1053:   // on `git checkout` of unchanged files.
1054:   const currentContentHash = options?.currentContentHash ?? getCurrentFileContentHash(filePath);
1055:   if (currentContentHash === null) return true;
1056:   return row.content_hash !== currentContentHash;
1057: }
1058: 
1059: /** Batch stale check for a set of file paths */
1060: export interface FreshFilesResult {
1061:   readonly stale: string[];
1062:   readonly fresh: string[];
1063: }
1064: 
1065: export function ensureFreshFiles(filePaths: string[]): FreshFilesResult {
1066:   const uniquePaths = [...new Set(filePaths)];
1067:   if (uniquePaths.length === 0) {
1068:     return { stale: [], fresh: [] };
1069:   }
1070: 
1071:   const d = getDb();
1072:   const placeholders = uniquePaths.map(() => '?').join(',');
1073:   const rows = d.prepare(`
1074:     SELECT file_path, file_mtime_ms, content_hash
1075:     FROM code_files
1076:     WHERE file_path IN (${placeholders})
1077:   `).all(...uniquePaths) as Array<{ file_path: string; file_mtime_ms: number; content_hash: string | null }>;
1078:   const storedFiles = new Map(rows.map((row) => [row.file_path, row]));
1079: 
1080:   const stale: string[] = [];
1081:   const fresh: string[] = [];
1082: 
1083:   for (const filePath of uniquePaths) {
1084:     const storedFile = storedFiles.get(filePath);
1085:     const currentMtimeMs = getCurrentFileMtimeMs(filePath);
1086:     if (storedFile === undefined || currentMtimeMs === null) {
1087:       stale.push(filePath);
1088:       continue;
1089:     }
1090:     if (!storedFile.content_hash) {
1091:       stale.push(filePath);
1092:       continue;
1093:     }
1094:     // Hash on mtime drift before declaring stale. Touch-only
1095:     // changes (mtime drift, content unchanged) stay fresh; only real content
1096:     // changes flip the file to stale.
1097:     const currentContentHash = getCurrentFileContentHash(filePath);
1098:     if (currentContentHash === null || storedFile.content_hash !== currentContentHash) {
1099:       stale.push(filePath);
1100:       continue;
1101:     }
1102:     fresh.push(filePath);
1103:   }
1104: 
1105:   return { stale, fresh };
1106: }
1107: 
1108: /** Get all tracked file paths from the graph database */
1109: export function getTrackedFiles(): string[] {
1110:   const d = getDb();
1111:   const rows = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_files').all() as Array<{ file_path: string }>;
1112:   return rows.map((row) => row.file_path);
1113: }

1115: export function countTrackedSkillFiles(): number {
1116:   const d = getDb();
1117:   const skillLikePatterns = CODE_GRAPH_SKILL_EXCLUDE_GLOBS.map((glob) => {
1118:     const pathSegment = glob.replace(/^\*\*\//, '').replace(/\/\*\*$/, '');
1119:     return `%${pathSegment}/%`;
1120:   });
1121:   if (skillLikePatterns.length === 0) {
1122:     return 0;
1123:   }
1124:   const whereClause = skillLikePatterns.map(() => 'file_path LIKE ?').join(' OR ');
1125:   const row = d.prepare(`
1126:     SELECT COUNT(*) AS count
1127:     FROM code_files
1128:     WHERE ${whereClause}
1129:   `).get(...skillLikePatterns) as { count: number } | undefined;
1130:   return row?.count ?? 0;
1131: }

1133: /** Remove a file and its nodes/edges from the graph */
1134: export function removeFile(filePath: string, options: DeleteAuditOptions = {}): void {
1135:   const d = getDb();
1136:   const file = d.prepare('SELECT id FROM code_files WHERE file_path = ?').get(filePath) as { id: number } | undefined;
1137:   if (!file) return;
1138:   // CG-003: wrap the edge-delete + file-delete (CASCADE drops nodes) in a single
1139:   // transaction so a crash mid-call cannot leave orphaned edges / partial graph state.
1140:   const tx = d.transaction(() => {
1141:     const deletedAt = new Date().toISOString();
1142:     const reason = options.reason ?? 'tracked_file_removed';
1143:     const nodeIds = d.prepare('SELECT symbol_id FROM code_nodes WHERE file_id = ?').all(file.id) as { symbol_id: string }[];
1144:     if (nodeIds.length > 0) {
1145:       const placeholders = nodeIds.map(() => '?').join(',');
1146:       const ids = nodeIds.map(n => n.symbol_id);
1147:       recordNodeTombstones(d, file.id, reason, deletedAt);
1148:       recordEdgeTombstonesForSymbols(d, ids, reason, deletedAt);
1149:       d.prepare(`DELETE FROM code_edges WHERE source_id IN (${placeholders}) OR target_id IN (${placeholders})`).run(...ids, ...ids);
1150:     }
1151:     recordFileTombstone(d, filePath, reason, deletedAt);
1152:     d.prepare('DELETE FROM code_files WHERE id = ?').run(file.id);
1153:   });
1154:   tx();
1155: }

1159:   const d = getDb();

1167: /** Query: startup-friendly highlights — most-called project symbols (incoming calls). */
1168: export function queryStartupHighlights(limit: number = 5): StartupHighlight[] {
1169:   const d = getDb();
1170:   const safeLimit = Number.isFinite(limit) ? Math.max(1, Math.min(Math.trunc(limit), 20)) : 5;
1171:   const rows = d.prepare(`
1172:     WITH filtered_nodes AS (
1173:       SELECT n.symbol_id, n.name, n.kind, n.file_path, n.start_line
1174:       FROM code_nodes n
1175:       WHERE n.kind IN ('class', 'function', 'method', 'interface', 'type_alias', 'module')
1176:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/site-packages/%'
1177:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/node_modules/%'
1178:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/.venv/%'
1179:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/vendor/%'
1180:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/dist/%'
1181:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/build/%'
1182:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/__pycache__/%'
1183:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/tests/%'
1184:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%/test_%'
1185:         AND n.file_path NOT LIKE '%__tests__%'
1186:     ),
1187:     aggregated AS (
1188:       SELECT
1189:         fn.name as name,
1190:         fn.kind as kind,
1191:         fn.file_path as file_path,
1192:         MIN(fn.start_line) as start_line,
1193:         COALESCE(SUM(CASE WHEN UPPER(e.edge_type) = 'CALLS' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END), 0) as call_count
1194:       FROM filtered_nodes fn
1195:       LEFT JOIN code_edges e
1196:         ON e.target_id = fn.symbol_id
1197:       GROUP BY fn.name, fn.kind, fn.file_path
1198:     ),
1199:     ranked AS (
1200:       SELECT
1201:         name,
1202:         kind,
1203:         file_path,
1204:         call_count,
1205:         start_line,
1206:         ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
1207:           PARTITION BY file_path
1208:           ORDER BY call_count DESC, start_line ASC
1209:         ) as file_rank
1210:       FROM aggregated
1211:     )
1212:     SELECT
1213:       name,
1214:       kind,
1215:       file_path,
1216:       call_count
1217:     FROM ranked
1218:     WHERE file_rank <= 2
1219:     ORDER BY call_count DESC, start_line ASC
1220:     LIMIT ?
1221:   `).all(safeLimit) as Array<{
1222:     name: string;
1223:     kind: string;
1224:     file_path: string;
1225:     call_count: number;
1226:   }>;
1227: 
1228:   return rows.map((row) => ({
1229:     name: row.name,
1230:     kind: row.kind,
1231:     filePath: row.file_path,
1232:     callCount: row.call_count,
1233:   }));
1234: }

1265: /** Query: get edges from a symbol */
1266: export interface CodeEdgeTargetResult {
1267:   readonly edge: CodeEdge;

1271: export function queryEdgesFrom(symbolId: string, edgeType?: string): CodeEdgeTargetResult[] {
1272:   const d = getDb();
1273:   let sql = 'SELECT * FROM code_edges WHERE source_id = ?';
1274:   const params: unknown[] = [symbolId];
1275:   if (edgeType) {
1276:     sql += ' AND edge_type = ?';
1277:     params.push(edgeType);
1278:   }
1279: 
1280:   const edges = d.prepare(sql).all(...params) as Record<string, unknown>[];
1281:   return edges.map(e => {
1282:     const edge = rowToEdge(e);
1283:     const targetRow = d.prepare('SELECT * FROM code_nodes WHERE symbol_id = ?').get(edge.targetId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;
1284:     return { edge, targetNode: targetRow ? rowToNode(targetRow) : null };
1285:   });

1294: export function queryEdgesTo(symbolId: string, edgeType?: string): CodeEdgeSourceResult[] {
1295:   const d = getDb();

1297:   const params: unknown[] = [symbolId];
1298:   if (edgeType) {
1299:     sql += ' AND edge_type = ?';
1300:     params.push(edgeType);
1301:   }

1311: export function resolveSubjectFilePath(subject: string): string | null {
1312:   const d = getDb();
1313: 
1314:   const candidates = [subject];
1315:   if (!isAbsolute(subject)) {
1316:     candidates.push(resolvePath(process.cwd(), subject));
1317:   }
1318: 
1319:   for (const candidate of candidates) {
1320:     const directFile = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_files WHERE file_path = ? LIMIT 1').get(candidate) as { file_path: string } | undefined;
1321:     if (directFile) return directFile.file_path;
1322:   }
1323: 
1324:   const byId = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_nodes WHERE symbol_id = ? LIMIT 1').get(subject) as { file_path: string } | undefined;
1325:   if (byId) return byId.file_path;
1326: 
1327:   const byFq = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_nodes WHERE fq_name = ? LIMIT 1').get(subject) as { file_path: string } | undefined;
1328:   if (byFq) return byFq.file_path;
1329: 
1330:   const byName = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_nodes WHERE name = ? LIMIT 1').get(subject) as { file_path: string } | undefined;
1331:   if (byName) return byName.file_path;
1332: 
1333:   const escapedSubject = subject.replace(/[%_]/g, '\\$&');
1334:   const like = d.prepare('SELECT file_path FROM code_files WHERE file_path LIKE ? LIMIT 1').get(`%${escapedSubject}`) as { file_path: string } | undefined;
1335:   if (like) return like.file_path;
1336: 
1337:   return null;
1338: }

1340: export function queryFileImportDependents(): FileImportDependent[] {
1341:   const d = getDb();
1342:   const rows = d.prepare(`
1343:     SELECT DISTINCT
1344:       target.file_path AS imported_file_path,
1345:       source.file_path AS importer_file_path
1346:     FROM code_edges edge
1347:     INNER JOIN code_nodes source ON source.symbol_id = edge.source_id
1348:     INNER JOIN code_nodes target ON target.symbol_id = edge.target_id
1349:     WHERE UPPER(edge.edge_type) = 'IMPORTS'
1350:       AND source.file_path != target.file_path
1351:   `).all() as Array<{
1352:     imported_file_path: string;
1353:     importer_file_path: string;
1354:   }>;
1355: 
1356:   return rows.map((row) => ({
1357:     importedFilePath: row.imported_file_path,
1358:     importerFilePath: row.importer_file_path,
1359:   }));
1360: }
