1362: export function queryFileDegrees(filePaths: string[]): FileDegree[] {
1363:   const uniquePaths = [...new Set(filePaths.filter((filePath) => typeof filePath === 'string' && filePath.length > 0))];
1364:   if (uniquePaths.length === 0) {
1365:     return [];
1366:   }
1367: 
1368:   const d = getDb();
1369:   const placeholders = uniquePaths.map(() => '?').join(',');
1370:   const rows = d.prepare(`
1371:     SELECT
1372:       node_file_path,
1373:       COUNT(DISTINCT connected_file_path) AS degree
1374:     FROM (
1375:       SELECT
1376:         source.file_path AS node_file_path,
1377:         target.file_path AS connected_file_path
1378:       FROM code_edges edge
1379:       INNER JOIN code_nodes source ON source.symbol_id = edge.source_id
1380:       INNER JOIN code_nodes target ON target.symbol_id = edge.target_id
1381:       WHERE source.file_path IN (${placeholders})
1382:         AND source.file_path != target.file_path
1383: 
1384:       UNION ALL
1385: 
1386:       SELECT
1387:         target.file_path AS node_file_path,
1388:         source.file_path AS connected_file_path
1389:       FROM code_edges edge
1390:       INNER JOIN code_nodes source ON source.symbol_id = edge.source_id
1391:       INNER JOIN code_nodes target ON target.symbol_id = edge.target_id
1392:       WHERE target.file_path IN (${placeholders})
1393:         AND source.file_path != target.file_path
1394:     )
1395:     GROUP BY node_file_path
1396:   `).all(...uniquePaths, ...uniquePaths) as Array<{
1397:     node_file_path: string;
1398:     degree: number;
1399:   }>;
1400: 
1401:   const degreeByFile = new Map(rows.map((row) => [row.node_file_path, row.degree]));
1402:   return uniquePaths.map((filePath) => ({
1403:     filePath,
1404:     degree: degreeByFile.get(filePath) ?? 0,
1405:   }));
1406: }

1425:   const totalNodes = (d.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as c FROM code_nodes').get() as { c: number }).c;
1426:   const totalEdges = (d.prepare('SELECT COUNT(*) as c FROM code_edges').get() as { c: number }).c;

1428:   const nodesByKind: Record<string, number> = {};

1436:   const parseHealthSummary: Record<string, number> = {};

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Code Graph Query Handler
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // MCP tool handler for code_graph_query — queries structural relationships.
5: 
6: import * as graphDb from '../lib/code-graph-db.js';
7: import { ensureCodeGraphReady, type ReadyResult } from '../lib/ensure-ready.js';
8: import type { CodeNode, EdgeType } from '../lib/indexer-types.js';

11:   attachStructuralTrustFields,
12:   type EdgeEvidenceClass,
13:   type HotFileBreadcrumb,
14: } from '../lib/shared/shared-payload.js';
15: import {
16:   buildQueryTrustMetadata,
17:   buildReadinessBlock,
18: } from '../lib/readiness-contract.js';

28: import {
29:   isCodeGraphBm25SymbolResolverEnabled,
30:   resolveSymbolBm25Candidates,
31:   type SymbolBm25Candidate,

33: // Emit a stable failure-counter metric so operators can distinguish DB /
34: // compute failures from legitimately empty blast radii.
35: import { isSpeckitMetricsEnabled, speckitMetrics } from '../lib/shared/metrics-stub.js';
36: 
37: export interface QueryArgs {
38:   operation: 'outline' | 'calls_from' | 'calls_to' | 'imports_from' | 'imports_to' | 'blast_radius';
39:   subject: string; // filePath, fqName, or symbolId
40:   subjects?: string[];
41:   edgeType?: string;
42:   limit?: number;
43:   includeTransitive?: boolean;
44:   maxDepth?: number;
45:   unionMode?: 'single' | 'multi';
46:   minConfidence?: number;
47:   includeTrace?: boolean;
48:   verificationGateBypass?: 'gold-query-verifier';
49: }
50: 
51: const SUPPORTED_EDGE_TYPES = [
52:   'CALLS',
53:   'CONTAINS',
54:   'DECORATES',
55:   'EXPORTS',
56:   'EXTENDS',
57:   'IMPLEMENTS',
58:   'IMPORTS',
59:   'OVERRIDES',
60:   'TESTED_BY',
61:   'TYPE_OF',
62: ] as const satisfies readonly EdgeType[];
63: 
64: const SUPPORTED_EDGE_TYPE_SET = new Set<string>(SUPPORTED_EDGE_TYPES);
65: const AMBIGUOUS_SUBJECT_WARNING_CANDIDATE_LIMIT = 10;
66: const SYMBOL_BM25_SUGGESTION_LIMIT = 5;
67: const SUPPORTED_RELATIONSHIP_OPERATIONS = [
68:   'calls_from',
69:   'calls_to',
70:   'imports_from',
71:   'imports_to',
72: ] as const;
73: const SUPPORTED_RELATIONSHIP_OPERATION_SET = new Set<string>(SUPPORTED_RELATIONSHIP_OPERATIONS);

75: /**
76:  * Regex used to decide whether a free-form `subject` argument looks like a
77:  * file path (so we should resolve it through `graphDb.resolveSubjectFilePath`)
78:  * versus a symbol name.
79:  *
80:  * Intentionally broader than `detectLanguage` in `lib/indexer-types.ts`: this
81:  * pattern recognises non-source assets the indexer does not parse but that
82:  * users still pass as subjects (e.g. `README.md`, `package.json`). Keep both
83:  * lists in sync at the language-coverage level only — extension drift between
84:  * the two lists is expected and documented here.
85:  */
86: const FILE_PATH_EXTENSION_PATTERN = /\.(?:[cm]?[jt]sx?|py|bash|zsh|sh|json|md)$/i;
87: const FILE_PATH_SEPARATOR_PATTERN = /[\\/]/;

90:   return SUPPORTED_EDGE_TYPE_SET.has(edgeType);
91: }

93: function isSupportedRelationshipOperation(
94:   operation: string,

104:     }],

107: 
108: function resolveRequestedEdgeType(args: QueryArgs): { edgeType?: EdgeType; error?: string } {
109:   if (typeof args.edgeType === 'string' && args.edgeType.trim().length > 0) {
110:     const normalizedEdgeType = args.edgeType.trim().toUpperCase();
111:     // Reject typoed filters so empty results never masquerade as valid absence.
112:     if (!isSupportedEdgeType(normalizedEdgeType)) {
113:       return {
114:         error: `Unsupported edgeType "${args.edgeType}". Supported edge types: ${SUPPORTED_EDGE_TYPES.join(', ')}`,
115:       };
116:     }
117:     return { edgeType: normalizedEdgeType };
118:   }
119: 
120:   if (args.operation.startsWith('calls')) {
121:     return { edgeType: 'CALLS' };
122:   }
123: 
124:   if (args.operation.startsWith('imports')) {
125:     return { edgeType: 'IMPORTS' };
126:   }
127: 
128:   return {};
129: }
130:

195: interface BlastRadiusFailureFallback {
196:   reason: string;
197:   /**
198:    * Stable machine-readable failure code so operators can distinguish
199:    * failure modes (DB error vs ambiguous subject vs unresolved
200:    * subject vs limit reached vs empty source) without parsing free-form
201:    * `reason` strings. Optional for backward compatibility with older
202:    * call sites.
203:    */
204:   code?: BlastRadiusFailureCode;
205:   partialResult?: {
206:     sourceFiles: string[];
207:     nodes: BlastRadiusAffectedFile[];
208:     affectedFiles: BlastRadiusAffectedFile[];

210:   };

213: /**
214:  * Stable failure-fallback code values surfaced via
215:  * `BlastRadiusFailureFallback.code`. Adding new values is non-breaking; do
216:  * not rename existing literals once consumers depend on them.
217:  */
218: type BlastRadiusFailureCode =
219:   | 'limit_reached'
220:   | 'unresolved_subject'
221:   | 'ambiguous_subject'
222:   | 'empty_source'
223:   | 'compute_error';
224: 
225: type FallbackDecision = {
226:   nextTool: 'code_graph_scan' | 'code_graph_query' | 'rg';
227:   reason: 'full_scan_required' | 'selective_reindex' | 'scan_failed' | 'scan_declined';
228:   retryAfter?: 'scan_complete';

231: function querySubjectMatches(
232:   field: 'fq_name' | 'name',
233:   subject: string,
234: ): SubjectMatchResult {
235:   const d = graphDb.getDb();
236:   const candidates = d.prepare(`
237:     SELECT symbol_id, fq_name, name, kind, file_path, start_line
238:     FROM code_nodes
239:     WHERE ${field} = ?
240:     ORDER BY file_path, start_line, symbol_id
241:   `).all(subject) as Array<{
242:     symbol_id: string;
243:     fq_name: string | null;
244:     name: string | null;
245:     kind: string | null;
246:     file_path: string | null;
247:     start_line: number | null;
248:   }>;
249: 
250:   if (candidates.length === 0) {
251:     return { candidates: [], count: 0 };
252:   }
253: 
254:   return {
255:     candidates: candidates.map((candidate) => ({
256:       symbolId: candidate.symbol_id,
257:       fqName: candidate.fq_name,
258:       name: candidate.name,
259:       kind: candidate.kind,
260:       filePath: candidate.file_path,
261:       startLine: candidate.start_line,
262:     })),
263:     count: candidates.length,
264:   };
265: }
266: 
267: function relationshipEdgeCount(
268:   candidate: SubjectCandidateMetadata,
269:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'],
270:   edgeType: EdgeType | undefined,
271: ): number {
272:   if (!candidate.symbolId) {
273:     return 0;
274:   }
275: 
276:   const resolvedEdgeType = edgeType ?? (
277:     operation.startsWith('calls')
278:       ? 'CALLS'
279:       : operation.startsWith('imports')
280:         ? 'IMPORTS'
281:         : undefined
282:   );
283: 
284:   if (!resolvedEdgeType) {
285:     return 0;
286:   }
287: 
288:   if (operation === 'calls_from' || operation === 'imports_from') {
289:     return graphDb.queryEdgesFrom(candidate.symbolId, resolvedEdgeType).length;
290:   }
291: 
292:   if (operation === 'calls_to' || operation === 'imports_to') {
293:     return graphDb.queryEdgesTo(candidate.symbolId, resolvedEdgeType).length;
294:   }
295: 
296:   return 0;
297: }
298: 
299: function callableKindRank(kind: string | null): number {
300:   switch (kind) {
301:     case 'function':
302:     case 'method':
303:       return 0;
304:     case 'class':
305:       return 1;
306:     case 'variable':
307:       return 2;
308:     default:
309:       return 3;
310:   }
311: }
312: 
313: function pickOperationAwareCandidate(
314:   candidates: SubjectCandidateMetadata[],
315:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'],
316:   edgeType: EdgeType | undefined,
317: ): {
318:   selectedCandidate: SubjectCandidateMetadata;
319:   candidates: SubjectCandidateMetadata[];
320:   selectionReason: string;
321: } {
322:   const rankedCandidates = candidates
323:     .map((candidate) => ({
324:       ...candidate,
325:       operationEdgeCount: relationshipEdgeCount(candidate, operation, edgeType),
326:       selectedForOperation: operation,
327:     }))
328:     .sort((left, right) => (
329:       (right.operationEdgeCount ?? 0) - (left.operationEdgeCount ?? 0)
330:       || callableKindRank(left.kind) - callableKindRank(right.kind)
331:       || (left.filePath ?? '').localeCompare(right.filePath ?? '')
332:       || (left.startLine ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER) - (right.startLine ?? Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER)
333:       || left.symbolId.localeCompare(right.symbolId)
334:     ));
335: 
336:   const selectedCandidate = rankedCandidates[0] ?? candidates[0];
337:   const highestEdgeCount = selectedCandidate?.operationEdgeCount ?? 0;
338:   const hasEdgeSignal = highestEdgeCount > 0;
339:   const selectionReason = hasEdgeSignal
340:     ? `${operation} edge count`
341:     : callableKindRank(selectedCandidate?.kind ?? null) === 0
342:       ? 'callable kind preference'
343:       : 'deterministic file ordering';
344: 
345:   return {
346:     selectedCandidate,
347:     candidates: rankedCandidates,
348:     selectionReason,
349:   };
350: }
351: 
352: function buildAmbiguousSubjectWarning(
353:   subject: string,
354:   matchField: 'fq_name' | 'name' | 'symbol_id',
355:   matchResult: SubjectMatchResult,
356:   selectedCandidate: SubjectCandidateMetadata,
357:   selectionReason: string,
358: ): AmbiguousSubjectWarning {
359:   const truncated = matchResult.count > matchResult.candidates.length;
360:   const shownCount = matchResult.candidates.length;
361:   const countLabel = truncated
362:     ? `${matchResult.count} total; showing first ${shownCount}`
363:     : `${matchResult.count}`;
364: 
365:   return {
366:     code: 'ambiguous_subject',
367:     subject,
368:     matchField,
369:     count: matchResult.count,
370:     candidates: matchResult.candidates,
371:     selectedCandidate,
372:     selectionReason,
373:     message: `Multiple code graph nodes matched subject "${subject}" by ${matchField} (${countLabel}). Selected ${selectedCandidate.symbolId} for ${selectedCandidate.selectedForOperation ?? 'query'} using ${selectionReason}. Use a symbolId to disambiguate the query.`,
374:   };
375: }
376: 
377: function querySymbolBm25Suggestions(subject: string): SymbolBm25Candidate[] {
378:   if (!isCodeGraphBm25SymbolResolverEnabled()) {
379:     return [];
380:   }
381: 
382:   return resolveSymbolBm25Candidates(
383:     subject,
384:     graphDb.querySymbolIndexRows(),
385:     { limit: SYMBOL_BM25_SUGGESTION_LIMIT },
386:   );
387: }
388: 
389: function buildUnresolvedSubjectError(subject: string, suggestions: SymbolBm25Candidate[]) {
390:   const base = {
391:     status: 'error' as const,
392:     error: `Could not resolve subject: ${subject}`,
393:   };
394: 
395:   if (suggestions.length === 0) {
396:     return base;
397:   }
398: 
399:   return {
400:     ...base,
401:     data: {
402:       symbolResolution: {
403:         resolver: 'bm25',
404:         disambiguationOnly: true,
405:         message: 'No exact symbol match was found. These are candidate symbols only; use a symbolId, fqName, or exact name to run a structural query.',
406:         candidates: suggestions,
407:       },
408:     },
409:   };
410: }
411: 
412: /** Resolve a subject string to a symbolId */
413: function resolveSubject(
414:   subject: string,
415:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'],
416:   edgeType: EdgeType | undefined,
417: ): ResolvedSubject {
418:   const d = graphDb.getDb();
419: 
420:   // Try as symbolId first
421:   const byId = d.prepare(`
422:     SELECT symbol_id, fq_name, name, kind, file_path, start_line
423:     FROM code_nodes
424:     WHERE symbol_id = ?
425:   `).get(subject) as {
426:     symbol_id: string;
427:     fq_name: string | null;
428:     name: string | null;
429:     kind: string | null;
430:     file_path: string | null;
431:     start_line: number | null;
432:   } | undefined;
433:   if (byId) {
434:     return {
435:       symbolId: byId.symbol_id,
436:       selectedCandidate: {
437:         symbolId: byId.symbol_id,
438:         fqName: byId.fq_name,
439:         name: byId.name,
440:         kind: byId.kind,
441:         filePath: byId.file_path,
442:         startLine: byId.start_line,
443:         selectedForOperation: operation,
444:       },
445:     };
446:   }
447: 
448:   // Try as fqName
449:   const byFq = querySubjectMatches('fq_name', subject);
450:   if (byFq.count > 0) {
451:     const selection = pickOperationAwareCandidate(byFq.candidates, operation, edgeType);
452:     return {
453:       symbolId: selection.selectedCandidate.symbolId,
454:       selectedCandidate: selection.selectedCandidate,
455:       ...(byFq.count > 1
456:         ? {
457:           warnings: [buildAmbiguousSubjectWarning(subject, 'fq_name', {
458:             ...byFq,
459:             candidates: selection.candidates.slice(0, AMBIGUOUS_SUBJECT_WARNING_CANDIDATE_LIMIT),
460:           }, selection.selectedCandidate, selection.selectionReason)]
461:         }
462:         : {}),
463:     };
464:   }
465: 
466:   // Try as name
467:   const byName = querySubjectMatches('name', subject);
468:   if (byName.count > 0) {
469:     const selection = pickOperationAwareCandidate(byName.candidates, operation, edgeType);
470:     return {
471:       symbolId: selection.selectedCandidate.symbolId,
472:       selectedCandidate: selection.selectedCandidate,
473:       ...(byName.count > 1
474:         ? {
475:           warnings: [buildAmbiguousSubjectWarning(subject, 'name', {
476:             ...byName,
477:             candidates: selection.candidates.slice(0, AMBIGUOUS_SUBJECT_WARNING_CANDIDATE_LIMIT),
478:           }, selection.selectedCandidate, selection.selectionReason)]
479:         }
480:         : {}),
481:     };
482:   }
483: 
484:   return { symbolId: null, suggestions: querySymbolBm25Suggestions(subject) };
485: }

487: // Readiness helpers extracted to lib/code-graph/readiness-contract.ts so the
488: // sibling code-graph handlers (scan, status, context) can share one
489: // vocabulary. See that module for durable mapping notes.
490: 
491: function buildGraphQueryPayload<T extends Record<string, unknown>>(
492:   payload: T,
493:   readiness: ReadyResult,
494:   label: string,
495:   graphEdgeEnrichment?: {
496:     edgeEvidenceClass: EdgeEvidenceClass;
497:     numericConfidence: number;
498:   } | null,
499: ) {
500:   const { readiness: _incomingReadiness, ...rest } = payload as Record<string, unknown>;
501:   const { graphMetadata, structuralTrust } = buildQueryTrustMetadata(readiness);
502:   const fallbackDecision = Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(rest, 'fallbackDecision')
503:     ? null
504:     : buildFallbackDecision(readiness);
505:   const payloadWithFallback = fallbackDecision
506:     ? { ...rest, fallbackDecision }
507:     : rest;
508:   const base = graphMetadata
509:     ? { ...payloadWithFallback, graphMetadata, readiness: buildReadinessBlock(readiness) }
510:     : { ...payloadWithFallback, readiness: buildReadinessBlock(readiness) };
511:   const withTrust = attachStructuralTrustFields(
512:     base as T & { readiness: ReturnType<typeof buildReadinessBlock> },
513:     structuralTrust,
514:     { label },
515:   );
516: 
517:   return graphEdgeEnrichment
518:     ? attachGraphEdgeEnrichment(withTrust, graphEdgeEnrichment, {
519:       label: `${label} graph edge enrichment`,
520:     })
521:     : withTrust;
522: }
523: 
524: type OutboundEdgeEntry = ReturnType<typeof graphDb.queryEdgesFrom>[number];
525: type InboundEdgeEntry = ReturnType<typeof graphDb.queryEdgesTo>[number];
526: 
527: interface DanglingEdgeWarning {
528:   code: 'dangling_edge';
529:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'];
530:   missingEndpoint: 'source' | 'target';
531:   count: number;
532:   danglingSymbolIds: string[];
533:   message: string;
534: }
535: 
536: type QueryWarning = AmbiguousSubjectWarning | DanglingEdgeWarning;
537: 
538: function mergeWarnings(...warningSets: Array<QueryWarning[] | undefined>): QueryWarning[] | undefined {
539:   const warnings = warningSets.flatMap((warningSet) => warningSet ?? []);
540:   return warnings.length > 0 ? warnings : undefined;
541: }
542: 
543: function excludeDanglingEdges<TEntry>(
544:   entries: TEntry[],
545:   limit: number,
546:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'],
547:   missingEndpoint: DanglingEdgeWarning['missingEndpoint'],
548:   isResolved: (entry: TEntry) => boolean,
549:   getDanglingId: (entry: TEntry) => string,
550: ): { resolvedEntries: TEntry[]; warnings?: DanglingEdgeWarning[] } {
551:   const resolvedEntries: TEntry[] = [];
552:   const danglingSymbolIds: string[] = [];
553: 
554:   for (const entry of entries) {
555:     if (!isResolved(entry)) {
556:       danglingSymbolIds.push(getDanglingId(entry));
557:       continue;
558:     }
559:     if (resolvedEntries.length < limit) {
560:       resolvedEntries.push(entry);
561:     }
562:   }
563: 
564:   if (danglingSymbolIds.length === 0) {
565:     return { resolvedEntries };
566:   }
567: 
568:   return {
569:     resolvedEntries,
570:     warnings: [{
571:       code: 'dangling_edge',
572:       operation,
573:       missingEndpoint,
574:       count: danglingSymbolIds.length,
575:       danglingSymbolIds: [...new Set(danglingSymbolIds)],
576:       message: `Excluded ${danglingSymbolIds.length} dangling ${operation} edge(s) because the ${missingEndpoint} node could not be resolved. Reindex the code graph to repair the missing node references.`,
577:     }],
578:   };
579: }
580: 
581: /**
582:  * BFS transitive traversal from a symbolId via the given edge type.
583:  *
584:  * Dangling nodes (where queryEdgesFrom/To returns an edge whose endpoint has
585:  * no node row) were previously surfaced as
586:  * successful traversal results with null fqName/filePath. They are now
587:  * flagged as corruption alongside the resolved results so callers can
588:  * trust the absence of `warnings` as "no dangling references".
589:  */
590: interface TransitiveTraversalResult {
591:   nodes: Array<{
592:     symbolId: string;
593:     fqName: string | null;
594:     filePath: string | null;
595:     line: number | null;
596:     depth: number;
597:   }>;
598:   warnings?: DanglingEdgeWarning[];
599: }
600: 
601: interface TransitiveTraversalNeighbor {
602:   readonly symbolId: string;
603:   readonly node: CodeNode | null;
604: }
605: 
606: function transitiveTraversal(
607:   startId: string,
608:   edgeType: string,
609:   direction: 'from' | 'to',
610:   maxDepth: number,
611:   limit: number,
612:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'],
613: ): TransitiveTraversalResult {
614:   const resultSymbolIds = new Set<string>();
615:   const missingEndpoint: DanglingEdgeWarning['missingEndpoint'] = direction === 'from' ? 'target' : 'source';
616:   const traversal = traverseGraphBfs<string, TransitiveTraversalNeighbor, TransitiveTraversalResult['nodes'][number]>({
617:     startIds: [startId],
618:     maxDepth,
619:     resultLimit: limit,
620:     visitTiming: 'dequeue',
621:     stopEnqueueWhenResultLimitReached: true,
622:     getNeighbors: (item) => {
623:       if (direction === 'from') {
624:         return graphDb.queryEdgesFrom(item.id, edgeType).map<GraphBfsNeighbor<string, TransitiveTraversalNeighbor>>(({ edge, targetNode }) => ({
625:           id: edge.targetId,
626:           payload: { symbolId: edge.targetId, node: targetNode },
627:           ...(targetNode == null ? { danglingId: edge.targetId } : {}),
628:         }));
629:       }
630: 
631:       return graphDb.queryEdgesTo(item.id, edgeType).map<GraphBfsNeighbor<string, TransitiveTraversalNeighbor>>(({ edge, sourceNode }) => ({
632:         id: edge.sourceId,
633:         payload: { symbolId: edge.sourceId, node: sourceNode },
634:         ...(sourceNode == null ? { danglingId: edge.sourceId } : {}),
635:       }));
636:     },
637:     mapResult: (visit) => {
638:       if (resultSymbolIds.has(visit.payload.symbolId) || visit.payload.node == null) {
639:         return null;
640:       }
641:       resultSymbolIds.add(visit.payload.symbolId);
642:       return {
643:         symbolId: visit.payload.symbolId,
644:         fqName: visit.payload.node.fqName ?? null,
645:         filePath: visit.payload.node.filePath ?? null,
646:         line: visit.payload.node.startLine ?? null,
647:         depth: visit.depth,
648:       };
649:     },
650:   });
651: 
652:   const nodes = traversal.results.map((entry) => entry.value).slice(0, limit);
653:   if (traversal.dangling.length === 0) {
654:     return { nodes };
655:   }
656: 
657:   const uniqueDangling = [...new Set(traversal.dangling.map((entry) => entry.id))];
658:   return {
659:     nodes,
660:     warnings: [{
661:       code: 'dangling_edge',
662:       operation,
663:       missingEndpoint,
664:       count: uniqueDangling.length,
665:       danglingSymbolIds: uniqueDangling,
666:       message: `Transitive ${operation} traversal encountered ${uniqueDangling.length} dangling ${missingEndpoint} node reference(s). Reindex the code graph to repair the missing node references.`,
667:     }],
668:   };
669: }
670: 
671: function classifyEdgeEvidenceClass(
672:   edgeType: string,
673:   metadata: Record<string, unknown> | undefined,
674: ): EdgeEvidenceClass {
675:   switch (edgeType) {
676:     case 'IMPORTS':
677:     case 'EXPORTS':
678:       return 'import';
679:     case 'EXTENDS':
680:     case 'IMPLEMENTS':
681:     case 'TYPE_OF':
682:       return 'type_reference';
683:     case 'TESTED_BY':
684:       return 'test_coverage';
685:     default:
686:       return metadata?.detectorProvenance === 'heuristic' || metadata?.evidenceClass === 'INFERRED'
687:         ? 'inferred_heuristic'
688:         : 'direct_call';
689:   }
690: }
691: 
692: function clampNumericConfidence(value: unknown): number {
693:   if (typeof value !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(value)) {
694:     return 0;
695:   }
696:   return Math.max(0, Math.min(1, value));
697: }
698: 
699: function edgeMetadataOutput(edge: OutboundEdgeEntry['edge'] | InboundEdgeEntry['edge']) {
700:   return {
701:     confidence: clampNumericConfidence(edge.metadata?.confidence ?? edge.weight),
702:     numericConfidence: clampNumericConfidence(edge.metadata?.confidence ?? edge.weight),
703:     detectorProvenance: edge.metadata?.detectorProvenance ?? null,
704:     evidenceClass: edge.metadata?.evidenceClass ?? null,
705:     reason: graphDb.sanitizeEdgeMetadataString(edge.metadata?.reason),
706:     step: graphDb.sanitizeEdgeMetadataString(edge.metadata?.step),
707:     edgeEvidenceClass: classifyEdgeEvidenceClass(
708:       edge.edgeType,
709:       edge.metadata as Record<string, unknown> | undefined,
710:     ),
711:   };
712: }

714: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
715:    Shared relationship-edge mapper
716:    --------------------------------------------------------------
717:    Replaces near-duplicate switch branches for `calls_from`,
718:    `calls_to`, `imports_from`, and `imports_to`. Two inputs control
719:    the variant: (1) direction = outbound (target side) vs inbound
720:    (source side); (2) whether to surface the start line.
721: ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── */
722: 
723: type RelationshipMappedEdge = RelationshipQueryEdge & {
724:   confidence: number;
725:   numericConfidence: number;
726:   detectorProvenance: string | null;
727:   evidenceClass: string | null;
728:   reason: string | null;
729:   step: string | null;
730:   edgeEvidenceClass: EdgeEvidenceClass;
731: };
732: 
733: function mapOutboundRelationshipEdge(
734:   entry: OutboundEdgeEntry,
735:   options: { includeLine: boolean },
736: ): RelationshipMappedEdge {
737:   return {
738:     target: entry.targetNode?.fqName ?? entry.edge.targetId,
739:     file: entry.targetNode?.filePath,
740:     ...(options.includeLine ? { line: entry.targetNode?.startLine } : {}),
741:     ...edgeMetadataOutput(entry.edge),
742:   };
743: }
744: 
745: function mapInboundRelationshipEdge(
746:   entry: InboundEdgeEntry,
747:   options: { includeLine: boolean },
748: ): RelationshipMappedEdge {
749:   return {
750:     source: entry.sourceNode?.fqName ?? entry.edge.sourceId,
751:     file: entry.sourceNode?.filePath,
752:     ...(options.includeLine ? { line: entry.sourceNode?.startLine } : {}),
753:     ...edgeMetadataOutput(entry.edge),
754:   };
755: }
756: 
757: function extractOutboundFilePaths(entries: OutboundEdgeEntry[]): string[] {
758:   return entries
759:     .map((entry) => entry.targetNode?.filePath)
760:     .filter((value): value is string => typeof value === 'string');
761: }
762: 
763: function extractInboundFilePaths(entries: InboundEdgeEntry[]): string[] {
764:   return entries
765:     .map((entry) => entry.sourceNode?.filePath)
766:     .filter((value): value is string => typeof value === 'string');
767: }
768: 
769: function subjectLooksLikeFilePath(subject: string): boolean {
770:   return FILE_PATH_SEPARATOR_PATTERN.test(subject) || FILE_PATH_EXTENSION_PATTERN.test(subject);
771: }

773: function resolveRelationshipFilePath(subject: string): string | null {
774:   if (!subjectLooksLikeFilePath(subject)) {
775:     return null;
776:   }
777: 
778:   const resolvedFilePath = graphDb.resolveSubjectFilePath(subject);
779:   return typeof resolvedFilePath === 'string' && resolvedFilePath.length > 0
780:     ? resolvedFilePath
781:     : null;

784: function queryRelationshipFileSymbols(filePath: string): CodeNode[] {
785:   return graphDb
786:     .queryOutline(filePath)
787:     .filter((node) => typeof node.symbolId === 'string' && node.symbolId.length > 0);

790: function relationshipFilePayload(input: {
791:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'];
792:   filePath: string;
793:   symbols: CodeNode[];
794:   edgeType: EdgeType | undefined;
795:   limit: number;
796: }): {
797:   result: {
798:     operation: QueryArgs['operation'];
799:     filePath: string;
800:     subjectKind: 'file_path';
801:     symbolCount: number;
802:     edgeType: EdgeType | undefined;
803:     edges: RelationshipMappedEdge[];
804:     warnings?: DanglingEdgeWarning[];
805:     hotFileBreadcrumbs: ReturnType<typeof buildHotFileBreadcrumbs>;
806:   };
807:   edgeEnrichment: ReturnType<typeof summarizeWeakestGraphEdgeEnrichment>;
808: } {
809:   const sourceIds = input.symbols.map((node) => node.symbolId);
810:   if (input.operation === 'calls_from' || input.operation === 'imports_from') {
811:     const entries = sourceIds.flatMap((symbolId) => graphDb.queryEdgesFrom(symbolId, input.edgeType));
812:     const { resolvedEntries, warnings } = excludeDanglingEdges(
813:       entries,
814:       input.limit,
815:       input.operation,
816:       'target',
817:       (entry: OutboundEdgeEntry) => entry.targetNode != null,
818:       (entry: OutboundEdgeEntry) => entry.edge.targetId,
819:     );
820:     const includeLine = input.operation === 'calls_from';
821:     const edges = resolvedEntries.map((entry) => mapOutboundRelationshipEdge(entry, { includeLine }));
822:     return {
823:       result: {
824:         operation: input.operation,
825:         filePath: input.filePath,
826:         subjectKind: 'file_path',
827:         symbolCount: input.symbols.length,
828:         edgeType: input.edgeType,
829:         edges,
830:         ...(warnings ? { warnings } : {}),
831:         hotFileBreadcrumbs: buildHotFileBreadcrumbs(extractOutboundFilePaths(resolvedEntries)),
832:       },
833:       edgeEnrichment: summarizeWeakestGraphEdgeEnrichment(edges),
834:     };
835:   }
836: 
837:   const entries = sourceIds.flatMap((symbolId) => graphDb.queryEdgesTo(symbolId, input.edgeType));
838:   const { resolvedEntries, warnings } = excludeDanglingEdges(
839:     entries,
840:     input.limit,
841:     input.operation,
842:     'source',
843:     (entry: InboundEdgeEntry) => entry.sourceNode != null,
844:     (entry: InboundEdgeEntry) => entry.edge.sourceId,
845:   );
846:   const includeLine = input.operation === 'calls_to';
847:   const edges = resolvedEntries.map((entry) => mapInboundRelationshipEdge(entry, { includeLine }));
848:   return {
849:     result: {
850:       operation: input.operation,
851:       filePath: input.filePath,
852:       subjectKind: 'file_path',
853:       symbolCount: input.symbols.length,
854:       edgeType: input.edgeType,
855:       edges,
856:       ...(warnings ? { warnings } : {}),
857:       hotFileBreadcrumbs: buildHotFileBreadcrumbs(extractInboundFilePaths(resolvedEntries)),
858:     },
859:     edgeEnrichment: summarizeWeakestGraphEdgeEnrichment(edges),
860:   };
861: }
862: 
863: const EDGE_EVIDENCE_CLASS_WEAKNESS: Record<EdgeEvidenceClass, number> = {
864:   inferred_heuristic: 0,
865:   test_coverage: 1,
866:   type_reference: 2,
867:   import: 3,
868:   direct_call: 4,
869: };
870: 
871: function summarizeWeakestGraphEdgeEnrichment(
872:   edges: Array<{
873:     edgeEvidenceClass: EdgeEvidenceClass;
874:     numericConfidence: number;
875:   }>,
876: ): {
877:   edgeEvidenceClass: EdgeEvidenceClass;
878:   numericConfidence: number;
879: } | null {
880:   let weakest: {
881:     edgeEvidenceClass: EdgeEvidenceClass;
882:     numericConfidence: number;
883:   } | null = null;
884: 
885:   for (const edge of edges) {
886:     if (weakest == null || edge.numericConfidence < weakest.numericConfidence) {
887:       weakest = edge;
888:       continue;
889:     }
890: 
891:     if (
892:       edge.numericConfidence === weakest.numericConfidence
893:       && EDGE_EVIDENCE_CLASS_WEAKNESS[edge.edgeEvidenceClass]
894:       < EDGE_EVIDENCE_CLASS_WEAKNESS[weakest.edgeEvidenceClass]
895:     ) {
896:       weakest = edge;
897:     }
898:   }
899: 
900:   return weakest;
901: }

903: function shouldBlockReadPath(readiness: ReadyResult, verificationGateBypassed = false): boolean {
904:   // False-safe contract: answer ONLY on a fresh graph. Any
905:   // non-fresh state must block rather than return a possibly-wrong structural
906:   // answer with status:'ok'. The previous predicate keyed only on
907:   // `action === 'full_scan' && !inlineIndexPerformed`, which let two non-fresh
908:   // states slip through: (a) a deleted-files-only `freshness:'stale', action:'none'`
909:   // result, and (b) a FAILED inline selective_reindex (`freshness:'stale',
910:   // inlineIndexPerformed:false`). Both returned ok over a stale graph. We now
911:   // gate on freshness directly and also refuse a failed gold-verification gate,
912:   // matching `detect_changes` readinessRequiresBlock for a single contract.
913:   return readiness.freshness !== 'fresh'
914:     || (!verificationGateBypassed && readiness.verificationGate === 'fail');
915: }
916: 
917: function buildFallbackDecision(readiness: ReadyResult): FallbackDecision | null {
918:   if (readiness.freshness === 'error') {
919:     return {
920:       nextTool: 'rg',
921:       reason: 'scan_failed',
922:     };
923:   }
924: 
925:   // Any non-fresh graph that reached the block path needs a scan before reads
926:   // can be trusted (full_scan, empty, or a stale graph a selective reindex
927:   // could not heal).
928:   if (readiness.freshness !== 'fresh') {
929:     return {
930:       nextTool: 'code_graph_scan',
931:       reason: readiness.action === 'selective_reindex' ? 'selective_reindex' : 'full_scan_required',
932:       retryAfter: 'scan_complete',
933:     };
934:   }
935: 
936:   return null;
937: }
938: 
939: function buildBlockedReadPayload(
940:   readiness: ReadyResult,
941:   operation: QueryArgs['operation'],
942:   subject: string,
943: ) {
944:   const fallbackDecision = buildFallbackDecision(readiness);
945:   // Surface scope + manifest diagnostics directly on the
946:   // blocked payload's `data` object so operators can route on
947:   // them without parsing `data.readiness`. Backward compatible:
948:   // legacy callers reading `status` + `data.blockReason` continue
949:   // to work; nested `data.readiness` still carries the same fields.
950:   return {
951:     status: 'blocked',
952:     message: `code_graph_full_scan_required: ${readiness.reason}`,
953:     data: buildGraphQueryPayload({
954:       operation,
955:       subject,
956:       blocked: true,
957:       degraded: true,
958:       graphAnswersOmitted: true,
959:       requiredAction: 'code_graph_scan',
960:       blockReason: 'full_scan_required',
961:       reason: readiness.reason,
962:       activeScope: readiness.activeScope ?? null,
963:       storedScope: readiness.storedScope ?? null,
964:       manifestCount: readiness.manifestCount ?? null,
965:       manifestDigest: readiness.manifestDigest ?? null,
966:       ...(fallbackDecision ? { fallbackDecision } : {}),
967:     }, readiness, `code_graph_query ${operation} blocked payload`),
968:   };
969: }
970: 
971: function buildHotFileBreadcrumbs(filePaths: string[]): Array<{
972:   filePath: string;
973:   hotFileBreadcrumb: HotFileBreadcrumb;
974: }> {
975:   const uniqueFilePaths = [...new Set(filePaths)];
976:   if (uniqueFilePaths.length === 0) {
977:     return [];
978:   }
979: 
980:   const degreeRows = graphDb.queryFileDegrees(uniqueFilePaths);
981:   const positiveDegrees = degreeRows
982:     .map((row) => row.degree)
983:     .filter((degree) => degree > 0)
984:     .sort((left, right) => right - left);
985:   if (positiveDegrees.length === 0) {
986:     return [];
987:   }
988: 
989:   const topCount = Math.max(1, Math.ceil(positiveDegrees.length * 0.1));
990:   const threshold = Math.min(20, positiveDegrees[topCount - 1] ?? positiveDegrees[0]);
991: 
992:   return degreeRows
993:     .filter((row) => row.degree >= threshold)
994:     .map((row) => ({
995:       filePath: row.filePath,
996:       hotFileBreadcrumb: {
997:         degree: row.degree,
998:         changeCarefullyReason: `High-degree node; change carefully because changes here ripple to ${row.degree} dependents.`,
999:       },
1000:     }));
1001: }
1002: 
1003: function parseEdgeMetadataConfidence(metadataJson: unknown, weight: unknown): number {
1004:   if (typeof metadataJson === 'string' && metadataJson.length > 0) {
1005:     try {
1006:       const metadata = JSON.parse(metadataJson) as Record<string, unknown>;
1007:       return clampNumericConfidence(metadata.confidence ?? weight);
1008:     } catch {
1009:       return clampNumericConfidence(weight);
1010:     }
1011:   }
1012:   return clampNumericConfidence(weight);
1013: }
1014: 
1015: function queryImportDependentsForBlastRadius(minConfidence: number, includeTrace = false): BlastRadiusDependency[] {
1016:   if (minConfidence <= 0 && !includeTrace) {
1017:     return graphDb.queryFileImportDependents().map((edge) => ({
1018:       ...edge,
1019:       confidence: 1,
1020:     }));
1021:   }
1022: 
1023:   const d = graphDb.getDb();
1024:   const rows = d.prepare(`
1025:     SELECT
1026:       target.file_path AS imported_file_path,
1027:       source.file_path AS importer_file_path,
1028:       edge.weight AS weight,
1029:       edge.metadata AS metadata
1030:     FROM code_edges edge
1031:     INNER JOIN code_nodes source ON source.symbol_id = edge.source_id
1032:     INNER JOIN code_nodes target ON target.symbol_id = edge.target_id
1033:     WHERE UPPER(edge.edge_type) = 'IMPORTS'
1034:       AND source.file_path != target.file_path
1035:   `).all() as Array<{
1036:     imported_file_path: string;
1037:     importer_file_path: string;
1038:     weight: number | null;
1039:     metadata: string | null;
1040:   }>;
1041: 
1042:   return rows
1043:     .map((row) => ({
1044:       importedFilePath: row.imported_file_path,
1045:       importerFilePath: row.importer_file_path,
1046:       confidence: parseEdgeMetadataConfidence(row.metadata, row.weight ?? 1),
1047:     }))
1048:     .filter((edge) => edge.confidence >= minConfidence);
1049: }
1050: 
1051: function buildDepthGroups(
1052:   affectedFiles: BlastRadiusAffectedFile[],
1053:   maxDepth: number,
1054: ): Record<number, BlastRadiusAffectedFile[]> {
1055:   const depthGroups: Record<number, BlastRadiusAffectedFile[]> = {};
1056:   for (let depth = 1; depth <= Math.max(0, maxDepth); depth++) {
1057:     depthGroups[depth] = [];
1058:   }
1059:   for (const affectedFile of affectedFiles) {
1060:     depthGroups[affectedFile.depth] ??= [];
1061:     depthGroups[affectedFile.depth].push(affectedFile);
1062:   }
1063:   return depthGroups;
1064: }
1065: 
1066: /**
1067:  * Classify a blast-radius response by depth-one fanout.
1068:  *
1069:  * Thresholds (008/D9 — documented):
1070:  *   - **high**: any of (a) ambiguityCandidates non-empty, (b) depth-one fanout > 10
1071:  *   - **medium**: depth-one fanout in [4, 10]
1072:  *   - **low**: depth-one fanout in [0, 3]
1073:  *
1074:  * Depth-one is used (not max depth) because the immediate consumers of
1075:  * the seed are the strongest blast-radius signal in practice.
1076:  */
1077: function classifyBlastRadiusRisk(
1078:   depthGroups: Record<number, BlastRadiusAffectedFile[]>,
1079:   ambiguityCandidates: SubjectCandidateMetadata[],
1080: ): BlastRadiusRiskLevel {
1081:   const depthOneCount = depthGroups[1]?.length ?? 0;
1082:   if (ambiguityCandidates.length > 0 || depthOneCount > 10) {
1083:     return 'high';
1084:   }
1085:   if (depthOneCount >= 4) {
1086:     return 'medium';
1087:   }
1088:   return 'low';
1089: }
1090: 
1091: function buildPartialBlastRadiusResult(
1092:   sourceFiles: string[],
1093:   nodes: BlastRadiusAffectedFile[],
1094:   affectedFiles: BlastRadiusAffectedFile[],
1095:   depthGroups: Record<number, BlastRadiusAffectedFile[]>,
1096: ): NonNullable<BlastRadiusFailureFallback['partialResult']> {
1097:   return {
1098:     sourceFiles,
1099:     nodes,
1100:     affectedFiles,
1101:     depthGroups,
1102:   };
1103: }
1104: 
1105: function computeBlastRadius(
1106:   sourceFiles: string[],
1107:   maxDepth: number,
1108:   limit: number,
1109:   minConfidence = 0,
1110:   ambiguityCandidates: SubjectCandidateMetadata[] = [],
1111:   includeTrace = false,
1112: ) {
1113:   const importedBy = new Map<string, BlastRadiusDependency[]>();
1114:   const importEdges = queryImportDependentsForBlastRadius(minConfidence, includeTrace);
1115:   for (const edge of importEdges) {
1116:     const importers = importedBy.get(edge.importedFilePath) ?? [];
1117:     if (!importers.some((existing) => existing.importerFilePath === edge.importerFilePath)) {
1118:       importers.push(edge);
1119:     }
1120:     importedBy.set(edge.importedFilePath, importers);
1121:   }
1122: 
1123:   const affectedByFile = new Map<string, { depth: number; why_included?: BlastRadiusWhyIncluded }>();
1124:   const normalizedSources = [...new Set(sourceFiles)];
1125:   let depthTruncated = false;
1126:   let overflowed = false;
1127: 
1128:   const buildWhyIncluded = (
1129:     depth: number,
1130:     path: ReadonlyArray<GraphBfsPathStep<string, BlastRadiusDependency>>,
1131:     truncationReason: BlastRadiusWhyIncluded['truncationReason'],
1132:   ): BlastRadiusWhyIncluded => {
1133:     const edgeChain = path.map((step) => ({
1134:       fromFile: step.payload.importedFilePath,
1135:       toFile: step.payload.importerFilePath,
1136:       edgeType: 'IMPORTS' as const,
1137:       confidence: step.payload.confidence,
1138:     }));
1139:     // Neutral seed for a min-reduce over confidences clamped to [0,1]; an
1140:     // empty chain yields 1 (no edge constrains the confidence).
1141:     const confidence = edgeChain.reduce(
1142:       (minimum, step) => Math.min(minimum, step.confidence),
1143:       1,
1144:     );
1145:     return {
1146:       depth,
1147:       edgeChain,
1148:       confidence,
1149:       ambiguous: ambiguityCandidates.length > 0,
1150:       truncationReason,
1151:     };
1152:   };
1153: 
1154:   for (const sourceFile of normalizedSources) {
1155:     const traversal = traverseGraphBfs<string, BlastRadiusDependency, BlastRadiusAffectedFile>({
1156:       startIds: [sourceFile],
1157:       maxDepth,
1158:       preVisitedIds: [sourceFile],
1159:       visitTiming: 'enqueue',
1160:       inspectDepthBoundary: true,
1161:       getNeighbors: (item) => [...(importedBy.get(item.id) ?? [])]
1162:         .sort((left, right) => left.importerFilePath.localeCompare(right.importerFilePath))
1163:         .map((edge) => ({
1164:           id: edge.importerFilePath,
1165:           payload: edge,
1166:         })),
1167:       mapResult: (visit) => normalizedSources.includes(visit.id)
1168:         ? null
1169:         : {
1170:           filePath: visit.id,
1171:           depth: visit.depth,
1172:           ...(includeTrace
1173:             ? { why_included: buildWhyIncluded(visit.depth, visit.path, null) }
1174:             : {}),
1175:         },
1176:     });
1177: 
1178:     depthTruncated ||= traversal.depthTruncated;
1179:     const maxDepthTruncatedFrom = new Set(traversal.truncated.map((entry) => entry.fromId));
1180:     for (const affectedFile of traversal.results.map((entry) => entry.value)) {
1181:       if (includeTrace && affectedFile.why_included && maxDepthTruncatedFrom.has(affectedFile.filePath)) {
1182:         affectedFile.why_included = {
1183:           ...affectedFile.why_included,
1184:           truncationReason: 'max_depth',
1185:         };
1186:       }
1187:       const previous = affectedByFile.get(affectedFile.filePath);
1188:       if (previous === undefined || affectedFile.depth < previous.depth) {
1189:         affectedByFile.set(affectedFile.filePath, {
1190:           depth: affectedFile.depth,
1191:           ...(affectedFile.why_included ? { why_included: affectedFile.why_included } : {}),
1192:         });
1193:       }
1194:     }
1195:   }
1196: 
1197:   // Detect true overflow by comparing the full traversal size against `limit`
1198:   // BEFORE slicing. Previously `affectedFiles.length >= limit`
1199:   // false-positived whenever the reachable set happened to equal `limit`
1200:   // exactly. We over-collect (`limit + 1` semantically — here the whole
1201:   // BFS frontier is already in `affectedByFile`) and slice down only after
1202:   // recording overflow.
1203:   const totalAffectedBeforeSlice = affectedByFile.size;
1204:   let affectedFiles = [...affectedByFile.entries()]
1205:     .map(([filePath, entry]) => ({
1206:       filePath,
1207:       depth: entry.depth,
1208:       ...(entry.why_included ? { why_included: entry.why_included } : {}),
1209:     }))
1210:     .sort((left, right) => left.depth - right.depth || left.filePath.localeCompare(right.filePath))
1211:     .slice(0, limit);
1212:   overflowed = totalAffectedBeforeSlice > limit;
1213:   if (includeTrace && overflowed && affectedFiles.length > 0) {
1214:     const lastIncluded = affectedFiles[affectedFiles.length - 1];
1215:     if (lastIncluded.why_included && lastIncluded.why_included.truncationReason == null) {
1216:       lastIncluded.why_included = {
1217:         ...lastIncluded.why_included,
1218:         truncationReason: 'result_limit',
1219:       };
1220:     }
1221:   }
1222:   const breadcrumbByFile = new Map(
1223:     buildHotFileBreadcrumbs([
1224:       ...normalizedSources,
1225:       ...affectedFiles.map((entry) => entry.filePath),
1226:     ]).map((entry) => [entry.filePath, entry.hotFileBreadcrumb]),
1227:   );
1228:   affectedFiles = affectedFiles.map((entry) => ({
1229:     ...entry,
1230:     ...(breadcrumbByFile.get(entry.filePath) ? { hotFileBreadcrumb: breadcrumbByFile.get(entry.filePath) } : {}),
1231:   }));
1232: 
1233:   const seedNodes = normalizedSources.map((filePath) => ({
1234:     filePath,
1235:     depth: 0,
1236:     isSeed: true,
1237:     ...(includeTrace
1238:       ? {
1239:         why_included: buildWhyIncluded(0, [], null),
1240:       }
1241:       : {}),
1242:     ...(breadcrumbByFile.get(filePath) ? { hotFileBreadcrumb: breadcrumbByFile.get(filePath) } : {}),
1243:   }));
1244:   const depthGroups = buildDepthGroups(affectedFiles, maxDepth);
1245: 
1246:   return {
1247:     sourceFiles: normalizedSources,
1248:     nodes: [
1249:       ...seedNodes,
1250:       ...affectedFiles,
1251:     ],
1252:     affectedFiles,
1253:     depthGroups,
1254:     riskLevel: classifyBlastRadiusRisk(depthGroups, ambiguityCandidates),
1255:     minConfidence,
1256:     ambiguityCandidates,
1257:     hotFileBreadcrumbs: [...breadcrumbByFile.entries()].map(([filePath, hotFileBreadcrumb]) => ({
1258:       filePath,
1259:       hotFileBreadcrumb,
1260:     })),
1261:     // Omitted when the traversal reached the full depth horizon, mirroring the
1262:     // `overflowed`-driven conditional spread below so the field is absent
1263:     // (rather than `false`) in the common, complete case.
1264:     ...(depthTruncated ? { depthTruncated: true } : {}),
1265:     ...(overflowed
1266:       ? {
1267:         failureFallback: {
1268:           reason: 'limit_reached',
1269:           code: 'limit_reached',
1270:           partialResult: buildPartialBlastRadiusResult(
1271:             normalizedSources,
1272:             [...seedNodes, ...affectedFiles],
1273:             affectedFiles,
1274:             depthGroups,
1275:           ),
1276:         } satisfies BlastRadiusFailureFallback,
1277:       }
1278:       : {}),
1279:   };
1280: }
1281: 
1282: /** Handle code_graph_query tool call */
1283: export async function handleCodeGraphQuery(args: QueryArgs): Promise<{ content: Array<{ type: string; text: string }> }> {
1284:   let readiness: ReadyResult = {
1285:     freshness: 'empty' as const,
1286:     action: 'none' as const,
1287:     inlineIndexPerformed: false,
1288:     reason: 'readiness check not run',
1289:   };
1290: 
1291:   // Auto-trigger: ensure graph is fresh before querying
1292:   try {
1293:     readiness = await ensureCodeGraphReady(process.cwd(), {
1294:       allowInlineIndex: true,
1295:       allowInlineFullScan: false,
1296:       allowGuardedInlineFullScan: true,
1297:     });
1298:   } catch (error) {
1299:     // Emit 'unavailable' trust-state on readiness-crash so query consumers
1300:     // see the same canonical vocabulary
1301:     // as code_graph_context. Previously this path dropped the readiness
1302:     // block entirely and only returned a status:'error' string.
1303:     const reason = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
1304:     const crashReadiness: ReadyResult = {
1305:       freshness: 'error' as const,
1306:       action: 'none' as const,
1307:       inlineIndexPerformed: false,
1308:       reason: 'readiness_check_crashed',
1309:     };
1310:     const crashBlock = buildReadinessBlock(crashReadiness);
1311:     return {
1312:       content: [{
1313:         type: 'text',
1314:         text: JSON.stringify({
1315:           // Align with code_graph_context + detect_changes — a non-fresh
1316:           // / unavailable graph (here: readiness probe crashed) refuses with
1317:           // status:'blocked', not 'error', so all three read handlers share one
1318:           // refusal token. The structured readiness block + rg fallback are
1319:           // preserved so consumers still see the crash detail.
1320:           status: 'blocked',
1321:           message: `code_graph_not_ready: ${reason}`,
1322:           data: {
1323:             readiness: crashBlock,
1324:             canonicalReadiness: crashBlock.canonicalReadiness,
1325:             trustState: crashBlock.trustState,
1326:             fallbackDecision: buildFallbackDecision(crashReadiness),
1327:           },
1328:         }),
1329:       }],
1330:     };
1331:   }
1332: 
1333:   const { operation, subject } = args;
1334:   const limit = Math.max(1, Math.min(Number(args.limit) || 50, 1000));
1335:   const { edgeType: requestedEdgeType, error: edgeTypeError } = resolveRequestedEdgeType(args);
1336:   const rawMaxDepth = args.maxDepth === undefined ? 3 : Number(args.maxDepth);
1337:   const maxDepth = Math.max(0, Math.min(Number.isFinite(rawMaxDepth) ? rawMaxDepth : 3, 20));
1338: 
1339:   if (edgeTypeError) {
1340:     return {
1341:       content: [{
1342:         type: 'text',
1343:         text: JSON.stringify({ status: 'error', error: edgeTypeError }),
1344:       }],
1345:     };
1346:   }
1347: 
1348:   if (shouldBlockReadPath(readiness, args.verificationGateBypass === 'gold-query-verifier')) {
1349:     return {
1350:       content: [{
1351:         type: 'text',
1352:         text: JSON.stringify(buildBlockedReadPayload(readiness, operation, subject), null, 2),
1353:       }],
1354:     };
1355:   }
1356: 
1357:   if (operation === 'outline') {
1358:     const resolvedSubject = graphDb.resolveSubjectFilePath(subject);
1359:     if (typeof resolvedSubject !== 'string' || resolvedSubject.length === 0) {
1360:       return {

1363:           text: JSON.stringify({
1364:             status: 'error',
1365:             error: `Could not resolve outline subject: ${subject}`,
1366:           }),
1367:         }],
1368:       };
1369:     }
1370: 
1371:     const nodes = graphDb.queryOutline(resolvedSubject);
1372:     const limited = nodes.slice(0, limit);
1373:     const outlineNodes: OutlineQueryNode[] = limited.map(n => ({
1374:       name: n.name,
1375:       kind: n.kind,
1376:       fqName: n.fqName,
1377:       line: n.startLine,
1378:       signature: n.signature,
1379:       symbolId: n.symbolId,
1380:     }));
1381:     return {
1382:       content: [{
1383:         type: 'text',
1384:         text: JSON.stringify({
1385:           status: 'ok',
1386:           data: buildGraphQueryPayload({
1387:             operation: 'outline',
1388:             filePath: resolvedSubject,
1389:             nodeCount: outlineNodes.length,
1390:             nodes: outlineNodes,
1391:           }, readiness, 'code_graph_query outline payload'),
1392:         }, null, 2),
1393:       }],
1394:     };
1395:   }
1396: 
1397:   if (operation === 'blast_radius') {
1398:     // Honor the documented `includeTransitive` flag for blast_radius: the schema
1399:     // describes it as "Enable multi-hop BFS traversal" (default false) with
1400:     // `maxDepth` applying only when it is true. Default returns direct importers
1401:     // only (depth 1); the flag opts into multi-hop closure up to maxDepth. (The
1402:     // call-graph operations gate on the same flag in their own branch.)
1403:     const effectiveDepth = args.includeTransitive ? maxDepth : 1;
1404:     const rawSubjects = args.unionMode === 'multi'
1405:       ? [subject, ...(args.subjects ?? [])]

1407:     const sourceFiles: string[] = [];
1408:     const ambiguityCandidates: SubjectCandidateMetadata[] = [];
1409:     const minConfidence = clampNumericConfidence(args.minConfidence ?? 0);
1410: 
1411:     for (const candidate of rawSubjects) {
1412:       const candidateLooksLikeFilePath = subjectLooksLikeFilePath(candidate);
1413:       let resolvedPathCandidate: string | null = null;
1414:       if (candidateLooksLikeFilePath) {
1415:         const resolvedFileCandidate = graphDb.resolveSubjectFilePath(candidate);
1416:         if (typeof resolvedFileCandidate === 'string' && resolvedFileCandidate.length > 0) {
1417:           sourceFiles.push(resolvedFileCandidate);
1418:           continue;
1419:         }
1420:         resolvedPathCandidate = resolvedFileCandidate;
1421:       }
1422: 
1423:       const byFqName = querySubjectMatches('fq_name', candidate);
1424:       if (byFqName.count > 1) {
1425:         ambiguityCandidates.push(...byFqName.candidates.slice(0, AMBIGUOUS_SUBJECT_WARNING_CANDIDATE_LIMIT));
1426:         continue;
1427:       }
1428:       if (byFqName.count === 0) {
1429:         const byName = querySubjectMatches('name', candidate);
1430:         if (byName.count > 1) {
1431:           ambiguityCandidates.push(...byName.candidates.slice(0, AMBIGUOUS_SUBJECT_WARNING_CANDIDATE_LIMIT));
1432:           continue;
1433:         }
1434:       }
1435: 
1436:       const resolvedSubject = resolvedPathCandidate ?? graphDb.resolveSubjectFilePath(candidate);
1437:       if (typeof resolvedSubject !== 'string' || resolvedSubject.length === 0) {
1438:         // Preserve any sibling seeds we have already resolved ahead of this

1440:         // discard work the caller can still use; instead surface the
1441:         // already-resolved seeds so multi-subject blast-radius queries
1442:         // degrade gracefully when one subject fails to resolve.
1443:         const preservedDepthGroups = buildDepthGroups([], effectiveDepth);
1444:         const preservedSeedNodes = sourceFiles.map((filePath) => ({
1445:           filePath,
1446:           depth: 0,
1447:           isSeed: true,
1448:         }));
1449:         return {
1450:           content: [{
1451:             type: 'text',
1452:             text: JSON.stringify({
1453:               status: 'ok',
1454:               data: buildGraphQueryPayload({
1455:                 operation,
1456:                 sourceFiles,
1457:                 nodes: preservedSeedNodes,
1458:                 affectedFiles: [],
1459:                 depthGroups: preservedDepthGroups,
1460:                 riskLevel: classifyBlastRadiusRisk(preservedDepthGroups, []),
1461:                 minConfidence,
1462:                 ambiguityCandidates: [],
1463:                 failureFallback: {
1464:                   reason: `unresolved_subject: ${candidate}`,
1465:                   code: 'unresolved_subject',
1466:                   partialResult: buildPartialBlastRadiusResult(
1467:                     sourceFiles,
1468:                     preservedSeedNodes,

1471:                   ),

1473:               }, readiness, 'code_graph_query blast_radius fallback payload'),
1474:             }, null, 2),
1475:           }],
1476:         };
1477:       }
1478:       sourceFiles.push(resolvedSubject);
1479:     }
1480: 
1481:     if (ambiguityCandidates.length > 0) {
1482:       const depthGroups = buildDepthGroups([], effectiveDepth);
1483:       return {
1484:         content: [{
1485:           type: 'text',
1486:           text: JSON.stringify({
1487:             status: 'ok',
1488:             data: buildGraphQueryPayload({
1489:               operation,
1490:               sourceFiles,
1491:               nodes: [],
1492:               affectedFiles: [],
1493:               depthGroups,
1494:               riskLevel: classifyBlastRadiusRisk(depthGroups, ambiguityCandidates),
1495:               minConfidence,

1501:               } satisfies BlastRadiusFailureFallback,
1502:             }, readiness, 'code_graph_query blast_radius ambiguity payload'),
1503:           }, null, 2),

1505:       };
1506:     }
1507: 
1508:     if (sourceFiles.length === 0) {
1509:       const depthGroups = buildDepthGroups([], effectiveDepth);
1510:       return {
1511:         content: [{
